name: Sherry Aaholm
title: Chief Digital Officer
bio: Sherry Aaholm is currently the Chief Digital Officer (CDO) of Cummins Inc. Ms. Aaholm joined Cummins in 2013 after more than 15 years at FedEx Services. She served as the Executive Vice President, Information Technology, at FedEx where she drove the IT transformation strategy for the global enterprise. At Cummins she is responsible for a similar IT transformation strategy involving daily operations of the global infrastructure footprint. Ms. Aaholm attended the University of Wisconsin and received her Bachelor’s in Business Administration with majors in Marketing and Economics. She recently was awarded a Master’s of Science in Sustainability from the University of Wisconsin, Green Bay. She has also attended the University Of Chicago Graduate School Of Business for Finance for Executives and Strategic Business Leadership. Ms. Aaholm is a strong advocate for helping to develop the next generation of talent, and for encouraging women to develop careers in IT. Her work in developing leadership programs has been recognized by Computerworld, and she has spoken at Gartner and Computerworld events on the subject. In addition, she believes strongly in helping women to achieve their goals, and was involved in the Women’s Foundation for a Greater Memphis, which focuses on helping single mothers get an education and support to succeed. She is currently championing iDEW (Informatics Diversity-Enhanced Workforce), a program that partners with the Indiana University School of Computing and Informatics at IUPUI, business partners and several Indianapolis area high schools to identify and build an IT talent pipeline through project-based learning while students are in high school in the Indianapolis metropolitan area.

name: Jane Aboyoun
title: Fractional / Interim CIO, CTO
bio: Jane Aboyoun is the Fractional/Interim Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Fortium Partners. Previously, she served as the CIO at SCO Family of Services. Ms. Aboyoun formerly held the position of Officer and Vice President, Technology, at the New York Public Library, the second-largest public library in the United States, behind the Library of Congress. In this role, she was responsible for developing the technology strategy and execution plan to propel the New York Public Library from a beloved city institution to a digital powerhouse in the 21st century. Ms. Aboyoun played a pivotal role as the architect of a complex, multi-year technology transformation that impacted 88 branch libraries and four scholarly research facilities. The New York Public Library serves over 17 million users in person on-site and millions more online. Before her tenure at the New York Public Library, Ms. Aboyoun held executive technology positions at various global organizations. She served as the CTO for Asset International Inc., a leading provider of critical data, business intelligence, and information services for the global investment management community. Additionally, she held the CIO position for Bertelsmann Media, a direct marketer of books, music, and DVDs to millions of members online. Ms. Aboyoun also served as the CIO for Reed Business Information, a division of Reed Elsevier, a B2B publishing organization with over 100+ publications, 75 web properties, and numerous online products. Ms. Aboyoun holds an MBA in International Business from The University of Connecticut and a BS in Industrial Engineering from Syracuse University. She has also completed a Strategic Leadership program at Harvard University. Currently, Ms. Aboyoun resides with her family in Brooklyn, NY.

name: Marene N. Allison
title: Board Advisor
bio: Marene N. Allison is currently serving as a Board Advisor at Lutron Electronics, a Business Advisor at IT Cadre, and a Strategic Advisor at Rubrik, Inc. She has held the position of Chief Information Security Officer for Johnson & Johnson, where she had responsibility for protecting the Information Technology (IT) systems and data worldwide through the elimination and mitigation of IT risk. She co-led the IT Risk Management Council and was a member of the Enterprise Compliance Council during her tenure at Johnson & Johnson. Ms. Allison joined Johnson & Johnson in September 2010. Prior to joining Johnson & Johnson, Ms. Allison was Chief Security Officer and Vice President for Medco, the largest pharmacy benefit manager in the United States. She was responsible for all aspects of the company's security, regulatory and compliance including, physical and logical security, executive protection as well as HIPPA, Payment Card Industry, Medicare and prescription fraud and IT controls. Prior to that, Ms. Allison was with Avaya as head of Global Security where she worked on securing the World Cup network in Korea and Japan in 2002. Before joining Avaya, she was Vice President of Loss Prevention and Safety for the Great Atlantic and Pacific Tea Company. Before joining the corporate world, she served as a Special Agent in the FBI working on undercover drug operations in Newark, NJ and also working on terrorist bombings in San Diego, CA. She developed and participated in the nuclear terrorism exercise, Compass Rose ’88, the largest mock terrorism incident exercise by the federal government. Ms. Allison holds a B.S. degree from The United States Military Academy at West Point, in the first class to include women. She has served in the US Army in the Military Police, at Ft Hood, TX, Ft Chaffee, AR and Ft McClellan, AL. She has served on the Defense Advisory Committee on Women in the Services appointed by the Secretary of Defense and the Overseas Security Advisory Committee appointed by the Secretary of State. She is also a founding member of West Point Women.

name: Tej Anand
title: Clinical Professor
company: University of Maryland - Robert H. Smith School of Business
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Tej Anand is an award-winning business-technology strategist, advisor, innovator, educator, and practitioner. As an electrical engineer and computer scientist with a specialization in artificial intelligence research in the late 1980s, he played a pioneering role in the development of commercial data mining, now recognized as data science. With over three decades of experience, Mr. Anand has dedicated his career to teaching computers how to learn and extract meaning from data for business professionals. Complementing his technical background, Tej Anand earned a doctorate in adult learning and organizational leadership from Teachers College, Columbia University, in 2014. His primary passion lies in helping businesses discover their competitive edge at the intersection of business, data, and technology, always keeping people at the forefront of his focus. Mr. Anand also serves as a Clinical Professor at the University of Maryland and is the Co-Founder of The Inceptary, a non-profit "thinking social network" advocating for disruption and transformation in the US healthcare sector. In the initial stage of his career, Tej Anand held positions at technology companies such as NCR/Teradata, A. C. Nielsen, and Philips Research Laboratories. During this time, he contributed to several patents and developed revenue-producing products. Subsequently, he spent five years refining his skills as a technology executive and management consultant before transitioning to the healthcare industry. Over 15 years, working as a senior business-technology executive at Medco and CareCentrix, he successfully implemented data-driven, technology-enabled business models and processes that reduced healthcare costs and improved clinical outcomes, resulting in several patents. In June 2018, Mr. Anand shifted his focus to education and consulting. Currently, he serves as an Assistant Clinical Professor at the McCombs School of Business, University of Texas in Austin, and as a Visiting Professor at the Columbia University Center for Technology Management. In addition, he is the President of Celeriac System, where he tackles complex societal challenges associated with the unintended consequences of technology. Furthermore, he holds the position of Clinical Professor at the University of Maryland - Robert H. Smith School of Business, where he continues to contribute his expertise in the fields of business and technology.

name: Julia Anderson
title: Chief Technology & Information Officer
bio: Julia Anderson is currently the Chief Technology & Information Officer at Campbell Soup Company. In her previous role, she served as the Global Chief Information Officer at Smithfield Foods, a $15 billion global food company. As a member of the executive leadership team at Smithfield Foods, she supported the company's strategic targets by executing enterprise-wide information technology strategies to allow the company to scale its operations and use analytics to drive decision making. Ms. Anderson is a business-focused IT executive with an extensive track record in Fortune 500 consumer branded companies. Prior to Smithfield, she was Vice President of Global Applications and CIO North America at HJ Heinz where she was instrumental in the successful privatization of the company. Prior to Heinz, she was an IT Leader at PepsiCo during the growth of Foodservice division and bottler acquisition years. Ms. Anderson held transformation and IT leadership roles at Kraft foods during the merger with General Foods and Oscar Mayer. She joined parent company Altria where she was responsible for corporate systems. She started her career at Baxter Healthcare. Ms. Anderson serves on the Board of Directors for Make-A-Wish Virginia and the CGT Consumer Goods Technology, Executive Advisory Council. She has been active in SIM (Society of Information Management) since 1999. Ms. Anderson served on the board of the Fairfield Westchester chapter for three years and is a member of the Advanced Practices Council since 2006.

name: Sara Andrews
title: Global Chief Information Security Officer
bio: Sara Andrews is the Global Chief Information Security Officer at Experian. She is also a Member of the Board of Directors at Marvell Technology and an active member of the Aspen Institute Cybersecurity Group. Additionally, She serves as a Corporate Board Member and Director at Mandiant. In her previous roles, Sara Andrews served as PepsiCo's Senior Vice President and Global Chief Information Security Officer. Sara Andrews is responsible for developing the risk-based information security strategy for PepsiCo and implementing this across the enterprise. She is the company’s top information security executive, responsible for safeguarding networks and data across the $66 billion food and beverage powerhouse, including all its business units and brands globally. Her accountabilities include developing a best-in-class security risk mitigation blueprint; creating a role-based user access management and identity management deployment; refreshing security policy for the enterprise globally; strengthening overall infrastructure security; and developing and implementing forward-looking security architecture. Prior to PepsiCo, Ms. Andrews was Verizon’s Chief Network Security Officer (CNSO). In this role, she led several organizations responsible for the security of all Verizon wireline networks serving Verizon's residential, small business and enterprise customers. She was responsible for defining Verizon Wireline Network's security strategy and risk tolerance for safeguarding company and customer data, intellectual and network assets, and providing functional direction and control of Verizon's Wireline Network Security Program. Before serving as the CNSO for Verizon, Ms. Andrews was the CISO for Verizon Business, a unit of Verizon focusing on enterprise customers. In this role, she was responsible for the security of all Verizon Business data and for ensuring the integrity of the Verizon Business network and information systems. Under her direction, Verizon received two National Information Security Leadership Awards, one for the company’s effective defense against distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks, and a second for the best achievement by an Internet service provider in fighting damage from network worm attacks. Ms. Andrews's professional achievements include: •Recipient of the Evanta 2016 Breakaway Leader Award honoring CISOs and senior security executives who demonstrate exceptional leadership •Being invited to testify at the “Protecting Cyberspace as a National Asset: Comprehensive Legislation for the 21st Century” hearing before the U.S. Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs •Selected as a finalist for the Executive Women’s Forum for Information Security, Risk Management and Privacy’s “Women of Influence” Awards •Selected as one of 20 “Security Superstars” by Information Security magazine and featured on the cover and in the lead story •Recognized by Verizon with the “Who Inspires You” award as one of 20 leaders who demonstrated the highest standards of personal integrity, character and professionalism •Member of the CISO Coalition National Leadership Board Ms. Andrews holds a B.S. in industrial engineering from Auburn University and an M.B.A. from Brenau University.

name: Tiago Azevedo
title: Chief Information Officer
bio: Tiago joined OutSystems as Chief Information Officer in September 2019 and is responsible for leading the Digital Team to build a world-class business architecture that secures a frictionless customer experience and enables the organization’s growth ambition. He merges a fundamental understanding of business, technology and new ways of working, establishing Digital as a central part of the company. He has 20+ years of experience, the first 10 in Management Consulting with Accenture and the latter as a CIO across bluechip companies in several countries, including online, publishing and broadcast media environments, telecommunications, high tech and energy, where he has led large-scale digital transformations. Tiago is a recognized thought leader in his field and has advised other CIOs on their Digital and Transformation strategies. He is a driven and inspiring leader passionate about building high-performing teams that exceed expectations.

name: Nidal Babar
title: Vice President & Global Infrastructure
bio: Nidal Babar currently serves as the Vice President of Global Infrastructure at American Express. Before his role at American Express, he was the Managing Director in the Consumer & Community Banking Technology division of JPMorgan Chase & Co. Mr. Babar has held several leadership positions throughout his career, including roles at Tradeweb, where he was Managing Director for Global Infrastructure & Enterprise Technology and Co-Head of Inter-Dealer Broking Technology. At Tradeweb, he played a key role in building the technology organization that supported Inter-Dealer Electronic and Hybrid trading businesses. Prior to his time at Tradeweb, he served as the Head of EMEA Technology Operations at ICAP Plc in London, overseeing system operations and delivery for trading, middle, and back office functions in the EMEA region. Nidal Babar has extensive experience in electronic trading technology and has held various leadership roles across the technology stack, starting at BrokerTec in 1999. Mr. Babar holds a B.S. in Computer Information Systems from Bellevue University and an M.S. in Technology Management from Columbia University. Additionally, he serves as an Adjunct Professor of Computer Science at Caldwell University.

name: Jan Babst
title: Group CIO and Global Business Services
bio: Jan Babst is the Group CIO and Global Business Services at OMV, one of the Austria’s largest listed industrial companies. He landed to this position after being the CIO at Logista S. A. for over a decade. Mr. Babst was formerly CEO/CIO at Holcim Services EMEA S. A. Prior to this role, he was Head of Applications at Holcim Ltd. Mr. Babst holds an M.B.A. from Dokky University, Japan, and has studied at Universitaet Duisburg, Germany, Universidad Alcala de Henares, Spain, Dublin University, Ireland and Universitaet Paderborn.

name: Sundi Balu
title: Co-Founder and CTO
bio: Sundi Balu is the Co-Founder and CTO of a XpertiseNow, and AI enabled start up focused on disrupting management consulting. Recognised by Hong Kong and Asian IT Telecoms and Media as a leading technology innovator, Mr. Balu is a dynamic leader, zealous problem solver and pragmatic decision maker. His career achievements are attributable to his dedication, long-term vision and passion for helping businesses grow. Mr. Balu has over 25 years of experience in several functions of IT and consulting and has held many senior level technology roles in international telecommunications and financial services companies. He is an inspirational technology innovator committed to maximising business value through the use of technology. In his previous role as CIO of Telstra Enterprise and International based in Hong Kong, Mr. Balu was responsible for defining business unit level IT strategy, managing a cumulative technology $750M budget, leading software engineering, leading global teams, digital transformation programs and technical thought leadership. He successfully co-led a $500M digital transformation for the B2B business in Australia and a $60M business transformation for Telstra Global across 15 countries, improved the way 1000 software engineers work across geographies to deliver a 30% reduction in time taken to launch new business capability. Mr. Balu has led technology based innovative productivity initiatives with cumulative multiyear benefits of $70M and has also spearheaded cultural transformation through the adoption of Agile, DevOps and Cloud technology, delivering a 40% improvement in time to market for product development. Mr. Balu holds an M.B.A. from the Melbourne Business School and an M.S. in Engineering from the Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology.

name: Steven Bandrowczak
title: Chief Executive Officer
bio: Steve Bandrowczak is the Chief Executive Officer at Xerox. Mr. Bandrowczak has more than 30 years of experience as an IT professional. He is an accomplished executive with a history of highly effective leadership within a diverse range of technologies. He has a proven track record of growing businesses consistently through innovation, and enhancing competitive positions while increasing profits. Mr. Bandrowczak is the former Chief Operating Officer and Chief Information Officer of Alight Solutions, where he oversaw the application portfolio and technical infrastructure of the organization, including digital and print operatons, operational performance, enterprise risk management, real estate, and supply chain. Previously, Mr. Bandrowczak was the President of Telecommunication Media and Technology at Sutherland Global Services. He also served as the Senior Vice President for Global Business Services at Hewlett Packard Enterprises, where he transformed its 16,000-employee shared service organization into a highly efficient operation with a focus on automation, business intelligence and labor optimization. In addition, he led the business process outsourcing (BPO) practice in the Enterprise Services Group. Prior to joining Hewlett Packard, he held senior leadership positions for various multi-billion-dollar global companies, including Avaya, Nortel, Lenovo, DHL, and a privately owned SAP consulting company. Mr. Bandrowczak serves as Chairman of the Board for The Samfund and was named one of the Top 100 CIOs by Computerworld. He frequently headlines conferences and teaches leadership at educational institutions.

name: Ash Banerjee
title: Global Chief Information Officer
bio: Ash Banerjee currently holds the position of Global Chief Information Officer at Dentons, a prominent law firm that assists clients in achieving their business outcomes and imperatives, whether related to growth, protection, operation, or financing. Ash Banerjee is recognized as a passionate international general manager, technology executive, management consultant, investor, and advisor, bringing over 20 years of expertise to the table. Ash has a proven track record of building high-performance global teams, driving results, and fostering innovations, particularly in industries such as Professional Service, Media, Telecom, and Technology. Throughout Ash’s dynamic career, a consistent presence on the frontlines of change and digital transformation has been evident. As a metrics-driven leader, Ash maximizes financial impact by carefully leveraging the levers of profitability. Known for being a savvy, collaborative, and inclusive leader, Ash excels in driving consensus and change across diverse organizations, making strategic and challenging business decisions to deliver results aligned with Dentons’ firm strategy. Ash’s expertise extends to successful post-merger integrations. Educationally, Ash holds an MA and MEng from Cambridge University, complemented by an AMP from Harvard Business School. In the most recent 5 years, Ash served as Chief Operating Officer – Americas and Global Chief Information Officer for Hogan Lovells, based in Washington DC. Prior to this role, Ash spent nearly 8 years as Chief Information Officer for WilmerHale in Washington DC, accumulating over 12 years of experience in the legal sector. Before entering the legal sector, Ash served for 3 years at EMI Music as EVP, Global Chief Information Officer, based in New York, NY. In a prior role, Ash served as VP at Equant (now Orange Business Services) Global Application Systems, based in Reston, Virginia, reporting to the CIO. Ash was responsible for all Global application systems, leading a team across the US, Europe, and Asia. Ash played a pivotal role in the merger integration plan with Global One, overseeing the conversion to common Global systems worldwide. The journey with Global One began in 1996 as Director, Carrier Systems, based in Brussels, and included a relocation to the US in 1999 as VP Billing Systems, concurrently holding the position of Global CIO for Global One. Earlier in the career journey, Ash briefly worked for Heidrick & Struggles International Inc as Director of Information, based in Paris. Prior to this, Ash spent over 10 years with Andersen Consulting (Accenture) in London, UK, leading several multimillion-dollar projects, including spearheading a Pan European Systems Integration and Change project for a food manufacturer. Ash’s specialties encompass executive management, international operational management, financial management, change agency, consulting management, practice operations, information technology strategy, technology adoption and innovation, digital transformation and change implementation, employee engagement, strategic planning, and merger planning and integration.

name: Shawn Banerji
title: Managing Partner, Technology, Digital & Data Leaders
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Shawn Banerji is the managing partner for the Technology, Digital and Data Leaders Practice at Caldwell Partners. As a trusted advisor to organizations seeking cultural and commercial transformation by unlocking the latent value of legacy assets and product portfolios, he advises clients on the convergence of talent and innovation across all facets of the evolving digital enterprise.Mr. Banerji is an expert on change management and specializes in the recruitment and assessment of Catalyst Leaders who are resetting value propositions through the power of technology, data & analytics, accelerated software engineering, IoT and cloud. Industry coverage includes retail & consumer, insurance, investments & banking, professional services, healthcare, process & industrial, technology & media as well as financial sponsors and their portfolio companies. Roles include CIO, CTO, chief digital officer, chief data officer, CISO, and the related leadership suites.Mr. Banerji joined Caldwell Partners from Russell Reynolds Associates, where he was a leader in the Technology Officers Practice, and a founding member of both the FinTech and Digital Health practices. He is an ex-officio board member of SIM New York and serves on the board of advisors for The Center for Technology Management at Columbia University. He is a regular contributor at MIT’s annual CIO Symposium and an advocate for increasing technology representation and diversity on corporate boards.Mr. Banerji holds a B.A. in speech communications from the University of Richmond, VA and an M.A. in Communications from the University of Westminster, London. He is certified in a variety of assessment tools and methodologies. He is co-located in Caldwell Partners’ New York and Stamford offices.

name: Mark Barnard
title: SVP Service
company: Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey
bio: With a long career at Blue Cross Blue Shield, Mark Barnard currently is the Senior Vice President of Service for Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey. Prior to this roll, Mr. Barnard was the President and Chief Executive Officer of National Account Service Company, an Atlanta-based IT infrastructure provider dedicated to claims processing for Blue Cross and Blue Shield plans nationwide. Mr. Barnard also spent 16 years with Electronic Data Systems, where he was ultimately responsible for overseeing all electronic data systems operations for various Blue Cross and Blue Shield plans across the nation. In addition, as Vice President at The MEDSTAT Group, he had responsibility for client services and product development. Mr. Barnard holds a B.S. in Mathematics Education from Michigan State University. Prior to his career in health care, he taught mathematics at the high school level and coached wrestling. Active in community affairs, Mr. Barnard serves on the boards of directors of several nonprofit groups, and is an active volunteer for Habitat for Humanity, nationally and internationally, and for the Boy Scouts of America.

name: Christopher Barron
title: Chief Digital and Technology Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Christopher Barron currently serves as the Chief Digital and Technology Officer at Our Daily Bread Ministries, where he leads the organization's digital and technology initiatives. He previously served as Executive Vice President and Chief Information Officer at EMF Broadcasting’s K-LOVE and Air1 Radio Networks. Barron holds a B.S. in Engineering from the University of Idaho and holds two post-graduate degrees: an M.B.A. from Phoenix University and an M.Sc. in Management and Systems from New York University. Mr. Barron started his career as an engineer, which was a great foundation for his migration into technology. He has enjoyed leadership roles and pushing himself to the limits of what can be accomplished, and says: “One of the greatest joys that I’ve found is in taking paths that other people may not tread, knowing that I would have to stretch and reach further. The best fruit is always on the top of the tree.” Mr. Barron brings more than twenty-two years of experience to K-LOVE and Air1. Recently, he started praying for the Lord to place him in a job where he could use his talents to further God’s work. “I’ve never been more thankful to be at a place ... I feel the call to pour myself out for this ministry; because I believe it has an incredible impact.” He leads the following team(s) of ministry professionals: Enterprise Applications, Cyber Security, Infrastructure, Service Excellence, Studio Technology, Media Delivery, Technology Innovation Mr. Barron and his wife, Rosa, live in Northern California with their three children Alexander, Isabella and Orion. As a family, they enjoy traveling and look forward to visiting Mr. Barron's ancestral homelands in Switzerland, someday.

name: Jack Barsky
title: Former Intelligence Officer
bio: Jack Barsky is a retired Information Technology executive with over thirty years of experience. During his career, he has held management positions, up to and including the role of CIO, at a number of Fortune 200 companies such as MetLife, United HealthCare, Prudential, ConEdison, and NRG Energy. Mr. Barsky is a long-standing member of the Society for Information Management – he founded the Albany chapter. He was one of the first recruits for the Columbia University Masters in Technology Metering Program, and successfully mentored four students.Mr. Barsky graduated as valedictorian with a Bachelor of Business Administration from Baruch College. He also holds an M.S. in Chemistry from the Friedrich Schiller University, Jena Germany. In those days, he was known as Albrecht Dittrich. Mr. Barsky cut short his university career to join the KGB, and in 1978 he entered the U.S. as an undercover agent to spy for the Soviet Union. His life story was featured in May of 2015 on CBS 60 Minutes. His memoir “Deep Undercover” was published in March of 2017. Mr. Barsky lives in Austin TX where he keeps busy with public appearances, mentoring, and additional writing projects.

name: Richard Barton
title: Chief Information Officer
bio: Richard J. Barton is CIO and Senior VP of American Management Association, where he is responsible for all information technology services and product development worldwide. Mr. Barton has extensive experience in aligning technology with growing business requirements. His credentials span more than 20 years, primarily at McGraw Hill's Standard & Poor's division where he began in 1992 as a VP of technology for S&P's Comstock business. Then, as CTO, he was a key adviser for all technology initiatives and practices on an enterprise-wide basis between 1995 and 2000. As VP, technology, from 2000 to 2002 he was responsible for strategic planning, development, and global technology operations of editorial and analytical production and web services systems for one of S&P's two major units. As managing director and CTO of MMS International, Mr. Barton was a principal in the company's divestiture/buyout from McGraw-Hill-S&P's, identifying global technology planning and costs in support of the financial planning to secure funds for the transaction. He was also charged with establishing and executing the global vision and strategy for the creation of MMS' technology platform and infrastructure and online product development. Later, he supported the company's eventual sale and integration with Informa Financial, retaining the same title and reporting to the CIO. Barton began his career at Commodity Quotations Inc. as a customer service technician/manager, rising through the ranks to become the company's quality assurance and operations director.Mr. Barton holds a B.S. in Computer Engineering from Farleigh Dickinson University and he completed Standard and Poor's Executive Development program at the University of Virginia 's Darden School of Business.

name: Diana Beecher
title: Former CIO
bio: Diana Beecher is the retired Senior Director of the Business Services Center at the NY Metropolitan Transportation Authority. In this role, she implemented a unified back office to support the MTA’s legacy businesses. Prior to her public service, Ms. Beecher had a successful career in financial services. Her most recent private sector position was CIO at Marsh, Inc. Prior to joining Marsh, she was Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Travelers Insurance. In that role, she was directly responsible for the development and application of technology for all business lines and personal lines. Ms. Beecher holds a B.S. in Accounting from Shippensburg University in Shippensburg, Pennsylvania.

name: Carr Bettis
title: Executive Chairman
bio: Carr Bettis is a serially successful entrepreneur, as the founder and chief architect of numerous market-leading financial science and technology innovations and businesses that have been acquired by Merrill Lynch, Thomson Financial (now Thomson Reuters), Primark/Disclosure (now Thomson Reuters), Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS), Advanced Equities/GreenBook Financial (now First Allied) and Sabrient Systems. He manages his family's private equity portfolio via Fathom Lab. Most recently Mr. Bettis founded, was Chairman and helped to incubate Incentive Lab which was sold to ISS in October 2014. He jointly owns intellectual property with Thomson Reuters via his IP firm CB Partners, and is co-founder and Chairman of Verus Analytics, a 20-year quantitative financial technology company. Mr. Bettis serves on the Board of Directors of three technology companies: iMemories, First Contact Entertainment , and is Executive Chairman of AudioEye (OTCBB: AEYE). He is the co-founder and managing member of Reach4Partners and serves on the Advisory Board of Symbolic IO. Mr. Bettis is a former tenured professor and government appointee having served as Securities Administrator, Tax Commissioner and Chief Financial Officer. He maintains an affiliation with Arizona State University as Research Professor of Finance and is frequently cited in national and international financial media. His research has been published in the most prestigious academic journals such as the Journal of Financial Economics, Review of Financial Studies, and Journal of Financial and Quantitative Analysis. He has also published in practitioner journals such as the Financial Analyst Journal and the Journal of Investment Management. He received his Ph.D. from Indiana University in 1992. Mr. Bettis is father of 3 daughters.

name: Puneet Bhasin
title: EVP, Global Chief Information Officer (CIO) & Chief Digital Officer (CDO)
bio: Puneet Bhasin is Executive Vice President, Global Chief Information Officer and Chief Digital Officer at Unum. Formerly, he was Executive Vice President, Corporate Operations and President, Recycling for Waste Management, Inc., a $14B, Fortune 200 environmental services company. In this role, Mr. Bhasin was responsible for the company’s Fleet Operations, Maintenance Operations, Logistics, Shared Services, Supply Chain, Information Technology, and the Recycling business. Prior to joining Waste Management, Mr. Bhasin held roles as Senior Vice President, Global Product and Technology and Chief Information Officer, North America for Monster Worldwide. Prior to that, he was a senior vice president for Putnam Investments and Chief Information Officer for Ryder TRS. Mr. Bhasin holds a B.S. from the National Institute of Technology, India and an M.S. in Operations Research from The Ohio State University.

name: Rolf Bischofberger
title: Director IT- Services
bio: Rolf Bischofberger is a highly accomplished IT professional with an impressive career spanning over 30 years, during which he has excelled as an ITC Manager within multinational corporations. Rolf has consistently demonstrated outstanding leadership capabilities, specializing in the execution of intricate IT and Business Transformation projects, as well as managing IT operations within diverse sourcing arrangements. His extensive experience has solidified his reputation for building strong, trust-based networks with both internal stakeholders and major external service providers, resulting in successful project outcomes. A true bridge between business and IT, Rolf's expertise lies in guiding projects and applications throughout their entire life cycle. He is renowned for his customer-centric approach, employing pragmatic, goal-oriented solutions to achieve desired results. Rolf's unique blend of resilience, motivation, and assertiveness make him a versatile professional, equally adept as a leader, team player, coach, and facilitator, all while maintaining a keen focus on essential details. Rolf's career is marked by numerous achievements, including his leadership in the comprehensive implementation of a Microsoft platform encompassing Dynamics 365 CRM, FinOps, Power Platform, and Azure Cloud. He also led the successful implementation of multiple SAP modules, including FiCo on a global scale, and was instrumental in the establishment of an SAP Competence Center in Switzerland. Notably, Rolf managed a service division as a general manager, complete with profit and loss responsibility. Currently, Rolf serves as the Director of IT Services for Switzerland Global Enterprise, showcasing his continued dedication to the field. In addition to these accomplishments, Rolf has overseen the successful implementation of an Insurance CRM Platform, facilitated the launch of two distinct Direct Insurance Companies in Switzerland, and managed the implementation of a new Banking Platform, successfully navigating the post-go-live and stabilization phases. Rolf's extensive experience also extends to program management on behalf of government authorities within the Federal Administration, further underscoring his expertise in handling large-scale governmental projects.

name: Charles Blauner
title: President
bio: Charles Blauner is an internationally recognized expert independent advisor on Cyber Resiliency, Information Security Risk Management and Data Privacy. Charles is the President of Cyber Aegis, a boutique cyber risk management consultancy. Charles is also a Partner and the CISO in Residence at Team8 Ventures, a Venture Advisor at the Cyber Mentor Fund, and an Executive in Residence at the Partnership for NYC FinTech Lab. Previously, Charles had a distinguished career working on Information and Cyber Security for over 30 years, 25 years in Financial Services, including being the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) at JP Morgan and Deutsche Bank, and most recently the Global Head of Information Security at Citi. During this time, Charles held numerous industry leadership roles including Chair of the Financial Services Sector Coordinating Council (FSSCC), founding Director of the Financial Services Information Sharing and Analysis Center (FS-ISAC), and the Chair of the OpenGroup’s Security Program. Charles has worked closely with banking regulators around the world (OCC, FRB, BoE, MAS, and HKMA) to help reduce the risk posed by cyber threats to the financial sector at large. Charles is a regular conference speaker and has had the honor of appearing in front of US House and Senate committees. In 2015, Charles was recognized by his peers, winning the Wasserman Award, which recognizes outstanding career achievement and contribution to the Information Systems Audit, Control, Security, Risk Management, and/or Governance professions. Charles has a M.S. in Computer Science from the University of Southern California (USC) and a B.S. in Computer Science from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI)

name: EJ Bodnar
title: Global CXO Marketing Leader
bio: EJ Bodnar is the Global CXO Marketing Leader at Nutanix. EJ is passionate about the intersection of technology and business and is a well-respected technologist, evangelist, story-teller, public speaker, executive-educator, board member and trusted advisor to c-level executives in global enterprises around the world. Prior to joining Nutanix, EJ was a Vice President of Marketing at Hewlett-Packard Enterprise. EJ’s educational background includes an MBA from Northwestern University; and a Master of Science (MSc) Degree from Columbia University’s Executive Master of Science Technology Management Program. EJ is an Author of the Book “C-Suite Conversations”. EJ is also an advocate and evangelist for Bitcoin for Corporations.

name: Adriaan L. Bouten
title: Strategic Advisor
bio: Mr. Bouten is currently serving as a Strategic Advisor at freelance. He is one of the world's leading experts on digital business transformation with over 20 years of experience in digital innovation. Mr. Bouten helps companies achieve their digital goals and navigate the new digital economy. He has a proven ability to build and grow businesses, maximize profitability, and establish successful industry relationships. For eight years, he served as SVP & CIO of the Commodities and Commercial Markets division of McGraw-Hill Financial, a $1B branch of McGraw-Hill Financial's $5B global information organization. There, he developed and successfully executed a large-scale digital transformation program across multiple businesses within McGraw-Hill. He also served for six years as VP, Business Development & Technology at USATODAY.com, leading the development and implementation of innovative digital technologies to facilitate business growth. In early 2014, Mr. Bouten founded Digital Prism Advisors, a company that guides established businesses through their most important investment of the next decade – mastering the digital marketplace. He and his team of experienced consultants understand how any business can become a disruptor in the digital ecosystem through the strategic application of technology. Digital Prism is currently engaged in digital transformation programs with an international bookseller, a large professional membership organization, a prominent think tank, and a proprietary data provider.

name: Miles Braffett
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: Miles Braffett is CTO of Success Academy Charter Schools. He was previously Principal at Lone Star Consulting LLC. Mr. Braffett has in-depth experience creating and executing business focused information technology strategies to build, grow, and transform global music and entertainment companies. He has significant achievements leading IT innovation, technology integrations, and digital initiatives to support major business change cycles. As a leader, Mr. Braffett has a legacy of developing positive organizational culture, hiring and retaining large teams, and driving change across organizations. As the former Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Sony Music Entertainment, Mr. Braffett led a high performing IT team of over 400 with an annual budget of $100M. He led the development of Digital Asset Management capabilities, an Artist Royalties analytics platform, rights management and metadata management systems, and the company’s first cloud-based consumer data analytics platform all in support of the rapid transformation to a digital music market. For the 2004 merger of Sony and BMG, he led the planning and execution of an ambitious technology integration plan and delivered $40million in operating cost savings. Recently, Mr. Braffett led a multi-year program to enhance Sony Music’s IT security posture to industry best practices and reduce overall risk in response to the increased global threats. He also implemented a Global Financial systems suite based on SAP that centralized financial accounting into a single system across 45 countries. Before this, Mr. Braffett was Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer for BMG Entertainment, where he developed and executed global IT strategy, planning and major global projects. He implemented BMG’s EROS Artist Royalty system throughout the North America and Europe covering 27 countries, which was a first in the industry. He served as interim head of BMG’s New Technologies Division for 18 months in addition to his Global CIO position. In this role, he was responsible for establishing commercial relationships with numerous start-up digital music distributors including negotiating BMG’s first Apple iTunes agreement and led the company’s strategy to combat online piracy. Previously Mr. Braffett was Vice President IS&T, North America leading all aspects of IT for BMG in the US. He implemented a Variable Marketing Expense (VME) tracking system which provided comprehensive planning and tracking of recording project development and marketing costs and increased management transparency, eliminating unauthorized project over-spend for the largest controllable expense category in the operation. Prior to BMG, he held several positions at EMI Music including as Senior Director/Director of Marketing Systems where he oversaw the planning, development, and implementation of strategic information systems supporting all functions of EMI’s record labels. Earlier, he was Marketing Analyst and Manager, Administration and Marketing Services in the International Marketing Division. Mr. Braffett holds an M.B.A. and an M.S. in Music, Music Media and Industry from University of Miami, School of Music and a B.S. in Music and Music Theory from University of Texas at Austin. Since 2006, Mr. Braffett has been a student mentor for graduate students at Columbia University, School of Professional Studies who are training to move into executive IT and engineering positions.

name: Thomas Britt
title: EVP & Chief Information Officer
bio: Thomas Britt's current role is EVP & Chief Information Officer at Crocs. In the past, he was a Partner at Keel Consulting. Mr. Britt is the former COO and CIO at Diane von Furstenberg. Before DVF, he spent 17 years at Coach, where he spearheaded a technology strategy that enabled a successful IPO, multiple acquisitions, global expansion and a top-ranked digital presence. Overall revenue growth was more than 14X during that time, with annual sales of over $5 billion in 2012. Prior to joining Coach, Mr. Britt was a consultant with Accenture for eight years. He focused on large-scale system implementations in the consumer products and pharmaceutical industries. Mr. Britt currently serves on a private board for AcuSport. He holds a B.S., Mechanical Engineering from General Motors Institute and an M.B.A from Michigan State University.

name: Eric Brown
title: Founder
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Eric D. Brown, D.Sc., is the Owner of Draco Ventures LLC, an angel investing firm focused on early-stage startup investments and building startups from scratch. He is also the Founder of The Workshop List, a platform that provides marketing services for photographers offering workshops. This service enables photographers to list, promote, and search for workshops. Prior to his current roles, Dr. Brown served as the Chief Information Officer and Partner at Sundial Capital Research, the publisher of SentimenTrader.com, a provider of financial market sentiment research and services for both individual and institutional investors. Dr. Brown holds a Doctor of Science in Information Systems from Dakota State University, specializing in Data Science and Decision Science. His doctoral research focused on analyzing Twitter messages for sentiment and insight in stock market decision-making. This research led him to his current role as CIO at Sundial Capital Research, where he oversees IT and Marketing Technology. With over 20 years of experience in technology across various industries and leadership roles in IT and Marketing Technology, Dr. Brown has a broad background in technology and has led teams in North and South America. He also holds an M.B.A. from The University of Texas at Dallas, a Master of Science in Electrical Engineering from Oklahoma State University, and a Bachelor of Science in Engineering Physics from Southwestern Oklahoma State University.

name: Leslie Brun
title: Founder, Chairman and CEO
bio: Les Brun is Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Ariel Alternatives, LLC. Additionally, he chairs the Investment Committee for the Firm. Les is also Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Sarr Group, LLC, a diversified holding company for his investments, as well as Senior Advisor to G100 and World 50, a private platform of peer-to-peer communities for senior most executives and directors from some of the world’s largest companies. Previously, he was a Managing Director in the New York office of CCMP Capital Advisors. Les also founded and was Chairman Emeritus of Hamilton Lane, the world’s leading Private Equity advisory and management firm. He was also a member of the board of directors of the Richcourt Group, a Hamilton Lane company specializing in hedge fund of funds management. Les is currently Lead Director of Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE “BR”), where he was formerly Chairman of the Board of Directors. He also serves on the Board of Directors of Corning, Inc. (NYSE “GLW”) and Footprint, LLC. Les was formerly Lead Director of Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE “MRK”) until August 2021, Chairman of the Board of Directors of CDK Global, Inc. (NASDAQ “CDK”) until July 2022, Chairman of the Board of Directors of Automatic Data Processing, Inc. (NYSE “ADP”) until November 2015, and Chair of the Compensation Committee of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE “HPE”). He is also a Member of the Council on Foreign Relations. In addition, he was Chairman of the U.S. Small Business Administration’s S.B.I.C. Advisory Council, and a member of the Committee for the Reinvention of the S.B.A. Les has over 42 years of investment banking, commercial banking and financial advisory experience. Les holds a B.S. from the State University of New York at Buffalo, where he has been recognized as a Distinguished Alumnus, and is a former Trustee of the University at Buffalo Foundation, Inc. He is also a former Trustee of Widener University, as well as Episcopal Academy in Merion, PA. Les was recognized by the NACD as one of America’s top 100 Corporate Directors in 2015 and most recently inducted into the 2019 Business Hall of Fame for Philadelphia Inquirer’s Industry Icons.

name: Kurt Brungardt
title: Chief Operating Officer
bio: Kurt Brungardt is the Managing Partner at Swifte, a private equity firm that he founded in 2015. Mr. Swifte invests equity capital in small and medium sized businesses with revenues of $500k - $100mm. His fundamental mandate is to help portfolio company owners, board, and management teams accelerate growth, increase profitability and create new value for both owners and employees. Mr. Brungardt is also a founder and the Chief Operating Officer for Central Shore Holdings, where he is responsible for business operations, organizational design and building processes, teams and technology to support its continued rapid growth. Prior to Central Shore Holdings, Mr. Brungardt served as Chief Information Officer and Managing Director for over 14 years at MSD Capital, L.P., the New York-based private investment firm managing Michael Dell and his family’s fortune. During this time, he worked with both investment and operations teams to enable MSD Capital’s growth to $15 billion in assets under management, while designing and implementing the business process and systems needed by MSD to manage, mine and share its proprietary research. Prior to MSD Capital, Mr. Brungardt spent 12 years in business operations and project roles at Digital Power Networks and General Dynamics Electric Boat Division. Mr. Brungardt co-founded the Hedge Fund Technology Group (HFTG), and currently serves as President of the New York chapter of the Society for Information Management. Kurt is also a mentor for Columbia University's Executive Master of Science in Technology Management Program. Mr. Brungardt holds an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Minnesota, Institute of Technology and is a member of the Aji Network’s Business Professionals program.

name: Lynne Budnovitch
title: Senior Vice President, Operations and Compliance
bio: Lynne Budnovitch is Senior Vice President of Operations and Compliance at Orthogenrx. Prior to Orthogenrx, Ms. Budnovich was Managing Director at UL EduNeering, Inc. Ms. Budnovitch served as the Chief Information Officer at EdisonLearning, Inc. and led its Information Technology team. Prior to Joining in EdisonLearning in 2000, she served as Senior Vice President for Technology Services prior to assuming her current position at EdisonLearning, Inc. in 2008. Ms. Budnovitch served as Vice President of Network Services for Dresdner Klienwort Benson, an investment banking firm. While there, she was responsible for network technologies for the North and South America regions. She served as network engineer for AT&T where she designed and implemented network solutions for clients such as JP Morgan and Pfizer. Ms. Budnovitch holds a B.A. in Mathematics and Computer Science from SUNY and an M.S. in Computer Science from New York University.

name: Thomas Burke
title: President, Tax Services
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Thomas Burke is President of Tax Services at Broadridge Financial Solutions. Prior to Broadridge, Mr. Burke was the former COO at Sterling Talent Solutions. In this role, he led his team with a focused strategy to deliver for Sterling's clients, which included developing global and mobile product platforms, refining the company's fulfillment platform and delivering increased automation through SmartData. Mr. Burke brings more than 20 years of experience in leading complex, technology-driven organizations to this role. He previously served as the Chairman and Group Chief Executive Officer for three technology companies (StoneRiver Insurance Solutions, RegEd and Emerald Connect). Hired by private equity owner Stone Point Capital and Fiserv Inc., his role was to turn these under-performing companies around. Before that, he was SVP and CIO at New York Life International and Head of Operations & Technology for Global Consumer Investments at Citigroup. Mr. Burke holds an M.B.A. in Finance from St. John's University and a B.A. in Computer Science from Fordham University.

name: Ulrich Bäumer
title: Partner
bio: Ulrich Baumer advises clients on drafting and negotiating complex, international, IT project contracts (especially outsourcing) and assists technology clients on M&A transactions. He also advises on all other aspects of technology and licensing law, and has many years of experience in drafting and negotiating all types of IT contracts, including contracts for (SAP) licensing, (agile) software development, maintenance, distribution, and projects, as well as IT-related general terms and conditions of business. He also assists in IT disputes in and out of court. Mr. Baumber's clients include international technology companies and diverse industrial companies (with a focus inter alia on financial services). He also provides legal guidance to many German companies in relation to their involvement in India and is a legal representative of the German Mechanical and Plant Engineering Federation (VDMA) and head of legal of the German IT Purchaser Organisation (VOICE e.V.). According to “Wirtschaftswoche” (2018) and Handelsblatt (2018), Mr. Baumer is one of the best lawyers for IT law in Germany. WHO IS WHO in Germany also lists him for IT law. Chambers & Partners named him one of the leading corporate M&A lawyers related to India. Mr. Baumer studied in Würzburg, Aberystwyth (UK), and Cologne. He completed a postgraduate law degree in 1997 at the George Washington University in Washington, DC. He began his career as a lawyer in 1999 and joined Osborne Clarke in 2004.Mr. Baumer is a guest lecturer (in IT law and M&A for technology companies) at the University of Cologne, at Columbia University (Paris/NY) as well as the National Law School in Bangalore, India, and also teaches “M&A in India” at the Frankfurt School of Finance &Management. He is the author of various articles on IT law. Osborne Clarke was awarded JUVE law firm of the year for Information Technology in 2017.

name: Jason Callahan
title: Chief Information Security Officer
bio: Jason Callahan is the CISO at Lam Research. Mr. Callahan has over 25 years of information security and technology leadership experience at a host of enterprises, including Fortune 150 companies, the Department of Defense, financial institutions, and life sciences and energy corporations. He has a demonstrated, authentic record of developing high-performance organizations. Mr. Callahan began his career in the United States Air Force, where he held both physical and information security functions. After leaving the Air Force, he went on to launch a successful private cybersecurity consulting firm, before co-founding the security program at Illumina, the world's largest biotechnology company. At Illumina, Mr. Callahan formerly served in two professional capacities. As the CISO, he was responsible for developing the enterprise security program strategy and execution, while also defining the security standards for Illumina's high-tech products and cloud-based solutions. As the Head of Global Infrastructure & Operations, Callahan was responsible for supporting all facets of enterprise technology platforms and high- performance computing. Mr. Callahan holds an M.B.A. from the University of Washington's Foster School of Business and a B.A. in IT. He serves on the board of directors for the San Diego CISO Round Table and is the Vice Chairman on the board of directors for Villa Musica, a nonprofit musical arts education center for the entire San Diego County student population.

name: Raphaël Capelli
title: Senior Advisor
bio: A 25-year business and IT consulting veteran and former IBM General Manager, Raphael has been responsible for the strategy, execution and results of many large businesses globally. He also held various positions in marketing and sales and developed an extensive experience in merger and acquisition. As IBM Senior Executive, Raphael directed lately the integration of Promontory Financial Group, a leading consulting firm operating at the intersection of strategy, risk management and regulatory compliance. Raphael's mission was to combine Promontory’s deep industry expertise with cutting edge cognitive technologies and services global capabilities to create a unique set of solutions that helped financial institutions solve their most complex business challenges. Prior to this role, Raphael led a team of more than 5,000 consultants who helped major financial services and insurance firms throughout North America accelerate their digital transformation by redefining their business processes and redesigning their operations using disruptive technologies like AI, advanced analytics and blockchain. Raphael also spent three years in Asia where he managed a team of 1,200 consultants operating in countries like Singapore, Thailand, Indonesia and Malaysia. His mission was to leverage both business and IT transformation solutions and capabilities to develop across South East Asia a strong and sustainable profitable growth, opening new markets in countries like Vietnam and The Philippines. In 2008, Raphael was appointed General Manager for IBM in France and North Africa, where he led a team of 3,000 IT consultants. In four years, he transformed the business by implementing an industry led model integrating both business and IT capabilities as well new technology to deliver differentiated solutions to the clients and helping them transition to the digital age. Raphael’s impressive experience, together with his knowledge and understanding of how to transform corporations using cutting-edge technologies, make him an invaluable change partner in today’s challenging, and intensely competitive, business environment. Main areas of experience and competencies Deep knowledge in Consulting and business transformation using cutting edge technologies like AI, data analytics, blockchain. Extensive experience in managing large Consulting businesses (revenue > $1.2B, 5000 consultants) with direct P&L responsibility and C-level management. 23 years of experience in Financial Services in various positions (Sector Leader, Partner, Services Manager). M&A experience: from disruptive business case to full integration implementation Global experience acquired through various assignments in Europe, Asia and North America

name: Dan Carney
title: Partner
bio: Dan Carney serves as a Partner in New York, where he advises clients across the healthcare ecosystem, nonprofit social services, and higher education sectors. With a strong focus on healthcare systems, Dan brings a strategic approach to his clients’ unique needs, leveraging his extensive experience to create impactful solutions. His work is marked by a commitment to excellence, ensuring positive outcomes for all stakeholders involved. Dan joined DHR in 2006, following a career that includes leadership roles in national search firms and as a Management Consultant at Diamond Technology Partners and CSC Consulting. There, he specialized in reengineering business processes and advancing digital strategies. Dan holds a Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering from North Carolina State University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

name: Vita Cassese
title: Executive Partner
bio: Vita Cassese is the CEO of Mardon Management Advisors, a prominent healthcare technology and analytics advisory firm. With over 30 years of experience in Life Sciences and Healthcare, she has a notable track record. During her tenure at Pfizer, Ms. Cassese held various significant roles, including CIO of Pfizer’s Worldwide Pharmaceuticals Group. She was instrumental in creating and leading Pfizer’s Global Innovation and Strategy organization. Beyond her role as CEO, Vita Cassese serves on the Boards of Loftware, The Medical Knowledge Group, Zitter Health Insights, and Context Matters. She has previously been on the Boards of StayWell and Decision Resources. Ms. Cassese is an active governance member. She currently chairs the Trusteeship and Governance Committee of the College of Mount Saint Vincent's Board of Trustees. She is also a member of the International Women’s Forum and the Women’s Forum of New York. In addition to her extensive experience, Ms. Cassese holds the position of Executive Partner at Health Enterprise Partners, a leading private equity firm in innovative healthcare businesses since 2017. She serves as a Member of the Board of Directors of Onto Innovation (NYSE: ONTO) and Loftware, Inc., a global leader in Enterprise Labeling Solutions. Vita Cassese earned her B.A. in Mathematics from the College of Mount Saint Vincent and an M.B.A. from New York University’s Stern School of Business. Her leadership continues to leave a significant mark on the healthcare and life sciences sectors.

name: Sanjeeb Chaudhuri
title: Chairman/ CEO/ Global Advisor Banking
bio: Sanjeeb Chaudhuri is the Founder and CEO of FasTrack Growth, as well as the Chairman of IDFC First Bank. With over four decades of senior multinational business experience, he has a deep understanding of consumer and commercial markets across India, the rest of Asia, Middle East, Africa, and Europe. He played a crucial role in Citibank's credit cards business expansion in multiple regions. Mr. Chaudhuri has held key positions at major financial institutions, including Standard Chartered Bank, where he led digital transformation and marketing innovation, generating substantial efficiencies. He also drove significant technology investments in analytics, decision management, big data, and cloud computing. He actively supports sustainability goals and has served as an Independent Director on the boards of various companies. Sanjeeb Chaudhuri's academic engagements include visiting faculty positions at prestigious institutions such as Wharton School of Business and Judge Business School, Cambridge. He holds an MBA in Marketing and is recognized as a leading speaker at global marketing and media events. In 2016, he was listed among the Top 25 Media Visionaries in Asia Pacific.

name: Simon Cheung
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: Simon Cheung is CTO of Ping An Puhui. Prior to this role, he was CTO at Fusion Bank. A graduate of the University of Wisconsin-Madison, Mr. Cheung is a seasoned IT expert with more than 20 years of experience in the area of Financial Technology. In addition to his ability to visualize trends internationally to stay a step ahead, he also possesses years of domestic implementation experience where he fully integrates traditional financial industry with an internet mindset. At the age of 25, Mr. Cheung founded an IT consulting company in North America, where he provided services to a number of large international financial institutions including Fidelity Investments, Manulife Financial, Citibank, and other start-ups. In 2007, Mr. Cheung returned to China to join Oracle as Senior Manager of Greater China. He participated in various projects including CLSA, HSBC, Bank of East Asia and Ping An. After the successes of those projects Ping An invited him to join as their General Manager. He participated in the core banking system project of Ping An Bank, smoothly realizing the replacement of the core system of the bank. Later, he worked for the Asia courier industry leader SF Express Group as the IT architecture and operations director, promoting a series of key IT changes and implementing system architecture upgrades for SF Express. In 2014, Mr. Cheung joined the entrepreneurial team and served as the Deputy General Manager of IT at WeBank, led by Tencent. He began to include “Internet DNAs” into the financial cornerstone and led the planning and implementation of the world’s first “non-IOE” banking infrastructure. The benchmark case was selected as one of the “2015 Financial Informatization Top Ten Events” by the “Financial Electronics” magazine, which was under the supervision of the People’s Bank of China, and won the “2015 Asian Internet Banking Technology Innovation Award” and “Southern Gold Brick Awards - Best Internet Finance Innovation Awards” among many other honors. Mr. Cheung then joined Dashu Financial and WeShare Technology as the Chief Technology Officer, integrating financial stability and internet innovative thinking to quickly provide online and offline business technology solutions. With innovative technologies such as artificial intelligence, big data and cloud computing, from standardization and modularization to automation and intelligence, perfection balancing business support and technological trend, Mr. Cheung was recognized by IT Manager World Magazine as “2016 Excellent CIO”. With a solid foundation of financial know-hows and his innovative capabilities, Mr. Cheung accepted the appointment of Bank of Qingdao as an independent director in 2017, further contributing himself to the financial and technology industry.

name: Thomas J. Clarke
title: Independent Director
bio: Thomas J. Clarke currently serves as an Independent Director at Clearstead Trust Company and as a Member Of The Board Of Advisors at Rialto Markets, where he provides valuable insights and guidance. In addition to these roles, Thomas J. Clarke is a Managing Director at KHTC Capital LLC, where he plays a significant role in the company's operations. Prior to this role, he was the CEO at Weiss Group. Before joining Weiss, Mr. Clarke was the CEO of TheStreet.com for ten years, where he provided strategy guiding the multimedia provider of financial commentary, analysis, news, and research. He joined TheStreet.com in 1999, bringing with him 20 years of management experience in the financial services industry, including a range of management positions and experience starting subsidiaries in foreign countries. Prior to joining TheStreet.com, he served as Chief Executive of Thomson Financial Investor Relations (formerly Technimetrics Inc.). In this capacity, Mr. Clarke grew Technimetrics into a global entity that services clients in over 20 countries, and successfully oversaw the 1998 sale of Technimetrics from Knight-Ridder to the Thomson Corporation. His previous management experience included positions at McAuto Systems Corp. and Media Records. Mr. Clarke is also a business information advisor for Plum Holdings L.P., a venture capital fund based in Philadelphia that focuses on early-stage media companies. He serves on the University of Albany's executive advisory board of the Center for Comparative Functional Genomics and on the board of Standing Stone, Inc., developers of disease state management solutions. He holds an M.B.A. from Hofstra University and a B.A. in Marketing from St. John's University.

name: Carol Clements
title: Chief Digital & Technology Officer
bio: Carol Clements is Chief Digital & Technology Officer at JetBlue. Formerly, Ms. Clements served as Chief Technology Officer for Pizza Hut, where she was responsible for enabling growth for Pizza Hut’s U.S. restaurants through technology and digital innovation. She oversaw the brand's e-commerce channels, restaurant & delivery technology, and data & analytics initiatives to power sales and operations across 6,000+ restaurants nationwide. Prior to joining Pizza Hut, Ms. Clements spent 11 years at Southwest Airlines in a variety of technology leadership roles. Prior to that, she began her career as a consultant at PricewaterhouseCoopers, where she led numerous CRM, customer analytics, and eCommerce engagements across the retail, entertainment, telecom and travel industries. Ms. Clements graduated from Southern Methodist University in Dallas, where she earned degrees in Mathematics and Management Science Engineering. She is the 2020 recipient of the Dallas CIO of the Year ORBIE award.

name: David Cole
title: Owner
bio: Mr. Cole has an extensive and diverse leadership and management experience covering IT security, cyber assessments, regulatory assessments, IT audits, and IT operations support. Mr. Cole is currently the owner of SysAudits.com. Mr. Cole held numerous Director of IS Audit positions at: - U.S. House of Representatives Office of Inspector General - Department of Education, Office of Inspector General - Smithsonian Office of Inspector General Leadership included: - Regulatory assessments (ITAR, FISMA, and HIPPA) of company and government IT operations, contract compliance, outsourced data centers, and IAAS, PAAS, and SAAS cloud operations - Drafted national cybersecurity policy for the National Industrial Security Program (NISP) under the Director of National Intelligence - C-Suite presentations and Congressional testimony - Technical testing and training – pentesting, disaster recovery, and others - Forensic and technical support to Federal Agent cybercrimes investigations Mr. Cole held numerous IT Operations positions: - Chief Information Officer, Defense Security Service (DSS): Executive leadership and management oversight for all IT operations to include multiple datacenters, systems engineering, application development, cybersecurity, IT policy, budget and resource planning. Responsible for a $100 million+ annual IT budget and 150+ technical staff supporting 70+ locations. - Director Designated Approving Authority, DSS – CISO for cleared industry with responsibility for certification and accreditation under the NISP of 40,000+ information systems at 14,000+ locations.

name: Matt Comyns
title: Co-Founder, President
bio: Matt Comyns is currently the Co-Founder and President at Artico Search, Inc. In addition to this role, he serves as a Strategic Advisor at ClearSky, leveraging his expertise in the field. Matt was the Managing Partner of the Cyber Security Practice at The Caldwell Partners and a member of the Stamford office. His focus is on recruiting chief information security officers and next-level-down top lieutenants in information security for large global corporations and fast-growing private companies, as well as cyber security consultants for leading professional services firms and top executives for cyber security technology companies. Mr. Comyns previously co-led Russell Reynolds Associates’ Global Cyber Security Practice, within the firm's Technology Sector. Prior to joining the executive search industry, he served as CEO of Pacific Epoch, a consulting firm that specializes in market intelligence and research for U.S.- based companies seeking to invest in and expand into China—until the completion of the firm’s sale. Prior to that, he was a pioneering executive at the beginning of the Internet in San Francisco. Mr. Comyns is a strategic advisor for ClearSky, a venture capital/growth equity firm with offices in Florida, New York, Boston and San Francisco. He previously served as a member of the Board of Directors for Music National Service, a national nonprofit organization and movement that supports music as a strategy for public good. Mr. Comyns holds a B.A. in Political Science from Bucknell University and is conversational in Mandarin.

name: Tim Conway
title: Chief Information Officer
bio: Tim Conway is the CIO and CISO for Amwell, which provides a hybrid healthcare platform for clinicians and patients. Prior to joining Amwell, Tim held executive positions at industry-leading companies such as Salesforce, Hartford Financial Services and Sapient. Tim has extensive experience in executive leadership, digital transformation, SaaS, IT strategy, and consulting. Tim received a BS in Engineering from WPI and an MBA from Babson College. Tim resides in Longmeadow, MA with his wife, Franca and daughter, Alexis.

name: Craig Cuyar
title: SVP & Global CIO
bio: Dr. Craig Cuyar is the SVP and Global CIO at Omnicom Group, a Fortune 200 marketing communications company with $15.5 billion in revenue and 74,000 employees. In this role, he leads the IT organization, driving an IT transformation for more innovative and client-focused technology capabilities. Additionally, Dr. Cuyar is an adjunct faculty of entrepreneurship and innovation and a mentor at Northeastern University's Center for Technology Management and Digital Leadership. With a background in professional services, he has been a CIO in healthcare, advertising, and residential and commercial real estate. Formerly the Global CIO of Cushman & Wakefield, a global commercial real estate services provider, he managed operations in 58 countries for a diverse customer base. Prior to that, he served as the CIO for the Realogy Franchise Group, overseeing brands like Better Homes & Gardens Real Estate, CENTURY 21, and Coldwell Banker. Dr. Cuyar earned his B.A., M.S., and Ph.D. from The Pennsylvania State University and holds an M.B.A. in Finance from Rutgers Business School. A frequent speaker on the business value of IT, he is actively involved in advisory boards in both the technology and professional services sectors. Currently, he serves as the President of the New Jersey Chapter of the Society for Information Management and has contributed to academic institutions as an adjunct faculty member and curriculum designer.

name: Rasul Damji
title: Founding Partner
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Rasul Damji is a Founding Partner at Altasparq. Prior to Altasparq, Mr. Damji was a Founding Partner at KDW Partners. Before that, he was SVP of Global Sales and Product Operations at Raritan, where he established DCIM and power management solutions while maintaining a competitive KVM business. Mr. Damji was responsible for worldwide operations, including end-to-end management of: sales, product development, advanced technology, IT, manufacturing, supply chain, sourcing, customer service and support. He was also involved in the development of strategic reseller and supplier relationships. Prior to joining Raritan, Mr. Damji held senior executive positions at AT&T and Lucent Technologies, launched GovXcel, a software company serving local governments, and co-founded Innovative Capital Partners, a consulting and capital investment firm. Mr. Damji holds a B.S. and M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Boston University and an Executive M.B.A. in Finance and Leadership from NYU - Stern School of Business.

name: Hal Danziger
title: EVP, General Merchandise & Food Service Technology
focus: Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Hal Danziger is EVP, General Merchandise and Food Service Technology at Circana, where he oversees systems development to support retail and consumer insights for thousands of brands and retailers. Mr. Danziger is the former CTO at DNAinfo.com, where he led product and technology for their award-winning neighborhood news web sites and spearheaded the development of a social network for neighborhoods. Previously, Mr. Danziger was the VP of Product Management and Digital Platforms at Condé Nast, where he drove current and next generation product offerings across the fragmented device landscape, including Condé Nast’s web sites and digital magazines. Danziger also drove cross-brand initiatives focused on community and social platforms, e-commerce, content management and content licensing. Prior to joining Condé Nast, Mr. Danziger was the CTO at New York Magazine and Menupages. He built up the brands’ top digital presences, garnered numerous awards for their suite of web sites and mobile apps, and helped develop profitable digital businesses. Mr. Danziger has also held technology and product leadership roles at CBS and Cablevision, providing expertise in all areas of digital product and technology including advertising, content management, video and social. Mr. Danziger received his Executive M.S. in Technology Management from Columbia University and his B.S. in Computer Science at Stony Brook University.

name: James Daquino
title: Founder/Investor
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: James M. Daquino currently serves as Founder and Investor at ESports Entertainment, LLC. Prior to this, he held the position of Executive, Global Service Delivery at Creative Artists Agency, and earlier, he was the Senior Vice President, IT at ICM Partners. Mr. Daquino is an ITIL certified operations executive and technology entrepreneur with over twenty years of broad-based professional experience. He possesses exceptional problem-solving ability, strategic leadership skills, excellent technical proficiency, organizational expertise, and strong decision-making capabilities. His extensive background includes managing data center operations, back-office critical environments, and infrastructure, along with expertise in DevOps engineering. As a corporate technology professional, Mr. Daquino has built an outstanding career in various roles, including serving as a CIO, Enterprise Architect, and Program Executive for several Fortune 500 companies such as Prudential, Interpublic Group (IPG), Warner Bros. Studios, The Walt Disney Company, Amgen, and Unisys. In addition to his corporate background, Mr. Daquino has played a pivotal role in founding and investing in a number of early-stage startups. Some of these startups were under his direct management, and he successfully raised capital for them, leading to significant multi-million dollar returns for investors. Mr. Daquino holds a B.S. in Management Science and Statistics from California State University, Northridge.

name: Phiroz Darukhanavala
title: Retired VP and Chief Technology Officer
bio: Dr. Phiroz (Daru) Darukhanavala is a seasoned professional with a rich and diverse career. He retired as the Vice President and Chief Technology Officer (CTO) for Information Technology and Services at BP. In this key role, he led a dynamic team responsible for swiftly and efficiently aligning external technology innovation with BP's business needs, employing a venture capital-style approach to rapid adoption. His team played a pivotal role as a catalyst for change within the BP group, applying digital technologies and services to instigate significant business process transformations and cost efficiencies across all business segments. The team's initiatives were often "game-changing," consistently delivering annual impacts exceeding $50 million.
