Additionally, they accelerated the adoption of emerging technologies by organizing executive education and awareness programs, aimed at increasing senior executives' understanding of the business opportunities created by emerging digital technology trends. As the VP of IT Integration, Mr. Darukhanavala led critical IT integration programs for the BP-Amoco merger and the Arco acquisition. These programs collectively aimed at achieving over $500 million in annual bottom-line IT cost reductions. In his previous role as the Director of Global IT Services for the BP Group, he was responsible for establishing standardized IT infrastructure services on a global scale. His extensive career at BP also included serving as the IT Functional Chief for BP Exploration globally, based in London, and as the Chief Information Officer (CIO) for BP Exploration in the Continental U.S. and Alaska. Before his association with BP, Dr. Darukhanavala served as the Director of the Corporate Supercomputing Center and Head of the Corporate Management Science Group for The Standard Oil Co. Furthermore, he gained experience as a consultant on various projects in both industrial and healthcare sectors. He also shared his knowledge as an adjunct professor of management at several universities in the United States. Since his retirement, Dr. Darukhanavala has remained active in the field. He has taken on numerous consulting assignments and has been involved on the boards of several companies, ensuring that he stays well-connected and informed about industry and digital advancements. Dr. Darukhanavala's academic achievements include holding a Master of Science and Ph.D. in Operations Research from Case Western Reserve University, as well as a Bachelor of Technology in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay. His extensive background and contributions underscore his significant impact on the technology and business landscape.

name: Emmanuel David
title: Member Board of Directors
bio: Emmanuel David is a Member of the Board of Directors at Aster DM Healthcare, one of the largest private healthcare service providers operating in the GCC and India. His career spans across various sectors, including the automobile, hospitality, infrastructure, financial services, and oil & gas industries. He has experience working in family-owned entities, joint ventures, and multinational corporations, covering the entire organizational life cycle from start-up to growth, downsizing, and turnaround. With over three decades of real-life experience, he has gained valuable insights into the relationships between culture, strategy, and decision-making. Emmanuel David served as the Director of Tata Management Training Centre (TMTC), the learning arm of the Tata Group's Human Resources. During his tenure, he introduced changes in the pedagogy to facilitate informal and unconventional learning and delivered well-received sessions on topics like Understanding Self, Conflict Management, Negotiation, and Ethics. He received the "British Gas Chairman's Safety Award" for his leadership in relief and rehabilitation efforts during the Surat Floods in 2006. His role in this crisis became a case study at IIM Ahmedabad, highlighting the leadership role of HR during times of crises. He has successfully resolved legacy industrial disputes in various companies and elevated employer-employee relations in greenfield ventures. Emmanuel David is a certified Belbin Team Role Facilitator from the UK and an Advanced Hogan Coach from the USA. He is an alumnus of XLRI and has completed an Advanced Management Program on Strategic Profitability Management from AIMA. In addition to his corporate involvement, he is dedicated to community activities, serving on the boards of St. Peter's High School in Panchgani and Under the Mango Tree (UTMT), a social enterprise promoting beekeeping to enhance the livelihoods of marginal farmers in India. Emmanuel David is also a versatile creative individual, being an avid blogger, writer, photographer, and experimental chef.

name: Tim Dawson
title: CISO
bio: Tim Dawson is the Chief Information Security Officer at UBS. Previously, he served as the EVP at Wells Fargo and was the CTO of Cybersecurity at HSBC. In the latter role, he provided long-term planning and technical leadership for various Cybersecurity disciplines to the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and key stakeholders across different lines of business. Tim's team was primarily responsible for driving Cybersecurity innovation and transformative initiatives, with a focus on investments in early-stage security companies. Before joining HSBC in 2016, Mr. Dawson led Cyber Technology at JP Morgan Chase, where he played a pivotal role in transforming the organization into a forward-thinking and technically proficient entity in the realm of Cybersecurity. He also oversaw various incubation projects, early-stage investments, and presented awards to companies such as Splunk (IPO 2012), FireEye (IPO 2013), Tanium, and Menlo Security. With over 20 years of experience in IT and Cybersecurity, Mr. Dawson has held multiple leadership positions in various organizations, including heading Security Operations and Security Engineering.

name: Dana Deasy
title: Chief Information Digital Officer and Senior Vice President
bio: Confirmed by the U.S. Senate in December 2019 to January 20, 2021, the Honorable Dana Deasy was formly the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer (DoD CIO). He was the primary advisor to the Secretary of Defense for matters of information management, information technology, and information assurance, as well as non-intelligence space systems, critical satellite communications, navigation and timing programs, spectrum, and telecommunications. Mr. Deasy has more than 38 years of experience leading and delivering large scale IT strategies in projects. He previously held several private sector senior leadership positions, most recently as Global Chief Information Officer (CIO) of JPMorgan Chase. There, he was responsible for the firm’s technology systems and managed a budget of more than $9 billion and over 40,000 technologists supporting JPMorgan Chase’s Retail, Wholesale, and Asset Management businesses. Earlier in his career, Mr. Deasy served as the Chief Information Officer and Group Vice President at BP and as CIO for General Motors North America, Tyco International, and Siemens Americas. He also held several senior leadership positions at Rockwell Space Systems Division, including as Director of Information Management for Rockwell’s space shuttle program. He was inducted into the CIO Hall of Fame in 2012 and the International Association of Outsourcing Professionals Hall of Fame in 2013, named Transformational CIO in 2017, and inducted into the 2019 Wash100 leaders.

name: Ted DellaVecchia
title: CEO and Managing Partner
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Ted DellaVecchia is Chief Executive Officer and Managing Partner of Symbotix, a management consulting firm with a long record of building, implementing, and advancing digital health innovations. He and his team serve startup, growth-stage, and enterprise-class healthcare organizations by enabling and accelerating the adoption bridge between entrepreneurial achievements and legacy healthcare ecosystems. Mr. DellaVecchia inspires and leads IT-enabled reinvention of business operating models at large academic medical centers, national payers, health systems and alliances, and other organizations in the healthcare industry. Mr. DellaVecchia is the founding chairman of the Healthcare Industry Transformation Project (HITP). Founded in 2011, the HITP is a digital-health innovation ecosystem of early-adopting healthcare organizations collaborating to invoke true reinvention of the healthcare industry. He is also a strategic advisor and the Business Transformation Executive for Bluedoor (bluedoor.us/) and sponsoring partner at DHIT (dhitglobal.org/). Prior to Symbotix, Mr. DellaVecchia led IT-enabled business transformations at several Fortune 100 enterprises across multiple industries. Examples include: delivered global elements of a corporate turnaround at IBM Corporation, defined and advanced world-wide supply-chains and drove earnings growth for Starbucks Coffee Company, and as senior vice president / CIO restructured IT divisions into high performance engines while innovating enterprise business strategies for three multi-billion dollar Blue Cross Blue Shield health insurance companies. Mr. DellaVecchia has served as sponsor and advising board member of the Center for Information Systems Research at MIT Sloan School of Management, has performed as guest lecturer at MIT Sloan and frequently speaks at industry events on the topic of accelerating/assimilating use of digital health solutions for symbiotic ecosystem interactions. He also served on Boards of Directors for companies transcending successful Initial Public Offerings and those pioneering the use of Customer Relationship Management (CRM) methodologies in healthcare.

name: Giri Devanur
title: Founder & CEO
bio: Giri Devanur is CEO of ReAlpha Tech Corp. Mr. Devanur is actively involved as a Board Member at Saara Inc, a Director at Taazu, and holds roles as Co-Founder at GenDeep, among his various contributions in the corporate world. Mr. Devanur served as the President and CEO of Ameri100 Inc., since its inception in November 2013. In 4 years, he scaled Ameri from $0 to $50M in revenue and completed the IPO on Nasdaq in under 4 years. He was awarded the E&Y Entrepreneur of the Year in 2017. He has also authored a book titled “Nothing to Nasdaq”. Mr. Devanur is a seasoned CEO who has raised seed capital, venture capital and private equity from global institutions. He has more than 25 years of experience in the information technology industry. In 2010, he founded WinHire Inc., an innovative company that builds software products through technology and human capital management experts and combines them with professional services. Mr. Devanur served as WinHire’s President and CEO. He also co-founded Ivega Corporation in 1997, an international niche IT consulting company with special focus on financial services. Ivega merged with TCG in 2004, creating a 1,000+ person focused differentiator in the IT consulting space. Mr. Devanur holds an M.S. in Technology Management from Columbia University and a B.S. in Computer Engineering from the University of Mysore, India. He has attended Executive Education programs at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and Harvard Law School. The Board believes that his qualifications to serve on the Board include his substantial experience in the information technology industry and his prior experience as a CEO.

name: Sarah Diamond
title: Independent Non-Executive Director
bio: Sarah Diamond is an Executive Leadership Mentor, Board Member, and Advisor. She is a Former Global Managing Director of Financial Services at IBM. In addition to her previous role at IBM, she currently holds the position of Independent Non-Executive Director at Quantexa and Dubber, where she provides valuable insights and guidance. Sarah is also a Member at Extraordinary Women on Boards (EWOB) and serves as an Executive Mentor with The ExCo Group. Sarah Diamond is a seasoned executive with deep experience in the financial services, technology, consulting and regulatory sectors. Curious, collaborative, and strategic, she is adept at framing complex issues and helping Fortune 100 companies thrive in rapidly changing business, technology, and regulatory landscapes. Over the course of her 30 plus year career, Diamond has worked with most major banks, Wall Street firms and regulators. A global leader, she has led teams across cultures and time zones in Europe, the United Kingdom, China, Japan, and the U.S. As the Global Managing Director, Financial Services at IBM, Diamond managed a $10B P&L and was responsible for helping top 50 global financial services clients embrace digitization to grow their businesses while creating customer value within the rapidly changing regulatory, business and technology landscape. Diamond was engaged in a wide range of governing roles at IBM, as an active member of the Performance Team (comprised of the top P&L leaders in the company), the Academy of Technology Advisory Board, the Culture Think Tank, and the Global Industry Academy where she was the founding president. She was also a sponsor of IBM’s Technical Women Program, an initiative focused on ensuring that IBM has a vibrant talent pool of qualified women in technical professions. Additionally, she served as Vice Chair of the Advisory Board of Directors and senior advisor to the Chairman and CEO of the Promontory Financial Group, an IBM company. In this role, Diamond provided technology and domain expertise in identifying the opportunity, leading the acquisition and optimizing brand, IP, client, talent and cultural synergies upon the successful execution of the merger. Previously, Diamond served as Strategy Leader for IBM’s Global Business Industries, General Manager of Global Industries where she created a framework to drive innovation and impact across IBM’s 12 industry sectors, and General Manager of Global Consulting Services where she was responsible for strategy, execution and business results in more than 170 countries. Prior to joining IBM, she served as senior vice president in charge of capital markets at KPMG, after launching her career in international banking at Chemical Bank in London. Diamond is an executive mentor and coach with The ExCo Group, a global firm of experienced CEOs, independent non-executive directors and and global business leaders. She is also an independent non-executive board member and advisory board member of several technology companies, based in the UK, Australia and India, and an Executive-in-Residence and Industry Advisor at Northeastern University's Center for Technology Management and Digital Leadership. Her not-for-profit commitments focus on education and women. She volunteers with PENCIL, a NYC based non-profit that connects public school students to the business community, and The Business Council for Peace (Bpeace) that creates jobs in crisis-affected communities through pro bono coaching and consulting services for promising entrepreneurs. She is also a board member of the American Friends of St. Hilda's College, University of Oxford, and past board member and President of the Women’s Bond Club of New York. Diamond holds an M.A. in International Relations from Johns Hopkins University, where she held a Fulbright-Hayes Fellowship, and an M.A. in Modern History from the University of Oxford. She lives in New York with her husband, a professor at New York University. She has two adult children, also with careers in education.

name: Nicholas Donofrio
title: Founder
bio: Nicholas Donofrio is the founder of NMD Group LLC, a strategic consulting firm specializing in projects aimed at advancing healthcare through data and analytics. With a remarkable 44-year tenure at IBM, he played a pivotal role in shaping the company's technology and innovation strategies until his retirement in October 2008. During his time at IBM, Mr. Donofrio held various significant positions, including Vice Chairman of the IBM International Foundation and Chairman of the Board of Governors for the IBM Academy of Technology. His responsibilities spanned diverse areas, encompassing IBM Research, Governmental Programs, Technical Support & Quality, Corporate Community Relations, and Environmental Health & Product Safety. Additionally, he oversaw the senior executives responsible for IBM's enterprise on-demand transformation. Mr. Donofrio made significant contributions to IBM by leading the development and retention of the company's technical talent, fostering diversity in culture and thought within the technical community. In recognition of his exceptional contributions, he was honored as an IBM Fellow in 2008, which is IBM's highest technical distinction. Nicholas Donofrio's association with IBM traces back to his early days as a college co-op student in 1964 when he worked on memory technology for the renowned IBM System/360 mainframe computing system. Over the years, he received several honorary doctorates, including those in Engineering from Polytechnic University in 1999, in Sciences from the University of Warwick in England in 2002, in Technology from Marist College in 2005, and in Sciences from the University of Edinburgh in Scotland in 2006. Further, he was awarded honorary doctorates in Sciences from Pace University in 2009 and the National University of Ireland, Maynooth in 2010. Mr. Donofrio is also a prolific inventor, holding seven technology patents. He is a member of numerous technical and science honor societies, a Fellow of the Institute for Electrical and Electronics Engineers, a Fellow of the UK-based Royal Academy of Engineering, and a member of the US-based National Academy of Engineering. He is also a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Nicholas Donofrio is actively involved in corporate governance and serves on various boards, including the Board of Directors for the Bank of New York/Mellon, the Board of Trustees at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, the Board of Directors of Liberty Mutual, the Board of Directors of AMD, the Board of Managers of the Delphi Company, and the Board of Trustees of The MITRE Corporation. He is a Senior Fellow of the Kauffman Foundation. In 2010, he was appointed as a member of the U.S. Secretary of Energy's Advisory Board (SEAB), further underscoring his commitment to contributing to the advancement of science and technology.

name: Thomas Doughty
title: Chief Information Security Officer
bio: Tom Doughty is an accomplished professional with extensive experience in cybersecurity, currently holding the position of Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) at Generate:Biomedicines. As the CISO, Tom plays a pivotal role in safeguarding the organization's information and technology assets. Previously, Tom served as the Vice President and CISO at Prudential, where he retired from his role in June 2023. Throughout his tenure as CISO since 2006, Tom led the global information and cybersecurity program, overseeing infrastructure-level protections, security-related policies and standards, and security operations in a highly regulated, cloud-enabled business environment. At Prudential, Tom spearheaded business-focused security initiatives, including a dynamic threat and vulnerability management program. He directly engaged with Prudential's Board and senior team, aligning management focus with emerging exposures. Tom also played a crucial role externally, representing Prudential's Information Security Program as a cross-industry leader. He collaborated with peer companies, government agencies, law enforcement, and intelligence sources to assimilate best practices and enhance the collective security landscape. Notably, he provided primary oversight and management of Information Technology-related regulatory liaison and response during Prudential's designation as the only non-bank Systemically Important Financial Institution (SIFI). Before joining Prudential, Tom served as a Military Intelligence Officer in the United States Army from 1993 to 1998, achieving the rank of Captain. His specialization during this period included Electronic and Information Warfare, focusing on emerging intelligence processing technology platforms. In addition to his role at Generate:Biomedicines, Tom currently serves as an Advisory Board Member at SecurityScorecard and Anomali, contributing valuable insights and guidance to these organizations in the realm of cybersecurity. Furthermore, he is actively involved in the Wall Street Technology Association as a Board of Directors member and has served on the board of Financial Top-Level Domain Services (fTLD), LLC until June 2023. Tom also dedicates his expertise as an advisor and mentor within Columbia University's Graduate School of Professional Studies and Northeastern University's Center for Technology Management & Digital Leadership. Tom holds a bachelor's degree in Political Science from the United States Military Academy at West Point (1993) and earned an MBA with a dual focus on Finance and Information Technology from New York University's Stern School of Business (2003). Additionally, he maintains a current Top Secret / SCI security clearance, attesting to his commitment to high standards of security and confidentiality.

name: Robert Duncan
title: Group Chief Information Security Officer
bio: Robert Duncan serves as the Group Chief Information Security Officer for Ardagh​​ Group, a global supplier of sustainable, infinitely recyclable, metal and glass packaging for brand owners around the world. Ardagh operates 65 metal and glass production facilities in 16 countries, employing 20,000 people with sales of approximately $10bn, producing over 35 billion containers a year. He is also very active on the European speaking circuit, attending and speaking at events such as the Financial Times Cyber Security Summit, the e-Crimes congress, EU focused conferences in Brussels and various others. Mr. Duncan has extensive international experience, having been based in New York, London, Hong Kong, and Australia. Prior to Ardagh, he was CISO of Direct Line Group, a large UK Insurance firm for over six years. Additional past experience includes being CISO of the Euronext Stock Exchange, which lists roughly 7 trillion Euro of financial products, along with past experience in Cyber Security and Technology at ANZ, Prudential plc, and Maersk.. Mr. Duncan holds a BA from Florida State University, an L.L.B. in Law (London), M.B.A. (Cranfield UK), and a postgraduate qualification in Executive IT Management from Columbia University in New York.

name: Nicolas Economou
title: Chair, Initiative on AI and the Rule of Law
focus: Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Nicolas Economou currently serves as Chair, Initiative on AI and the Rule of Law at The Future Society. Mr. Economou previously served for two decades as the Chief Executive of H5, a San Francisco-based company that was a pioneer in advancing the application of artificial intelligence in the law and in advocating norms for its trustworthy governance. He also currently chairs the Law Committee of the IEEE Global Initiative on Ethics of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems and leads The Future Society’s Law, Science, and Society Initiative. He serves as Principal Coordinator of The Athens Roundtable on Artificial Intelligence and the Rule of Law, a forum founded in 2019 under the auspices of the President of the Hellenic Republic. He is a member of the International Advisory Board of the European Parliament’s Panel for the Future of Science and Technology (STOA) and of the Council on Extended Intelligence (CXI), a joint initiative of the MIT Media Lab and IEEE-SA. Nicolas Economou was trained in political science at the Graduate Institute of International Studies of the University of Geneva. He holds an M.B.A. from the Wharton School of Business and chose to forgo completion of his M.P.A at Harvard’s Kennedy School in order to co-found H5.

name: Gary Edwards
title: Co-Founder and Principal
bio: Gary Edwards is a seasoned industry leader and management consultant, with decades of experience advising growth-focused business leaders in Canada, the US, and the UK. Gary is recognized for his expertise in leveraging data-driven insights to enhance market positions and customer loyalty. Exceptional evidence-based leadership skills combined with practical experience overseeing the growth of sales, marketing, client success, and data science. As co-founder of Golfdale Consulting, Gary’s mission is to solve complex business challenges concentrating on three areas: global market research, the application of data science, and advocacy for evidence-based regulatory reform. Current research work focuses on cybersecurity, privacy, and financial inclusion. Gary previously was co-owner and Chief Customer Officer at Empathica (now InMoment), a software company pioneering Customer Experience Management. Prior, Gary served as SVP and Country Manager of Gallup Canada, providing strategic market insights to global industries. Gary began his early career in research as an epidemiologist in the field of mental health. Gary has a Hon BA (Psych) from the University of Western Ontario and an M.A. (Psych), PhD (Social Work) from Wilfrid Laurier University specializing in social research methods and statistics.

name: Joseph Eng
title: Chief Technology Office
bio: Joseph Eng serves as the Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Billtrust and holds the position of Senior Advisor at Sixth Street Growth Partners. Before joining Billtrust, Mr. Eng served as the Chief Information Officer (CIO) of TravelClick, a SaaS provider specializing in technology solutions for the travel and hospitality industry. He also held the role of Managing Partner at TBH Holdings, an investment holdings group. Prior to his tenure at TravelClick and TBH Holdings, Mr. Eng was the Executive Vice President (EVP) and CIO of JetBlue, where he was responsible for shaping the airline's IT strategy, development, and execution. His leadership and achievements, along with his team, were recognized by CIO Magazine when they received the CIO 100 award in 2009. Mr. Eng has previously held the position of CIO at SWIFT, where he led product development, technology, and operations for the global provider of mission-critical financial messaging services. His accomplishments at SWIFT, along with his teams, earned recognition with the CIO 100 award in both 2005 and 2006. Joseph Eng also served as the President and CIO of Spectrum Systems, a company offering IT solutions and services to both public and private sectors. He has held leadership positions within the telecommunications industry at Ameritech (now part of AT&T) and Bellcore, which has since been renamed Telcordia Technologies. Beyond his professional roles, Mr. Eng is the owner of the Gary SouthShore RailCats, a professional baseball team based in Gary, Indiana. Additionally, he recently joined the board of directors at Team4Tech, an organization working on technology projects to enhance education in underserved communities.

name: Clive England
title: Managing Director & Head of Business Development
bio: Clive England is Managing Director and Head of Business Development at Tradition, Inc. a leading Interdealer Broker specializing in over-the-counter financial and commodity related products across 28 countries. Mr. England is a successful financial executive with over 25 years experience as a derivative broker specializing in Swiss, German and Spanish international and domestic fixed income trading and as a software provider, tailoring fully electronic, hybrid trading and market data solutions to the financial markets. He brings to the table a strong understanding of the financial markets, modern e-commerce principals and leadership skills applicable to driving businesses forward in today’s agile environment. Mr. England holds an M.B.A. from London Metropolitan University in Business Process Re-engineering and a Post Graduate Diploma in Management Studies, DMS.

name: Richard Entrup
title: Managing Director, Head of Emerging Solutions - Enterprise Innovation
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Richard Entrup is the Managing Director and Head of Emerging Solutions - Enterprise Innovation at KPMG US, where he previously held the position of Senior Advisor for Enterprise Innovation. He is an esteemed startup advisor and investor, known for his global technology leadership. Throughout his career, Mr. Entrup has taken the helm of technology, innovation, and digital transformation initiatives at renowned global brands such as Verizon, Christie's, Disney/ABC, Time Warner, The Museum of Modern Art, Viacom, and Tiffany & Company. He has also served as a Chief Information Officer (CIO) at several healthcare companies and a major international law firm, demonstrating a track record of successfully aligning technology investments with strategic goals. In his most recent role as Managing Director for 5G & Edge Innovation at Verizon Business Group, Mr. Entrup focused on helping enterprise clients harness the full potential of 5G, edge computing, and emerging technologies to drive digital transformation and innovation. Previously, as the Global Chief Information Officer and Chief Digital Officer (CDO) at Christie's, he led a significant digital transformation program, marked by pioneering achievements in the art and technology space. Notable milestones included the first blockchain-based auction, an augmented reality virtual hanging app, predictive analytics, and computer vision applications using artificial intelligence and machine learning. Additionally, he introduced innovations such as online mobile bidding during live auctions. In 2018, Mr. Entrup played a pivotal role in launching the inaugural Christie's Art & Tech Conference, with a focus on blockchain and the introduction of NFTs to the traditional art world. Mr. Entrup extends his expertise as a mentor to students in the Executive Masters in Technology Management Program at Columbia University. He is also a Startup Mentor for leAD Sports Startup Accelerator and provides guidance to the venture capital community as a CIO Advisory Board member for Greylock Partners, Lightspeed Venture Partners, the Mayfield Fund, and HMG Strategy. In addition, he holds strategic advisory roles at House of First, an NFT platform for artists; ClubNFT, a backup and search tool for NFT collectors; Edge Video, an AI video player; and InfoSec Global, a pioneer in cryptographic agility management solutions.

name: Andres Araya Falcone
title: Board member
bio: Andrés Araya Falcone is currently the Executive Vice President and Chief Business Officer of the Santiago Stock Exchange and CCLV Clearing and Settlement in Chile. He also serves as a Board member at Fintree Financial Services. In his role, Mr. Araya Falcone leads the commercial and customer experience team as well as the development of new products and services. Formerly, he was Chief Technology and Information Officer of the Santiago Stock Exchange. Mr. Araya Falcone has worked with broker dealers and securities service technology providers for over 30 years in Chile and Latin America. Since 2017, he has focused on innovation and distributed ledger technology (DLT) on securities services and has experience engaging with fintech start-ups related to DLT solutions with the AUNA Blockchain Consortium. Mr. Araya Falcone has entrepreneurial experience co-founding tutenlabs.com, and developing sharing economy business with offices in Chile, Mexico, Colombia, and US. He is a Finance Professor for the Master Information Technology Program (MTIG) at Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile. He has been named 2017 CIO of the Year by the CETIUC (Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile). Mr. Araya Falcone holds a B.S. in Information Technology from UDP, Santiago, Chile and an M.B.A. from Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile. He holds certificates for: Financial Risk from Universidad de Chile; Advanced Financial Management from Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile; and the Executive Program for Growing Companies from Stanford University Graduate School of Business.

name: Robert Farina
title: CEO
bio: Robert E. Farina is CEO at Magna5. Mr. Farina is an experienced CEO of private equity and venture capital backed technology companies with over 30 years of experience leading start-ups, turnarounds and high growth enterprises in the software-as-a-service (“SaaS”), business process outsourcing ("BPO"), professional services, payment processing and packaged software business segments. Prior to Magna5, Mr. Farina was CEO of Kim Technologies, a global SaaS software company focused on leveraging artificial intelligence in the development and deployment of business applications. In addition to his experience successfully running multiple businesses backed by professional investors, Mr. Farina has served on a number of Boards of Directors, started and successfully exited his own business, and ran a division of a publicly held company. He has been a finalist for the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year award for the State of New Jersey three times in his career and serves on the Advisory Board of Columbia University Master’s Degree program for Information Technology Management. Mr. Farina is a graduate of the Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania.

name: Charlie Federman
title: Board Member
bio: Charlie Federman is a founding Partner with Silvertech Ventures, a venture fund and a hub affiliated with the Silverstein family in New York City. Complementing these efforts, he is also CEO of SILVERspac, a public company. Charlie is a well-known investor and advisor to technology companies and was a Partner with the Israeli/US venture firm BRM, whose partners are best known for co-founding the security company Checkpoint. He is currently on the boards of Semperis, SQream Technologies, TrackerDetect, and iGentify. Charlie has recently taken on new roles as a board member at Alma Security and Onyxia Cyber, further expanding his commitment to the technology and cybersecurity sectors. Prior to Silvertech, he was an active personal investor and was the seed investor and board member in Payoneer (NASDAQ “PAYO”). Before joining BRM, Mr. Federman spent 15 years, culminating as Chairman with Broadview, where he advised on more than 100 acquisitions of software companies and was instrumental in establishing its affiliated venture relationships and growth in Europe and Silicon Valley. Mr. Federman holds a B.S. in Economics from the Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania. His extensive experience, leadership, and dedication to the technology industry continue to make a significant impact.

name: Michael Fegan
title: Chief Technology and Operations Officer
bio: Michael Fegan is EVP and CTO at Dime Community Bank. In this role, Mr. Fegan leads the IT Operations Area, the IT Business Relationship Management Function as well as the Innovation and Enterprise Data Management teams. He has is tasked with spearheading the ongoing transformation of the bank’s digital and technology platforms while focusing on creating a culture of leadership, personal growth, high morale and change resiliency. Prior to joining Dime, Mr. Fegan served as the Chief Information and Operations Officer of Investors Bank. The business functions he directed included Information Technology, The Client Care Center, Enterprise Project Management Office, Deposit Services, Cash Management and Loan Servicing for both Commercial and Residential clients. He was the Executive Sponsor for the banks CRM Transformation (Salesforce) as well as the implementation of a new Loan Origination System (nCino). Prior to joining Investors Bank, Mr. Fegan was Chief Operations and Technology Officer at Bank Leumi. He built, led and evolved an organization, with responsibility for Banking and Brokerage Operations, as well as Technology, Information Security, Program Management, Business Relationship Management and Vendor management. He was the executive sponsor who managed the transformation of the bank’s core banking system. Upon completion, the bank achieved significant growth in deposits, lending, and cash management services, which ultimately produced large increases in revenue and profits. Previously, he served as CIO for Suffolk County National Bank where he helped formulate the company’s strategic vision and deployed more advanced technologies to launch mobile banking and improve enterprise data management, fraud detection, online account opening, business intelligence, and disaster recovery. He also managed the day-to-day execution of Bank Operations, Information Technology, Program Management Office, and Information Security. Earlier in his career, Mr. Fegan held Management positions at Omgeo (subsidiary of DTCC and Thomson Reuters), JPMorgan Chase, Citibank and Andersen Consulting. Mr. Fegan sits on the Board of Directors for QSAC (Quality Services for the Autism Community) and ART/NY (Alliance of Resident Theatres/New York). Mr. Fegan is a graduate of Northwestern University and holds a B.S. in Education and Social Policy with a concentration in Business Management and Organizational Studies.

name: Conrad Fernandes
title: Chief Commercial Officer
bio: Conrad Fernandes is the Chief Commercial Officer at LI-COR Environmental. Previously, he served as the Vice President of Sales and Marketing at Teledyne LeCroy, a leader in electronic test and measurement instrumentation. Before that, he was the Vice President of Asia Business Development for the Electronic and Environmental Measuring business at Teledyne Corporation. Teledyne provides enabling technologies for industrial growth markets, including aerospace and defense, deepwater oil and gas, oceanographic research, air and water quality monitoring, electronic design, factory automation, and medical imaging. Fernandes earned an Electronic Engineering degree from NIHE (now the University of Limerick) in Ireland and an MBA from City University Business School (now Cass Business School) in London. He is currently a PhD candidate at the International School of Management in Paris, focusing on managing risk in foreign direct investment. Fernandes has lived and worked in the UK, Ireland, Switzerland, and China and is now based in New York. He speaks French, Mandarin Chinese proficiently, and German and Japanese with limited proficiency. Reflecting his interest in international business, he recently presented a paper on the venture capital industry in China at the 2016 IABD conference.

name: Christopher Ferreri
title: Chief Operating Officer
bio: Chris Ferreri is Chief Operating Officer (COO) at Hartfield, Titus, and Donnelly. He previously was an electronic brokerage pioneer with more than 30 years of experience in business development, product positioning, systems design, development and deployment. He is well regarded in the financial services industry for his deep understanding of complex financial concepts, market structures and regulatory impacts and his ability to explain these complexities in clear terms. It was his ability to communicate effectively that led Mr. Ferreri to his leadership of ICAP’s advocacy efforts in Washington, D.C. in 2008. As an integral component of the Dodd Frank legislative process, he created the Educate then Influence program and has helped influence effective governance and compliance. He was elected the inaugural Chairmanship of the Wholesale Markets Brokers Association, Americas and served in that role for three terms. Working at the center of the over the counter financial markets, Mr. Ferreri has developed key skills in strategic planning and consensus building within the context of accelerating change. The systems which he helped to design remain the predominant trading platform for the interdealer market across the globe. Mr. Ferreri spent more than three decades as a leader at ICAP, the world’s premier Interdealer Broker where he led the efforts to design, develop and deliver their hybrid voice and electronic platforms. He is currently Chief Operating Officer for Hartfield, Titus and Donnelly and CEO of his consulting agency, Eight Point Strategies, LLC. Mr. Ferreri holds a B.E. in Electrical Engineering at Stevens Institute of Technology and is an active alumnus, serving on the Advisory Board of the School of Systems and Enterprises and is a founding Board Member for the University’s Financial Systems Center. He is dedicated to the mentoring of graduate students for Columbia University’s Executive Master of Science in Technology Management program and was awarded the University’s inaugural Mentor of the Year.

name: Kevin Field
title: VP of IT/CIO and Business Operation
bio: Kevin Field currently holds the position of VP of IT/CIO and Business Operations at Fairco. He brings a wealth of experience and expertise to his roles, encompassing various aspects of business and technology: Mr. Field is a board member and operations and technology officer, with a successful track record of fostering growth, streamlining operations, enhancing the customer experience, and reducing costs. His leadership has been instrumental in driving digital transformations and strategic change initiatives. He has co-led 35 mergers and acquisitions (M&As), modernized and consolidated technology infrastructure, and improved data assets. With a diverse perspective gained from working in corporate, consulting, and private equity-owned companies, Mr. Field has encountered a wide range of revenue scenarios, from startups to companies with revenue as high as $50 billion. In addition to his professional roles, Kevin Field is recognized as a thought leader. He serves as a mentor in the Columbia University Executive Master’s Program, offers his expertise on various company and industry boards, mentors startups, influences the direction of mentorship, and evaluates innovation and transformation strategies as a judge. He has also shared his successful approach to cultivating a high-performance culture at conferences for Chief Human Resources Officers (CHRO) and Chief Information Officers (CIO).

name: Mark Filer
title: Head of Infrastructure Engineering
bio: Mark grew up in London & holds a Bachelor’s degree in Software Engineering from the University of Manchester. 2023 Assumed the role of Global Head of Compute – Managing operations and engineering, responsible for all physical, virtual and cloud based infrastructure 2018 Joined UBS to run cloud engineering. Focused on building a regulatory compliant cloud and business partnerships, enabling innovation & cloud adoption. 2015 Moved to Bridgewater Associates managing Technology Strategy & Incubation, driving agile transformation and cloud enablement. 2008 Joined Time Warner to manage IT Operations & Engineering. Delivering a cross-divisional private cloud & running aggressive virtualization programs. 1997 Joined Goldman Sachs, moving to New York in 1999, where he served as Vice President and managed their nascent Identity & Access management practice.

name: John Finley
title: Chief Legal Officer
bio: John Finley is Chief Legal Officer and Chief Compliance Officer of AIS Healthcare. AIS is a national healthcare company based in Dallas, Texas. Mr. Finley provides leadership, oversight and direction in the areas of IT security, compliance, legal affairs, government affairs, affiliated organizations and risk management. For 20 years, Mr. Finley has held the Chief Legal, IT Security, Compliance and Audit roles academic at medical centers, integrated hospital systems, private equity backed pharma and Fortune 500 health care companies. He has also served in key legal and policy-making positions at the White House and the FDA. Mr. Finley has led complex programs across national and international jurisdictions. He brings significant experience and accomplishments in corporate compliance, IT security, enterprise-risk management, mergers and acquisitions, private equity, health care operations, clinical research, government investigations and material litigation. Mr. Finley serves as an Advisory Board Member for the University of Tulsa Henry Kendall College and a Board Member representing Foremankind.org. Mr. Finley holds a B.S. from the University of Tulsa and a law degree and M.S. from the University of Oklahoma and Oxford University. He resides in Dallas, Texas with his wife and three daughters.

name: Kathleen Fitzpatrick
title: Chief Information Officer, Managing Director
bio: Kathleen Fitzpatrick's most recent position is as a Member at Chief. In the past, she served as the CIO and Managing Director of Russell Reynolds Associates, where she was responsible for driving the firm's technology strategy and supporting major IS initiatives. Ms. Fitzpatrick leverages her significant experience in key areas such as innovative systems architecture, client management platforms, hardware and software integration, user interface design and end-user training programs to ensure that the IS team understands and translates business trends and needs into viable technology solutions to support Russell Reynolds 46 offices across the globe. Ms. Fitzpatrick has more than 20 years of experience in the development of complex client management applications and IT architecture, having spent the last 15 years in senior IT roles for major financial services institutions. Prior to Russell Reynolds Associates, she was the Managing Director and the Head of Global Development for Citigroup’s Global Wealth Management Group where she was responsible for the successful global rollout of the strategic client profiling and on-boarding platform at Citi Private Bank. Prior to that, she held roles of increasing responsibility at UBS, most recently serving as a Managing Director and Head of Front Office Technology for UBS Financial Services’ U.S. Wealth Management where she was responsible for all technology used by financial advisors and their clients. While at UBS, Ms. Fitzpatrick served as Chief Architect. She also played a significant role in several acquisition integrations that were instrumental in building the organization’s current structure. Earlier in her career, she served as Project Manager for Smith Barney Shearson and spent her early years as a software developer. Ms. Fitzpatrick holds a B.S. in Mathematics and Computer Science from the State University of New York at Binghamton.

name: Michael Foster
title: Chief Information Technology Officer - 2019 to 2021
bio: Michael Foster served as Sysco's Chief Information Technology Officer until 2021 and was responsible for all aspects of Sysco's global technology. He also partnered with other Sysco leaders in helping shape the company's broader strategic direction and future. Prior to joining Sysco, Mr. Foster was EVP and CIO for CN Railway, where he was responsible for information technology, operational technology, and information security. Before his role at CN Railway, he spent more than 20 years at Federal Express in various technology leadership and executive roles across multiple global subsidiaries.

name: Larry Fried
title: Senior Director of Information Technology
company: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
location: South Orange Village, NJ, United States
bio: Larry Fried is the Senior Director of IT at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai. He manages an organization responsible for designing and implementing technology and systems in support of the academic mission of the school, and for delivering a variety of technology services to the school’s constituents. Prior to his current position, Mr. Fried was the founding CIO at Stella and Charles Guttman Community College, the newest college in the City University of New York System. In this role, he was responsible for establishing the IT infrastructure, student systems and technology services in support of the launch of the new college, as well as designing and implementing technology for the various student and classroom spaces. Mr. Fried previously served as the Assistant Dean of Technology for the Columbia Journalism School. He managed an organization responsible for researching and implementing technology in support of the school’s transition to digital journalism, and for the delivery of systems and technology services to the school’s faculty, students and staff. Previously, Mr. Fried spent 22 years at Bell Laboratories, where he started as a technical staff member developing systems and emerging technologies in support of AT&T, government and corporate customers. He later became a manager of technology groups responsible for the development and delivery of systems and integrated solutions for telecommunications service providers. During his tenure at Bell Labs, Mr. Fried also did a five-year stint as an organizational consultant, managing a practice that helped organizations achieve compliance with and gain certification to the ISO-9000 series of standards. His group additionally provided services in quality systems assessment, software process assessment and re-engineering, and project management audits. He has also held technology positions at Morgan Guaranty Trust, the University of Medicine and Dentistry of New Jersey, and Loral Electronic Systems. Mr. Fried holds an M.S. in Computer Science from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute, an M.S. in Statistics from Rutgers University, and a B.A. in Mathematics from Queens College of the City University of New York. He is a certified Project Management Professional and holds an ITIL Foundations certification.

name: Tsvi Gal
title: Head of Enterprise Technology Services (Infrastructure)
bio: Tsvi Gal is Head of Enterprise Technology Services (Infrastructure) at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center. Prior to MSKCC, Mr. Gal was CTO of Morgan Stanley, where he was responsible for constructing an industry-leading, evolutionary architecture competitively positioned both on the buy side (asset management) and on the sell side (investment banking) for future growth through innovation, processing scale, and efficiency. Before his position at Morgan Stanley, Mr. Gal was CTO and Managing Director for Deutsche Bank Capital Markets. Previously, he was CIO at Time Warner Music Group (WMG). Under his leadership, WMG introduced mobile ring tones to the USA and was the first partner of Apple's iTunes/iPod drive. He also led the transformation of WMG's web assets into a full fledge eCommerce site with over 14 million unique users. From 1999 to 2002, Mr. Gal was the President and COO of ATT.com. In this position, he was responsible for AT&T's eBusiness and eCommerce activities. Mr. Gal also led the global sourcing and vendor management effort for all of AT&T – resulting in a net savings of $145M per annum. Starting his IT career in the Israeli army in 1976, Mr. Gal served in executive management roles as CIO & CTO at Merrill Lynch (heading global infrastructure), Wells Fargo (heading investment banking, global infrastructure, and the on-line bank), ABN AMRO Bank (CIO of North America), and Bank of America (CTO and Head of Infrastructure and Capital Markets). From 1996 to 2001 Mr. Gal served on the USA technology delegation to the G7 conference of leading economy nations. He is credited with developing the first Internet bank while at Wells Fargo Bank and initiating the first major brokerage on-line trading system at Merrill Lynch. As a result of these achievements and others, he was presented with the 2001 Einstein Award for achievements in Science and Technology by the State of Israel President, Moshe Katzav. Mr. Gal is a Computer Science graduate of Rutgers University and holds an M.B.A. from Golden Gate University. He is the author of Distributed Computing Management and is on the Board of Directors at Expand, Cardean University, MobileSec, Dati, and also serves as an Advisor to the President of Rutgers University.

name: Robert Galvin
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: Robert Galvin is CTO of the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, which builds, operates, and maintains the airports, rail, bridges, and tunnels between the two states, as well as seaport facilities, and the 16-acre World Trade Center site. Since joining the Port Authority in December 2013, Mr. Galvin has created a strategy for implementing technology at the Agency, developed a cybersecurity program, deployed or upgraded over 6,500 computers and mobile devices, and moved the organization to Microsoft Office 365. Prior to joining the Port Authority, Mr. Galvin was CTO of the NYC School Construction Authority, an organization responsible for the design, construction, and maintenance of over 1,500 public schools supporting over 1 million K-12 students. There, he reorganized the Technology Department and modernized their enterprise applications by deploying data warehousing, cloud, and mobile technologies. Before entering the public sector, Mr. Galvin provided corporate IT leadership in a number of industries, including manufacturing, healthcare, consumer products, consulting services, software design, engineering and commercial construction. As a senior management consultant for Penta Technologies, a leading supplier of ERP and field service management software for the construction industry, Mr. Galvin provided support and business process change guidance to AMEC, the world’s third largest engineering services company. Mr. Galvin served in a senior leadership role for the international curtain wall company, Enclos Corporation, renown for projects such as the Petronas Towers in Kuala Lumpur, the Getty Center in Los Angeles, and Bloomberg Tower in New York City. Previously, his work at 3M in the consumer products, international, and IT divisions earned him the company’s IT award for excellence. Mr. Galvin serves on the board of the New York Metro Society for Information Management (SIMNY). He holds a B.S. in Management Information Systems from Central Michigan University.

name: Virginia Gambale
title: Chair of the Board, Chair of Nominating & Governance and Audit Committee Member
bio: Virginia Gambale is a successful technology leader and investor. As the Chair of the Board, Chair of Nominating & Governance, and Audit Committee Member at Nutanix, she plays a key role in the company's governance and oversight. In addition, she serves as a Board Director and Audit Committee Member at Jamf, contributing to the strategic direction and financial oversight of the organization. Furthermore, she is a Board Director and Chair of the Risk Committee at Virtu Financial, where she focuses on risk management and governance. Virginia also holds the position of Board Director and Chair of Nominating & Governance, as well as her previous role as Former Audit Chair at First Derivatives. Additionally, she serves on the boards of Evertec, 10x Banking, and Consumer Edge, contributing her expertise to a diverse range of organizations. Ms. Gambale operates globally as the Managing Partner of Azimuth Partners LLC, as an advisor and consultant, focused on growth and innovation strategies for technology and technology driven services companies in both middle market and large corporations and advisor to startup companies. She is highly regarded for her innovative thinking, leadership, and ability to deliver results. Ms. Gambale has served on over 20 Public and Private boards including: JetBlue (NASDAQ-JBLU), Motive (NASDAQ-MOTV), Piper Jaffray (NYSE-PJC), Workbrain (TSE-WB), Synchronoss Technologies (NASDAQ-SNCR), ), and IQ Financial (Chairman). She is a member of the Audit Committee, and often serves as Compensation, Transaction or Technology Committee Chair. Ms. Gambale currently serves on the boards of: Virtu Financial, Nutanix, and First Derivatives. As a former senior operating executive of Global Corporations, Ms. Gambale held senior management positions (including CIO and Managing Director) at: Merrill Lynch, Bankers Trust, Deutsche Bank and Marsh & McLennan. Additionally, she was the Head of Deutsche Bank Strategic Ventures, and subsequently a General Partner at Deutsche Bank Capital until founding Azimuth Partners in 2003. She is also the Board President of the Newport Music Festival and adjunct faculty member at Columbia University and Mentor for Columbia University’s Masters in Technology Management program. Ms. Gambale is a frequent lecturer and keynote speaker on the strategic use of emerging technologies. She resides in CT with her two children and husband Dr. Austin W. Feeney.

name: Edward Ganom
title: Senior Manager, IT Risk & Compliance
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics
bio: Edward Ganom is currently the Senior Manager, IT Risk & Compliance at Osaic. He has a background as a Cybersecurity Consultant at CyberKnight, a technology security distributor specializing in helping enterprises and governments simplify and accelerate breach detection, prevention, and incident response, while addressing compliance requirements. Mr. Ganom has also held roles as a GM in international companies and as a CISO in the financial and public sectors in the U.S. and the Middle East. He has led the development of national cybersecurity policies, strategies, and cybercrime legislation. Mr. Ganom holds a B.S. in Electronic Engineering and Political Science from two American universities and is a Certified Information System Security Professional (CISSP). He is also a recognized speaker at technology, cybersecurity, and risk conferences.

name: Andrew Garlick
title: Owner
bio: Andrew Garlick is CEO and Principal of The Garlick Group, a consulting firm specializing in Cyber Security, Cyber Security Compliance, complex digital trans- formation, and project and program management. Prior to forming his company, Mr. Garlick was SVP, Business Systems, for WPP Group USA. He was responsible for the policy development and oversight of financial, HCM, and other business systems in North America for all the WPP Companies. He also served as the Group CIO for WPP’s Specialized Communications Group and WPP’s Digital Group. In addition, he was involved in leading the transformation on a number of global initiatives including benchmarking WPP’s IT Infrastructure and Costs worldwide, the global consolidation of WPP’s IT infrastructure, the consolidation and outsourcing of WPP’s HR, Payroll and Benefit Administration Systems. Before WPP, Mr. Garlick was CIO of Young & Rubicam, Inc. Mr. Garlick’s particular specialty is the end to end project management of complex business systems. He has experience in working with all the major system vendors, including Oracle, PeopleSoft, Microsoft Dynamics, Exact, Lawson, and NetSuite. Mr. Garlick has a reputation of building and deploying systems that address the needs of the user. He is also knowledgeable and adept in the major Business Intelligence tools. Mr. Garlick has been in the advertising and media industry for 25 years, working for Campbell-Ewald in Detroit in the 80’s, and joining Young & Rubicam in 1993 as IT Director for North America. He spent the first half of his career in finance, and was Treasurer of the University of Detroit and Controller of Campbell-Ewald Co, where he built and installed a number of financial systems. He made the switch from finance to technology in 1990. Mr. Garlick has broad experience in the not for profit sector. In addition to the 10 years he spent as a financial officer at the University of Detroit, he has served on the Boards of the CYO of the Archdiocese of Detroit and Michigan Performing Arts (Youtheatre), the Society for Information Management, and is Founder and Board President of the Family Support Organization of Bergen County. He has been active in a number of organizations as a volunteer, serving on the Committee to Save Orchestra Hall in Detroit, and as an Allocation Panel Chair of the United Way for Southeastern Michigan. Mr. Garlick was instrumental in helping United Way develop a strategic plan for the growth of technology in the nonprofit sector in 1996. For his work in the Detroit Metropolitan area, he was recognized as an “Executive Hero” by Crain’s Detroit Business. Mr. Garlick is a recognized expert in the area of non profit governance and non profit (fund) accounting, and has taught nonprofit accounting at the graduate level at the University of Michigan, Dearborn and has served as an adjunct professor at Columbia University. In addition to holding an M.B.A. from the University of Detroit, Mr. Garlick has a B.A. in Philosophy from Sacred Heart Seminary, and an M.A. in Classical Literature from Wayne State University. He is a certified Project Management Professional (PMP) and Human Resources Information Professional (HRIP). He also holds a Certificate in College Business Management from the University of Kentucky, Lexington, a Certificate in Fund Raising from the Center on Philanthropy at the University of Indiana, and a Certificate in Forgiveness Therapy Training from the Forgive for Good Center affiliated with Stanford University. Mr. Garlick resides with his family in New Bern, NC.

name: Michael Garrett
title: Director
company: Law School Association at Columbia University
bio: Michael Garrett is a seasoned legal and business advisor with diverse experience in global financial services, corporate, nonprofit, consulting and coaching. After earning his Bachelor’s, MBA and JD degrees at Columbia University, Michael received his basic training in corporate and international legal practice as an associate with prominent law firms in New York City. He then served for many years as a partner and associate general counsel of Coopers and Lybrand, with responsibility for human resource policies and claims, management and benefits consulting, enterprise and industry technology, intellectual property, professional practice problems and diverse regulatory matters. He then became general counsel of the global investments division of the Swiss Reinsurance Company. In the wake of the September 11th terrorist attacks, Michael created, managed and grew The Gift of New York, one of the most successful nonprofit initiatives to benefit family survivors of the attack victims. Returning to legal matters, Michael served as general counsel in residence of a 700-attorney national law firm. Currently, Michael is an independent attorney, executive coach, and business and nonprofit strategy advisor. Michael is also a very active Columbia alumnus, serving as executive mentor in the Business School, president of the Society of Columbia Graduates, a director of the Law School Association and of the Stephen Whitney Phoenix Society, and a member of the Advisory Committee for the Columbia Libraries.

name: Michelle Garvey
title: Senior Advisor
bio: Michelle Garvey is currently serving as a Senior Advisor at Alvarez & Marsal. Before this role, she was part of the Advisory Board of Total Retail Group and Vation Ventures. She was also a member of the AWS Retail Leaders Advisory Forum. Prior to these advisory roles, Ms. Garvey had a distinguished career in various leadership positions. She held the position of Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Deputy Chief Transformation Officer at J. Crew. Earlier in her career, she served as the Senior Vice President (SVP) and CIO of Ann Inc., a leading specialty retailer for fashionable women's clothing, with a presence in the United States, Puerto Rico, and Canada. Ms. Garvey's extensive experience also includes CIO roles at Warnaco, Brooks Brothers, Memberworks, and Sportmart. She has previously worked with notable retailers like Crate and Barrel, the Registry Stores, and Ames Department Stores. She initiated her career in consulting at Arthur Andersen, which provided her with valuable insights and expertise in the field. Ms. Garvey holds a Bachelor of Science (B.S.) degree in Civil Engineering and a Master of Business Administration (M.B.A.) degree in Finance, both earned from Cornell University. Her educational background and wealth of experience reflect her strong foundation and contributions to the business and technology sectors.

name: Ashifi Gogo
title: Founder & CEO
bio: Ashifi Gogo founded Sproxil in 2009 and is CEO of the company. Under his leadership, Sproxil developed its award-winning Sproxil Defender™ technology that has been used over 50 million times by consumers in 6 countries to verify the authenticity of their products and earn instant rewards for their loyalty. In 2015, Gogo was named to Fortune's 40 under 40 list. He was awarded the Social Entrepreneur of the Year award in 2014 by the Schwab Foundation. In 2013, Sproxil was named the world’s most innovative company in health care by Fast Company, and #7 most innovative worldwide, beating 99 of the Fortune 100 companies. Gogo served on the World Economic Forum’s Global Agenda Council on Social Innovation and on the Meta-Council on the Illicit Economy. He holds a US Patent, earned a Ph.D. from Dartmouth College and a B.A. from Whitman College. He is Dartmouth's first-ever Ph.D. Innovation Fellow and teaches a course in New Business Ventures Technology Management at Columbia University.

name: Clark Golestani
title: Managing Director
bio: Clark Golestani is Managing Director at C Sensei Group LLC. Formerly, the executive vice president and CIO at Merck, Golestani was responsible for the company's global information technology. Golestani was also a member of Merckís Executive Committee and reported to Merck chairman, president and chief executive officer Kenneth C. Frazier. Previous responsibilities included global head of IT for Merckís Research & Development division including Basic Research, Pre- Clinical, Clinical and Regulatory, spanning target identification through post marketed trials and pharmacovigilance; Vice President, Corporate IT supporting Finance, HR, Procurement, Legal, Public Affairs, Site Services, Real Estate, and Shared Business Services operations; leadership and development of the Companyís IT strategy, portfolio, and enterprise architecture as Chief Architect; leadership of the companyís global computing services, eBusiness, and information security as Exec. Dir., IS Global Computing Services; leadership of the company's technology architecture as Senior Director, IS Architecture; and various leadership and management roles within Merckís Research Labs as IT Director. Prior to joining Merck, Golestani was principal with Oracle Corporationís consulting practice and was responsible for establishing and managing strategic client relationships from business development through consulting services engagement delivery. Golestani serves as a director on the Board of Directors of Liaison Technologies, and serves on Sierra Ventures CIO Advisory Board. Previously, he served on the Stevens Institute of Technology Service Management and Engineering Masters Program Advisory Board, Intel's Enterprise Advisory Board, Carnegie Mellon Universityís CyLab / Sustainable Computing Consortium Board of Governors, PhRMA Foundation Informatics Advisory Committee and Juice Softwareís Advisory Board. Golestani has a degree in Management Science from Massachusetts Institute of Technology Sloan School of Management, and is a co-founder of Cross Road Technologies, Inc., in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

name: Michael Golz
title: Managing Director & CTO, SAP Advisory
bio: Michael Golz is the Managing Director & CTO, SAP Advisory at KPMG US. He was a former SVP, Head of Customer Advisory North America at SAP. As a member of the Senior Executive team, he was responsible for Industry, Value, Architecture, and Solution Advisory, enabling >$14 billion per year in cloud, software, services & support revenue with a focus on Digital Transformation, Cloud ERP, Generative AI & Machine Learning, and the complete SAP portfolio across all industries. He now advises clients in various industries in technology, strategy and transformation matters. In his previous role, he oversaw all IT solutions and services for SAP in North America, aligning information and technology strategy with SAP’s business strategy, and providing best-in-class processes and optimized operations for all lines of business. He led the “SAP Runs SAP” program, highlighting IT and product innovation in the areas of cloud, real-time business platform, applications, analytics, and mobile. Under his leadership, SAP Global IT became the company’s best customer reference. Michael also oversaw IT for Latin America and the Caribbean. Before assuming the CIO role, he was senior vice president of Global IT Application Services, with worldwide responsibility for internal business applications and external platforms, covering the entire spectrum of SAP solutions for SAP employees. Golz joined SAP AG in 1998 at the company’s Walldorf, Germany headquarters. He began his tenure as SAP’s head of infrastructure for Demo, Training and Consulting systems. After moving to the United States in 2002, he served as the vice president of IT for SAP America, overseeing the strategic role and management of the IT function in the United States. Prior to joining SAP, he led Information Management for the largest division of OTTO Group in Cologne, Germany. Golz holds a B.S. in Business Administration and Information Technology from European Business School in Germany.

name: Sara Gori
title: Entrepreneur in Residence / Reputation Risk Officer / Board Member
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Sara Gori is on the EIR team at R3i Ventures mentoring healthcare startups and is CEO of Goriatis Group currently establishing a premier reputation risk training and advisory firm. She has 30 years of extensive experience in helping companies develop and execute stakeholder relations strategies, build robust communications programs, investor relations benchmarking and manage reputation risk and crisis communication management national, regional and global. Ms Gori was formerly the Global Head of Reputation Risk Management at AXA where she held various roles notably spearheading the establishment of reputation risk management as a business function across a 50+ country footprint, and pioneering a comprehensive suite of services that extends beyond traditional communication, brand, and operational risk perspectives to cyber risk governance, digital asset risk transformation and reputation maturity assessments. Tools that have been developed and implemented include a centralized reputation risk framework, creation of a Reputation Risk Standard, the reputation risk maturity model assessments, and implementation of crisis communication management protocols. Ms Gori passion for mentoring startups and supporting career growth in women, undergraduates and MBA students have led her to work with accelerators, present as a guest lecturer, industry expert, and create academic masterclasses and career related workshops with universities, including HEC, ESSEC and Trium. Prior to AXA, Ms Gori worked in various accounting roles within the financial and entertainment industry along with a detour into telemarketing. Her educational background includes a BBA in Accounting from Bernard M. Baruch College along with certificates in Investor Relations. Ms Gori spent 10 years on the NIRI New York Chapter board spearheading Professional Developments events for the Investor Relations members, She is a constant learner acquiring professional development certifications in Negotiations at Harvard Law School and Artificial Intelligence at Section.

name: Karen Gould
title: Independent Director
focus: Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Karen Gould is a distinguished thought leader, mentor, and advisor known for her entrepreneurial spirit and growth mindset. With extensive experience as a corporate and nonprofit director, senior executive, and technology investor, she has established herself as a systems thinker and a catalyst for creativity and innovation. Her expertise lies in adaptive leadership, creating shareholder value through collaboration, driving culture change, and developing sustainable global business practices, particularly in the field of infrastructure, which includes technology, communications, and construction. Karen Gould currently holds several significant roles, including Director at Gil-Bar Industries, which is backed by Intermediate Capital Group, PLC. She is also a Board Observer representing Golden Seeds Venture Fund for Chromatic 3D Materials and an Independent director for REV Renewables, a leader in renewable power generation and energy storage facilities. Throughout her career, Karen has been an active board member, often assuming leadership positions, including board chairman. She has served on various board committees, including audit, nominating, governance, and compensation. Her notable board experience includes roles at Orbcomm Inc. (NASDAQ: ORBC) and Turner Construction Company & affiliates. Earlier in her career, she served as a board member for Puretracks Inc. in Canada and Bell Communications Research (Bellcore). Karen Gould currently serves as the CEO and founder of Gould Consulting Group. In this capacity, she provides strategic guidance to boards and the C-suite and offers coaching and mentoring to executives on matters related to strategy, ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) integration, and leadership and organizational development. Additionally, she holds the position of Managing Director at Golden Seeds, an early-stage investment firm that champions gender diversity and equitable access to capital for women. In this role, she has conducted due diligence and made investments in eight technology companies, including leading a $5 million Series B investment in Chromatic 3D Materials. In her prior position, Ms. Gould served as the Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Board Member at Turner Construction Company. During her tenure, she led strategic initiatives that transformed the company's culture, improved cash flow, tripled earnings, and scaled revenue from $8 billion to $16 billion. Her responsibilities included overseeing Finance, IT, cybersecurity, risk management, insurance, communications, and marketing, managing a staff of approximately 600. She was also instrumental in leading business continuity planning and execution during the challenges of 2020, contributing to the development of industry-leading response protocols for pandemic, civil unrest, systemic racism, and cybersecurity attacks. Furthermore, Karen spearheaded Turner's digital transformation, establishing business intelligence capabilities and enhancing operational efficiencies. Earlier in her career, Ms. Gould held senior positions at Bell Canada and AT&T Corporation, including roles as Financial Vice President and CFO at AT&T Long Distance Services. She also served as CFO and Vice President of Administration at SaaS pioneer CenterPost Communications and held positions as Vice President of Finance and CFO for Enterprise Business Services and Director of Treasury at Ameritech. Karen Gould holds an M.B.A. with Honors in Finance, Strategy, and International Business from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business. She earned her Bachelor of Science in Finance from Indiana University Kelley School of Business. Her commitment to community involvement is evident through her active participation on the Advisory Board and Real Estate Executive Committee for the Anti-Defamation League in New York and New Jersey. She also serves on the CFO Advisory Board for the University of Chicago Booth, where she occasionally acts as a guest lecturer.

name: Steve Greenblatt
title: Board Member
bio: Steve Greenblatt currently serves as a board member for Provable Market, a financial institution based in New York. With over 25 years of experience in financial technology, Steve has a diverse background, having worked for both startups and large firms. Prior to his role at Provable Market, Steve held the position of Director of Operations, Portfolio Management & Trading Systems at FactSet. FactSet is a company specializing in building technology solutions for the entire Portfolio Lifecycle. In this role, Steve played a pivotal part in leading the development of tools tailored for portfolio managers and institutional traders. Before joining FactSet, Steve was involved in Sales at OpenFin, a FinTech startup that played a significant role in advancing the development of web applications within the financial services sector. Notably, Steve was one of the founding employees at Liquidnet, a groundbreaking electronic brokerage firm that catered to the world's largest money managers. His contributions included designing a trading application that propelled Liquidnet to become the tenth largest broker in the United States and a major broker in over 40 other global markets. Throughout his tenure, he held various senior positions, including Sales, Business Development, and Corporate Strategy. Steve embarked on his career in the consulting sector, starting with PricewaterhouseCoopers, and later held roles at esteemed institutions such as J.P. Morgan, Vie Systems, and Armonie Software. Steve Greenblatt's educational background includes an M.B.A. from the NYU Stern School of Business and a B.S. in Finance from Lehigh University. His extensive experience and expertise in financial technology make him a valuable contributor to the industry.

name: Allan Hackney
title: Independent Board Member
bio: Allan has been a leader of technology-enabled businesses characterized by large-scale multi-product, multi-channel and multi-geography product and service delivery in regulated business environments. Within these environments, his specialty has been to step into IT and Operations roles that were created for the first time or required repairs to distressed client, business or public relationships. Currently, he is an Independent Board Member at City National Bank. He is also an independent director of Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) US Group Holdings, LLC, a $165B financial holding company, and RBC Bank, a $6B specialty bank serving Canadians in the US. He is also an executive coach for senior IT leaders at a large insurance company. Previously, Lt. Governor Wyman asked Allan to establish Connecticut’s Health Information Exchange (conniect.org). Prior to this turn in public service, Allan served as SVP & Chief Information Officer (CIO) at John Hancock Financial Services with oversight of the company's technical teams. He has also held CIO and operations roles at AIG Consumer Finance Group and Bank of America Commercial Finance. Allan started his career at GE, where he held several leadership positions in the USA and Japan for GE Capital's global consumer finance business. Allan’s personal passion is learning and development of talent, with a particular emphasis on women's challenges. Professionally, he’s created and led development programs spanning entry-level to senior levels, including specialized programs for military officers. In the community, he has a long track record of hands-on board leadership of buildOn and Common Impact, nonprofits focused on building skills and mentorship. As a Mentor in Columbia University’s Master of Science in Technology Management program, Allan’s students, all women, have a track record of success, including overseeing the technology for global clinical trials of a Covid vaccine, leading the risk transformation of a major bank and starting a successful education company. He is also a mentor in Ouellette and Associate’s TechXL leadership development program. He has been a lecturer at Sacred Heart Univ., Univ. of New Haven, SUNY Levin Institute and others. Allan graduated from Colgate University. He holds CISM and CRISC certifications and was named a Computerworld Premier 100 CIO. He and his wife Jane reside in Philadelphia, PA.

name: Ken Haertling
title: Senior Vice President
bio: Ken Haertling currently holds the role of Senior Vice President (SVP), Global Cybersecurity at Las Vegas Sands.
