With a diverse and accomplished background in cybersecurity and telecommunications, Ken's career began in the US Air Force as a Network Engineer, where they played a crucial role in establishing computer networks and leading network enhancement projects. Subsequently, Ken served as the Executive Officer at the Air Force Information Warfare Center, providing essential support to the Commander and overseeing organizational initiatives. Notably, they also led a Computer Emergency Response Team responsible for intrusion detection and incident response at various Air Force bases. Following their military service, Ken transitioned to Level 3 Communications, taking on various roles such as Senior Manager of Information Assurance, Senior Manager of Security Architecture & Engineering, Director of Security Operations, and Vice President of Network Operations. In these capacities, Ken was responsible for managing security specialists. Ken further advanced their career by joining TELUS as Vice President and Chief Security Officer, where they had comprehensive oversight of internal security, including physical security, network security, and information technology security. Additionally, they directed TELUS' telecommunications and IT services security operations center (SOC). In their most recent role, Ken Haertling served as the Senior Vice President of Global Cybersecurity at Las Vegas Sands Corp, where they had overarching responsibility for the company's cybersecurity efforts. Throughout their career, Ken Haertling consistently demonstrated exceptional expertise in cybersecurity, telecommunications, and network operations. Ken Haertling's educational journey is equally impressive. They earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Operations Research from the United States Air Force Academy between 1988 and 1992. Subsequently, from 1995 to 1997, they completed a Master of Science in Operations Analysis from the United States Air Force Institute of Technology. Their dedication to education continued as they pursued an MBA in Information Systems, starting at The University of Texas in San Antonio in 1998 and successfully completing it in 2000. Ken Haertling's professional qualifications also include holding a Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP) certification from (ISC)², which they obtained in March 2013.

name: Michael Haney
title: Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Michael Haney is Executive VP and CIO at Battelle Memorial Institute, the largest independent nonprofit applied science and technology organization in the world with revenues over $11B. As a veteran technology leader with over 30 years of experience in the aerospace, defense and government services industry, Mr. Haney has repeatedly driven great efficiencies, rapid growth and overall success for technology-driven corporations. As CIO and a member of the firm’s senior leadership team, Mr. Haney is responsible for all aspects of Battelle’s information technology, including information security, enterprise infrastructure, collaboration solutions, enterprise systems and service desk. Prior to Battelle, Haney was an executive VP and CIO at SOS International LLC (SOSi). In that role, Mr. Haney integrated several acquisitions into the $600M firm. With work ranging from the Middle east operations to immigration courts in the US, Mr. Haney was integral in several large scale programs for the firm. Prior to SOSi, Haney enjoyed a long and distinguished career with Booz Allen Hamilton. He most recently served as Senior Director Enterprise Systems Delivery at Booz Allen Hamilton. In that role, he was responsible for the development, maintenance and support of all enterprise systems such as financials, HR, collaboration, endpoint engineering etc. for the $6.5B global management and technology consulting firm. Haney holds a B.S. in Management Information Systems from James Madison University in Harrisonburg, VA and serves on the Audit Committee for the Pacific Northwest Nuclear Lab (PNNL)

name: Gary Hanley
title: Global Enterprise Account Director
bio: Gary’s is currently a Global Enterprise Account Director at Vistra, responsible for the organization’s largest client’s, ensuring they have the strategic support they need to plan and expand their operations globally. Gary is an accomplished leader in Global Expansion, Offshore Development and Global Business Services. Over the past 20 years, he has worked with hundreds of organizations to plan and expand their global operations and has extensive experience delivering solutions to manage risk, reduce costs and access global talent across financial services, technology, and business services sectors. Gary is a part-time lecturer at Northeastern University’s College of Professional Studies teaching the Ms. in Global Studies and International Relations. He has been recognized amongst the Top 100 Most Influential Irish Business Leaders by Irish publication Business & Finance, the Top 100 Irish and Irish American Business Leaders by Irish America magazine. A frequent public speaker, Gary often moderates C-suite panels on global expansion strategy and on best practices in global expansion. Prior to joining Vistra, Gary held various leadership roles in sales, marketing and operations at Invest Northern Ireland, EMC, Baltimore Technologies, GTE, and Sun Microsystems. Gary holds a Bachelor of Business Studies from the University of Limerick, Ireland as well as a Master of Business Studies from the National University of Ireland at Galway and resides outside Boston Massachusetts.

name: Jeremy Hartman
title: Board Member
bio: Jeremy has over 25 years of technology, political, and non-profit experience, specializing in building organizations that activate audiences around new technology, products, or causes. Most recently, he served as the Vice President of Marketing at GoDaddy, where he led the world's first initiative to quantify the impact of microbusinesses on the economies of the US and the UK. This groundbreaking initiative garnered over 4 billion media impressions and catalyzed significant change at the local government level. Jeremy currently advises several companies and organizations, including the AI Asia-Pacific Institute, the CalPoly Center for Innovation and Entrepreneurship, and The Arena, an organization dedicated to convening, training, and supporting the next generation of U.S. political candidates and campaign staff. An alumnus of Washington University in St. Louis and Northwestern University, Jeremy currently resides with his family in the Bay Area, California.

name: Melissa Hathaway
title: President
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Melissa Hathaway, President of Hathaway Global Strategies LLC, is a globally recognized expert in cybersecurity, cyberspace policy, and digital risk management. She consults with governments, international organizations, and Fortune 500 companies, providing insights on cybersecurity, enterprise risk management, and technology assessments. Melissa's expertise extends to advising institutions like the UN, NATO, the ITU, and the OAS. She also has affiliations with esteemed universities and think tanks worldwide. Melissa Hathaway's distinguished career includes work with two U.S. Presidents, where she played pivotal roles in shaping the nation's cybersecurity strategies. She spearheaded the Cyberspace Policy Review for President Barack Obama and led the Comprehensive National Cybersecurity Initiative for President George W. Bush. Her commendable leadership earned her the National Intelligence Reform Medal and the National Intelligence Meritorious Unit Citation. Notably, Melissa developed the Cyber Readiness Index (CRI), a methodology for assessing national cybersecurity preparedness, which has received international acclaim. She has held key roles in two Presidential administrations, helping to secure and defend the nation's critical infrastructures against evolving cyber threats. Adding to her impressive career, Melissa Hathaway has joined Operation Homefront's Board of Directors, where she offers her extensive experience to help military families. Additionally, she chairs the Commvault Cyber Resilience Council, guiding the council in addressing emerging security trends and threats, promoting cyber resilience, and contributing to product development and business strategy. With over three decades in cybersecurity, Melissa Hathaway is a luminary in the field, dedicated to advancing resilience in an era of growing cyber threats. Her deep expertise and strategic guidance significantly impact the cybersecurity landscape.

name: Toni Hawkins-Scribner
title: Academic Advisor to the Wing Commander & Director of Research
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Dr. Toni Hawkins-Scribner is an accomplished educator and retired Air Force veteran, currently serving as the Academic Advisor to the Wing Commander and Director of Research at Air University. Prior to this role, she held the position of Director, Learning Sciences and Faculty Education at USAF Squadron Officer School within The Air University. With a distinguished career, she has made significant contributions to various military leadership education and medical training programs. Her extensive background includes the management of 24 Air Force medical degree programs at the Community College of the Air Force, overseeing teaching and curricula teams in DoD USAF medical courses and Air Force Professional Military Education programs, and leading innovation teams at the Headquarters Air University. These endeavors involve pioneering new approaches to design, develop, and integrate learning strategies and emerging educational technologies into both student and faculty learning experiences. Dr. Hawkins-Scribner earned her doctoral degree from Peabody College at Vanderbilt University, with research interests encompassing learning technologies, immersive learning, mentoring/coaching, and virtual learning environments.

name: Bruce Hedin
title: President
bio: Dr. Bruce Hedin is currently the President and Consultant at Hedin B Consulting. He is a leading expert in the assessment of the effectiveness of advanced search and analytics technologies. As a consultant, he supports clients in the design and oversight of measurement protocols to validate the results of AI-enabled technologies. He also provides guidance to thought-leadership and standard-setting organizations, such as the IEEE, on the responsible adoption, testing, and use of AI. Dr. Hedin’s work is animated by the view that the trustworthy adoption and use of AI derives from sound evidence of the effectiveness of the technology, the competence of its operators, the accountability of those responsible for its adoption and operation, and the transparency of the process in which the technology is incorporated. As Principal Scientist at H5 until its sale in 2021, Dr. Hedin designed and oversaw the sampling, testing, and measurement protocols that propelled H5, one of the first developers of AI for use in the legal domain, to a reputation for quality and made it a leading voice in the responsible use of AI. Beyond H5, Dr. Hedin contributes to national and international initiatives designed to ground the ethical use of AI in evidence-based protocols and standards, including: · Studies conducted at the US National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Text Retrieval Conference; · A model ESI protocol, together with tutorial commentary, to be published by the IEEE in 2023; · The Law Chapter of Ethically Aligned Design (the flagship publication of IEEE’s Global Initiative on Ethics of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems). In addition, Dr. Hedin consults for companies, law firms, and government agencies seeking to implement AI practically, effectively, and responsibly. Dr. Hedin is currently president of Hedin B Consulting. He is a frequent contributor to publications and conference proceedings focused on the intersection of AI and the law, including: The Journal of Artificial Intelligence and Law, New York Law Journal, ILTACON, LegalAIIA (in association with ICAIL), and MER. Dr. Hedin also supports technology, information governance, and legal professionals with CLE-certified training. Dr. Hedin earned his Ph.D. from Stanford University and his B.A. from Cornell University.

name: John Heidelberger
title: Head of Technology Consulting
bio: John Heidelberger is Head of Technology Consulting at Willis Towers Watson. Prior to this role, John was the SVP & General Manager, InsPro at Majesco Heidelberger is an executive leader with extensive experience in driving business transformation through the application of technology-based solutions. He has more than 24 years of industry experience in business operations, technology, and management consulting. He is the former CIO at Guardian Life Insurance. Prior to joining Guardian, Heidelberger held positions at Prudential Financial as well as Business Edge Solutions and American Management systems. Heidelberger holds an M.S. in Computer Science from Monmouth University and a B.A. in Computer Information Systems from the University of Scranton.

name: John Helm
title: Principal
bio: John works as an analyst and optimizer, specializing in understanding IT capabilities in the context of creating business value. John is currently helping a large private retail financial services firm prepare for an IPO. Prior to this, John was a Distinguished Engineer and Technical Advisor to the CTO at MongoDB. Before this role, John was the Chief Digital Officer at SiteHands.com, in charge of all technology, i.e. development, operations, support, security, and data science. Prior to Sitehands John worked as the Head of Quality for the London Stock Exchange Group; was the engineering manager of the scrambling compiler group at Polyverse.com; was a co-founder and the CTO of OMNI Retail Group, a startup focused on interactive, goal directed product discovery. Prior to OMNI, John performed consulting via JHelm Associates, LLC. Previously John was the Vice President of Engineering and Chief Technology Officer of Imprev.com, CTO of Drugstore.com, and a founding partner of Umaxon, a company specializing in system and software architecture, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance. Before Umaxon, John had several senior management positions at Merrill Lynch, including Head of Architecture; COO of Global Messaging, Collaboration Services and Directory Services; and Chief Information Architect. While at Merrill Lynch, in 2003 one of John's Open Source projects garnered a CIO Magazine "Resourceful 100 Award"; later he was tapped to rebuild and update the company's global messaging and directory infrastructures following the September 11 terrorist attack. Prior to Merrill Lynch, John has held key technology management positions at Deutsche Bank and Morgan Stanley. Other experience includes a variety of positions in academia and in the nonprofit and public sectors. John was on the faculty of the Department of Applied Physics at Columbia University, a Fellow at the National Academy of Engineering, and the Director of Computer Services at the American Health Foundation, where he responsible for the management of epidemiological databases and bio-statistics software. John has served on the advisory boards for University of Washington Agile and Cloud Computing Certificate Programs; and, mentors Technology MBA students from Columbia University, Northwestern University, and Georgetown University. John holds a Ph.D. in Applied Physics, a master's in Nuclear Engineering, and a bachelor's degree in Applied Chemistry from Columbia University. Prior to becoming educated, John was commercial lobserman, a sous chef, and pipefitter. John's pastimes and interests include sea kayaking, hiking, photography, cooking, philosophy, and theater.

name: Karen Higgins-Carter
title: Executive Vice President, Chief Information and Digital Officer
bio: Karen Higgins-Carter currently holds the position of Executive Vice President and Chief Information and Digital Officer at Gilbane Building Company, one of the largest privately-held real estate development and construction firms. In this role, she is entrusted with optimizing the value of innovation, digital technologies, analytics, AI, and overseeing technology and cybersecurity. Furthermore, she serves as an Independent Director at OPDC | OAPI for Otsuka Pharmaceutical Companies (U.S.). Previously, Higgins-Carter was the EVP and CIO of Webster Financial Corporation (NYSE: WBS) and Webster Bank, where she joined in July 2018. Before her time at Webster, she served as the Managing Director and Head of the Office of the Chief Information and Operations Officer for the Americas at Mitsubishi UFJ (MUFG) Financial Group. In this capacity, she was responsible for the development and execution of the company's IT strategic plan, IT governance, information risk management, communications, employee development, and engagement. Prior to that, she held roles as the Technology General Manager at Bridgewater Associates and the Managing Director and Head of Consumer Risk Technology at JP Morgan Chase. Her career also included progressively increasing responsibilities at General Electric Corporation, including GE Capital and GE Commercial Finance. She commenced her professional journey at Accenture, where she collaborated with clients to launch emerging technologies in the telecommunications industry. Higgins-Carter holds a B.S. degree in Mechanical Engineering and a B.A. in Economics and Business, both obtained from Lafayette College in Easton, Pennsylvania.

name: Victoria Hildebrand
title: Chief Information Officer & Senior Vice President
company: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Victoria (Vicki) Hildebrand is the SVP and CIO of Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts. Previously, she was the VP and CIO of Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont where she was responsible for all Information Technology and Claims Processing, as well as implementing future technology and data management capabilities to better serve clients, consumers, and employees. Prior to joining Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont, Ms. Hildebrand served as the CIO for the United States Department of Transportation. She drove a digital transformation program that resulted in tens of millions of savings, a more resilient cyber security infrastructure, and the implementation of modern data management techniques across all the Department’s agencies. She was the recipient of the prestigious 2018 FedScoop Leadership award, which recognizes tech leaders who make a positive impact on local and national communities. Before working in Washington, Ms. Hildebrand worked for Hewlett-Packard Inc. and Hewlett-Packard Enterprise for more than 30 years. Her tenure covered leadership positions in hardware and software development, systems engineering, architecture, operations, global services, and most recently as VP for Customer and Partner Advocacy where she advised customers on how to implement Digital and Business Transformation. She led the Marketing IT development effort through the complex acquisitions of Compaq and EDS. Ms. Hildebrand holds a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Vermont and an M.S. in Technology Management from Columbia University where she continues to teach Digital Transformation. She has been active in STEM-related learning her entire career and supports women with an interest in technology. She is Vice Chair of the NASCO Board and on the Advisory Committee of the University of Vermont College of Engineering and Mathematical Sciences. As a U.S. Figure Skating gold medalist and former coach, she spends her free time as a national level figure skating judge.

name: Peter Hlavnicka
title: Founder
bio: Peter Hlavnicka is the founder and CEO of Phi Ventures Pte. Ltd., a cross-industry management consulting firm specializing in IP Strategy, Commercialization and Protection, M&A, and Transformation. He is also a Venture Partner of R3i Capital, a global deep-tech venture capital firm specializing in artificial intelligence and sustainable development. With over 30 years of experience in leadership and innovation, technology, and strategy consulting for MNCs such as Blackberry, Fitbit, Avaya, Sunpower, Lattice Semiconductor, Cypress Semiconductor, and Nortel Networks, Peter is also a serial entrepreneur and angel investor. He has provided advisory services to over 200 startups and MNCs across hi-tech, deep tech, Medtech, mobility, semiconductor, metaverse, AI, Senior Care, and other sectors in the Americas, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Middle East. Peter has successfully advised on core business-related IP strategy and market expansion and helped raise capital for the portfolio companies. Peter's expertise spans intellectual property strategy, commercialization, and protection – including Brand Protection, cybersecurity, Medtech, senior care, healthcare, supply chains, and artificial intelligence. Peter holds advisory roles in his portfolio companies BuzzAR, Stratificare, Kooky, SenzeHub, The Empathy Company, and others. Peter actively mentors and advises 50+ companies. Peter earned an Executive MBA from the Richard Ivey School of Business, University of Western Ontario, and a Master's in Technical Cybernetics and Artificial Intelligence from the Technical University of Kosice, Slovakia. Peter is also a published author of books on Enhanced Enterprise Risk Management and Protecting the Brand, Volume I: Counterfeiting and Grey Markets & Volume II: Busting the Bootlegs with Law Journal Press (LJP) and Business Expert Press (BEP). In recognition of his achievements, APAC Insider Singapore Business Awards named Peter's Phi Ventures as the Best in IP Protection & Compliance Specialists in 2024, as well as named Peter was selected as IAM Strategy 300: The World's Leading IP Strategists 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024.

name: Herb Hofmann
title: Vice President, Information Technology
bio: Herb Hofmann is VP of IT for Loews Corporation. In this role, he is responsible for leading all aspects of the company’s information and technology requirements and strategy. Hofmann joined Loews in 1988 as a programmer trainee. Over his three decades at Loews, he has held numerous positions including managing IT services for all business areas as well as leading technical teams and data center operations. Loews Corporation is one of the largest diversified companies in the United States, with businesses in the insurance, energy, hospitality and packaging industries. Its consolidated subsidiaries are: CNA Financial (NYSE: CNA), Boardwalk Pipelines, Loews Hotels and Altium Packaging. Loews is focused on long-term value creation for its shareholders, and conservative financial management. Loews’s multi-industry holding company structure is a key factor in its ability to create value, providing it with the freedom to make investments across a broad spectrum of industries, wherever it perceives the opportunity to enhance returns to its shareholders. Loews is traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol L. Hofmann is holds a B.B.A. in Management Information Systems from Pace University Lubin School of Business.

name: Tami Hudson
title: EVP & Cybersecurity Client Officer
bio: Tami Hudson is EVP & Cybersecurity Client Officer at Wells Fargo. Prior to Wells Fargo, Ms. Hudson was a recognized cybersecurity leader and CISO for Randstad, the largest global HR service provider. There, she was responsible for developing and leading the implementation of the risk-based cybersecurity strategy. Additionally, as a member of the Global Incident Response Team and Global Security Strategic Team, she was responsible for driving key response and recovery decisions, and establishing strategic and tactical cybersecurity priorities across the company’s 39 operating countries, and $24 billion recruiting and staffing services. Integrating the pillars of people, process and technology, Ms. Hudson set the cyber agenda and aligned with internal and external stakeholders to design risk-based solutions that protect assets, align with the business, and enable growth. With a career surpassing 20 years and a depth of experience across each of the cybersecurity domains, Ms. Hudson has designed and led the successful implementation of corporate wide cyber solutions within the areas of Identity & Access Management, Threat & Vulnerability, Incident & Breach Response, Risk & Compliance, and Security Architect & Design. Prior to joining Randstad, she had a breadth of experience in Big 4 Consulting (Deloitte & EY) where she held multiple leadership positions including Chief of Staff, and was recognized for developing solutions that aligned IT, cyber risk strategy, and investments with the strategic business priorities in order to strengthen the security posture and overall organization. A purpose driven leader, Ms. Hudson has received numerous awards and recognitions, and has provided guidance on global security breaches, the post breach process, and building inaugural risk-based security programs. In her spare time, she volunteers in underprivileged communities through reading development, and computer skill programs. She is incredibly passionate about mentoring women in technology, and dedicating time to motivate girls (including her own tech savvy daughters) on the importance of STEAM programs. Ms. Hudson holds a B.S. from Georgia Tech and an M.B.A. from Duke University.

name: Mark Hutchison
title: Asset Management
bio: Mark Hutchison is CIO at Scotiabank. Scotiabank is a leading bank in Canada and a leading financial services provider in the Americas. Scotiabank helps its customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets. Prior to joining Scotiabank, Hutchison was CIO for M&G Investment Management, where he was responsible for the technology that drove M&G’s Investment Strategies which included Fixed Income, Equities, Infrastructure and Alternatives. He was also responsible for M&G’s Data Platform and Client Reporting Solutions. Previously Hutchison was Managing Director and Head of Infrastructure Transformation at Deutsche Bank where he was responsible for delivering the global data centre strategy as well as the Private and Public cloud solutions. Also at Deutsche Hutchison Served as the Head of Technology for the Asset and Wealth Management Division, and the Head of Technology for the Commodities Business. Before joining Deutsche Hutchison held positions including Head of Commodities Technology and Head of Emerging Markets Technology at Credit Suisse. Hutchison has worked around the globe in these roles, living in Australia, Japan, Switzerland and the US. He is now based in London, United Kingdom. Hutchison holds a BEng in Aeronautical Engineering from Loughborough University and a Masters in Technology Management from Columbia.

name: Yvonne Hyland
title: Senior Business Advisor, former Gartner Leader and VC Operator
bio: With 35 years at the forefront of the global technology industry, Yvonne Hyland’s experience spans from startups to Fortune 100 companies. Currently she is an advisor, investor, board member and speaker. Her exploration and advice are focused on driving business value by understanding the opportunities and risks of technology, digital innovation, and AI. Her corporate career roles include business leader at SAP where she led the Products and Services organization in Denmark. For Gartner, she launched and led the Executive Advisory Service for enterprise IT leaders (EITL) and high-tech and telco general managers (GGM). As founder, co-founder and CXO of multiple technology product and service companies, she achieved two successful exits including IBM as a strategic acquirer. In her role as a business transformation consultant working with Fortune 500 companies, she led global initiatives with her clients driving significant operational efficiency and growth. At B Capital, as a venture capital operator, she launched and led the business development group to increase portfolio enterprise value. Yvonne attended the MBA program at the Martin J. Whitman School of Management. She is currently an entrepreneur in residence at Syracuse University’s Blackstone LaunchPad and member of Syracuse University Libraries Advisory Board. She obtained her Ethics of AI certification from the London School of Economics. She is a member of the of the Private Directors Association (PDA) and the Society for Information Management (SIM) in the US. She is also a member of the European Women on Boards (EWOB EU) and the Institute of Directors (IOD) in the UK. Yvonne resides with her family in Greenwich, CT having previously lived in the United Kingdom, Scandinavia, and Bermuda.

name: Michael Iacona
title: Senior Director - Software & Technology - Private Equity Services
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Michael Iacona is a Senior Director with Alvarez & Marsal’s Private Equity Services in New York. His primary areas of concentration are in software product development, SaaS, and digital technologies. Iacona specializes in software, product, and technology assessments across large, complex global environments, as well as in SMBs and startups. With more than 20 years of experience, Iacona has led companies in turnarounds, M&A, and revenue growth, and managing profitable P&Ls. He most recently served as Chief Strategy & Growth Officer at Fusemachines Inc., an A.I. solutions and talent provider that specializes in machine learning, deep learning, computer vision, and natural language processing. He was also the President of the Institute for Integrative Nutrition, where he was responsible for turning around the company's balance sheet, driving revenue, and rolling out a unified mobile and web learning management platform for 100,000+ students and graduates in 150+ countries worldwide. Prior to joining A&M, Iacona held various executive technology roles, including VP of Technology at Thomson Reuters, where he created and managed the company's first global technology due diligence framework. He has also been involved in more than 12 deals totaling over $1.5 billion in transactions. Iacona’s efforts in these projects were instrumental in assessing the technology risks as well as the product rationalization benefits for each deal. Before Thomson Reuters, Iacona spent 11 years at Monster/TMP Worldwide, where he held several positions including VP of Software Engineering and VP of Strategic Systems. He led the carve-out of TMP Worldwide from Monster Worldwide to create a standalone entity, where he then became the CIO and SVP of Product. Under Iacona’s leadership, TMP transformed its business model from a traditional agency to a software solutions provider with new SaaS product offerings, which resulted in recurring revenue streams with significant contributions to EBITDA. Iacona holds a B.S. in Computer Science from Hofstra University in New York, an M.S. in Information Systems from Pace University in New York, and an M.B.A. from Columbia Business School in New York.

name: Angelo Incorvaia
title: US Core Banking IT Architect
bio: Angelo Incorvaia serves as U.S. Chief Architect for Core Banking at TD Bank where he has responsibility for the Bank’s IT Strategy and for the firm’s Core Banking roadmap. Prior to his current role, Incorvaia held the position of Managing Director of the Computer Management Group, Inc. As Managing Director, he was responsible for building and developing the Financial Service practice of CMG. Previously, Incorvaia was SVP and Head of IT for Bank Leumi USA. With responsibility for IT services across all Bank Leumi, he supported the Bank Leumi client base as well as IT infrastructure and back-office operations. Incorvaia has a long history of work in financial institutions. At Liberty Mutual Group, he served as Enterprise Technology Officer for Global Technology Architecture and Design. At Citigroup, he oversaw technology development of new retail banking products as Senior Director of Core Banking Applications. Also while at Citigroup, he managed the technology team for Item Processing, IT Testing, and IT Quality Assurance. During his 11 years with Citigroup, he led the system integrations for all Citibank North America mergers and acquisitions. Prior to Citigroup, Incorvaia held various technology positions at; Barclay’s Bank PLC, Alltel Information Services, Manufactures Hanover Trust, Sterling National Bank, and Community National Bank. Incorvaia is a Board member of the Society of Information Management. He sits on the Fordham University CIO forum and several industry Advisory Boards. Angelo Incorvaia attended Polytechnic Institute and completed his undergraduate work in computer science at the College of Staten Island. He holds an MBA from the State University of New York. He also holds a PMI Certification in Project Management and is certified as a Six Sigma Black Belt.

name: Punit Jain
title: Independent Director
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Punit Jain serves as the Co-Founder and Director of Helper4U.in, a startup that empowers uneducated, unskilled, and unorganized individuals to find better job opportunities. In addition to his role at Helper4U, he also serves as an Independent Director of ABM Knowledgeware Ltd, a publicly listed software company specializing in e-governance solutions. Furthermore, Jain is an active member of NASSCOM's Product Council, collaborating with other IT industry leaders to develop and strategize for the growth of the Indian software product ecosystem. Prior to his involvement with Helper4U, he held the position of CEO at Nelito Systems, a Mumbai-based software products company with a focus on banking and finance, employing 1200 people. Punit Jain holds a B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering from IIT Bombay and an M.B.A. from IIM Bangalore. Beyond his professional endeavors, he is an enthusiastic tennis player, trekker, and has completed numerous half marathons.

name: Arvind Jangi
title: Co-Founder DigiCoffer, Inc. and Biz Exponential, Inc
bio: Arvind Jangi is Co-Founder of DigiCoffer, Inc. and Biz Exponential, Inc. Formerly, Mr. Jangi was EVP, Processing Operations ; Technology at Mastercard. His top responsibility was to understand vision and strategy of the business units and identify and deploy game-changing technology-driven execution plan to provide the best in class innovative business solutions in time and budget. Mr. Jangi brings more than wealth of experience in the financial services industry with expertise in Retail Banking, Treasury, Mortgage, Consumer Finance and Credit Card businesses. He has held leadership positions in information technology and business. During the last 18 years he has partnered with CEOs/Presidents as CIO and has been part of their senior leadership team, to lead, grow and transform organizations to achieve additional success. Prior to joining MasterCard, Mr. Jangi also founded, built and managed a Management Consulting and IT Solutions business with operations in US and India. He consulted with and delivered IT solutions for Fortune 500 companies and small and mid-sized businesses/enterprises in the financial services and retail industry. Mr. Jangi holds a B.A. from the University of Delhi, India in Mathematics and Economics.

name: David Johnson
title: Senior Partner
bio: David Johnson is an accomplished professional with a wealth of experience in various leadership and strategic roles. He most recently served as the Senior Partner at HOLDING TLG, a role he has held since February 2021. Concurrently, he assumed the position of Chairman at Intsights, where he contributed his expertise from November 2019 to September 2021. Johnson is also an advisor for CyGov and Director of Mphasis, an IT Service company based in Mumbai, India. Previously, Johnson was the Director at Cylance Inc., where he played a pivotal role before concluding his tenure in December 2019. Prior to Cylance, he was the Senior Managing Director in the Private Equity Group at Blackstone. Earlier in his career, he took on the role of SVP of Strategy at Dell Corporation, overseeing corporate strategy, corporate development, and acquisition integration. His earlier career encompassed several positions at International Business Machines (IBM), including Vice President of Corporate Development, where he managed the company's acquisitions, divestitures, minority investments, and acquisition integration efforts. Johnson's academic background includes a B.A. in English and an M.B.A. from Boston College.

name: Brian Johnson
title: VP, R&D Technology
bio: Brian H. Johnson, Ph.D., currently serves as VP, R&D Technology at Takeda since July 2022. In this pivotal role, he plays a central part in ensuring the delivery of critical medicines to patients by partnering with R&D leadership to craft impactful information technology strategies, delivering essential application platforms and capabilities to the function, and actively contributing to the growth and development of exceptional leaders in the R&D technology discipline. Furthermore, Dr. Johnson holds the position of CEO and President at Olokun Consulting LLC, a firm he established in August 2019. This endeavor is dedicated to addressing healthcare disparities on a global scale, aiming to alleviate the suffering of disenfranchised populations and cultivate the next generation of healthcare leaders to confront these challenges head-on. Before these roles, from October 2020 to July 2022, he served as LEAD-R&D IT at Spark Therapeutics, Inc., a pioneering company committed to challenging the inevitability of genetic diseases. In this capacity, he worked in close partnership with Spark's R&D team to deliver IT solutions that would enable the company to achieve remarkable results in the field. Brian H. Johnson, Ph.D., has a profound background with Johnson & Johnson. Before his recent positions, he held the title of Senior Director of Surgical Innovation at Johnson & Johnson Medical. In this role, he was focused on exploring innovative ways to enhance medical procedures, harness data insights, engage patients, and integrate previously separate data sets to revolutionize the field of medicine. Prior to his innovative role in Surgical Innovation, Dr. Johnson played a crucial part in Johnson & Johnson's device sector. He served as the Senior Director responsible for overseeing the R&D, Clinical, and Regulatory IT divisions, and his leadership significantly contributed to product development, resulting in $11 billion in sales across seven distinct franchises. His career at Johnson & Johnson spanned over 13 years, where he successfully managed R&D organizations across various sectors, including pharmaceuticals, consumer products, and devices. His contributions extended to serving as a board member for Johnson & Johnson's Baby and Therakos franchises and playing a key role in the leadership team of TruMatch, an incubation program focusing on creating customized knee and joint replacement products. Before joining Johnson & Johnson, Dr. Johnson built his career through consulting engagements in insurance and financial services, small biotech companies, and major pharmaceutical corporations, where he specialized in strategy development, process optimization, and technology integration. Dr. Johnson's academic journey included earning a Ph.D. from Princeton University, specializing in chemistry and molecular biology. His research focus was on the fundamental aspects of protein folding, including both wet lab experiments and computational modeling. Additionally, he co-founded GTG, a 501(c)(3) foundation aimed at promoting leadership development and networking opportunities for minorities within the healthcare industry.

name: Anthony Johnson
title: Founder & Managing Partner
bio: Anthony Johnson is the Managing Partner at Delve Risk. Prior to Delve, Johnson was the Managing Director and Business Information Security Officer for the Corporate & Investment Bank (CIB) at J.P. Morgan Chase & Company. In this role, he was responsible for all aspects of cyber security including emerging threats/risk mitigation and the protection of the firm’s data and system assets. Johnson started his career in the U.S. Air Force where he focused on computer and network security. He has consulted with many of the Fortune 100 financial and retail organizations where he provided strategy and technical guidance in all aspects of cyber security. Johnson has held various senior cyber security leadership roles in large U.S. based retail and Global Finance organizations and participated as a voting Board Member for ANSI X9. Before J.P. Morgan Chase, he served as the Global CISO for Fannie Mae based in Washington D.C. Johnson regularly speaks at industry security conferences and is a firm advocate of protecting the collective ecosystems of enterprises as business dependencies are often deeply integrated. His passions include working with newly formed/startup technology companies to help understand their value proposition to the marketplace and he is a true champion for driving innovation and bleeding edge solutions to meet business needs. Johnson holds a B.S. in Computer Information Systems from Regis University and an M.B.A. from Indiana University.

name: Evon Jones
title: Managing Partner
bio: Evon Jones is Managing Partner at The ELLoyd Group. Jones is an experienced IT and business executive with over 20 plus years as a CIO in multiple industries including retail, life sciences, consumer goods, travel and aviation, and financial services. Prior to the ELLoyd Group, he was Chief Technology Officer at Aventiv Technologies, and SVP and CIO at Hallmark Cards Inc. where he was responsible for developing and implementing IT strategies to enable business goals and drive company performance. In addition, he provided vision and leadership to develop technology solutions that advanced retail, operational and business strategies as well as deliver cost efficiencies and a roadmap for ERP and IT infrastructure improvements. Jones was also a corporate officer of Hallmark Cards Inc. As former CIO of Fifth & Pacific companies, formerly Liz Claiborne, he oversaw the global IT organization for all portfolio brands including Juicy Couture, Kate Spade, Liz Claiborne and others. Jones also served as CIO for Bausch + Lomb, The Dial Corporation, America West Holdings (airline and Leisure company), and held leadership roles at American Express and Lehman Brothers. Jones received his B.A. from Baruch College in New York City and served on the boards of Henkel North America, Central & Northern Arizona Better Business Bureau, and was a member of the Grocery Manufacturers Association (GMA) IT Committee. Jones’s former community involvement included serving on the board of Aid to the Adoption of Special Kids in Phoenix, a member of the Valley of the Sun United Way Funds Allocation Committee, and was a member of the State of Arizona Attorney General’s Model Court Stewardship and Stakeholders Advisory Group on Behavioral Health and Substance Abuse.

name: Daniel Zelem, Jr.
title: Senior Advisor
bio: Daniel Zelem is a Senior Advisor at OMERS, where he provides technology advisory services to OMERS and its portfolio of companies. His responsibilities encompass a wide range of topics, including due diligence, technology and risk assessments, vendor assessments and referrals, value creation, and mentoring and coaching of portfolio companies' CxO's. Zelem is also a Senior Advisor at Turning Rock Partners, where he supports deal teams by performing technology due diligence on proposed investments. Before joining OMERS and Turning Rock Partners, Zelem served in key technology leadership roles at various organizations. He was the CIO of Mercy College, and prior to that, the CTO of Johnson & Johnson (J&J). Preceding his role at J&J, Zelem served as SVP and Chief of Business Technology at Medco Health Solutions, Inc., where he was responsible for IT strategy and the ongoing operations of the company's extensive technology assets. During his nearly 20-year career with Medco, Zelem played a critical role in implementing transformative strategies, which provided a competitive advantage to Medco and its clients. These strategies included the development of an industry-leading enterprise information warehouse platform, the launch of medco.com – the world's largest online pharmacy, and the creation of the Enterprise Image Service platform that enabled Medco's nationwide network of pharmacies to electronically process millions of mail-order prescriptions annually. Zelem also contributed to Medco's enterprise IT Strategy and the modernization of IT assets. In addition to his role at OMERS and Turning Rock Partners, Zelem is a Senior Advisor at McKinsey and Company, where he offers his expertise in technology advisory services.

name: Chad Kapadia
title: EVP, Chief Technology and Product Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Chad Kapadia is Chief Technology and Product Officer at Interior Logic Group (ILG), a Blackstone Company. In his role, Mr. Kapadia heads Product and Engineering. Mr. Kapadia brings over 25 years of technology experience to ILG in his role as CTPO where he is leading the company’s digital transformation. Prior to ILG, Mr. Kapadia was EVP and CTO at Regis Corporation. During his time, he conceived, built and launched Regis’s Opensalon Demand Generation Platform. He also led the building of Regis’ Store Management Platform, Opensalon PRO, which powers Regis Brand Salons. Mr. Kapadia also migrated all of Regis’s legacy applications to the public and private cloud. Before Regis, Mr. Kapadia served as Head of Engineering at Target Corporation's New Ventures and Accelerators. Before Target Corporation, he served in technology positions of increasing responsibility including CTO and Product Head at Swissclear Global, Inc. and as an Engineering Leader and founding member of Netflix, Inc.’s streaming team. Mr. Kapadia holds a B.S. from California State University, East Bay.

name: Parag Karnik
title: Chief Technology Officer - Enterprise Platforms
bio: Parag Karnik is the Chief Technology Officer of Enterprise Platforms at Nutanix, where he applies over three decades of global engineering and operational leadership to shape strategy and align enterprise platform capabilities with the needs of major IT organizations. His expertise spans technology products, platforms, and enterprise-scale operations across highly regulated sectors such as Healthcare, Life Sciences, and Financial Services. Before joining Nutanix, Mr. Karnik served as Managing Director at Credit Suisse, where he led infrastructure services, platform engineering, and architecture within a centralized global shared service model. He drove a multi-year digital transformation initiative focused on accelerating business value and modernizing the firm’s risk control framework. Prior to that, he was Vice President at Johnson & Johnson, overseeing global hosting and data platform services across all business lines. In that role, he designed and implemented an enterprise-wide hybrid multi-cloud and data strategy, delivering a hybrid AI/ML platform, multi-region S/4 HANA deployment, and Software Defined Data Center and edge modernization initiatives for R&D labs, manufacturing, and warehousing operations. Earlier in his career, Mr. Karnik spent over 21 years at Express Scripts/Medco, rising from a developer to a senior technology leader managing large global teams. He played a pivotal role in driving digital transformation programs, including the $26 billion merger between Medco and Express Scripts Specialty Pharmacy business. Throughout his career, Mr. Karnik has built a reputation for transforming infrastructure engineering and operations functions into agile, innovation-driven organizations, improving developer and data scientist experiences while maintaining a strong focus on business outcomes. His leadership approach blends enterprise-level strategy with a startup mindset. In addition to his role at Nutanix, he advises EsperHealth, a healthcare AI startup, and continues to serve as a trusted advisor to C-suite executives, engineers, and architects - aligning strategy, execution, and innovation to deliver measurable business value.

name: Frederick Kauber
title: Chief Innovation Officer
bio: Fred Kauber is the Chief Innovation Officer of Travel Nurse Across America, where he spearheads the digital transformation and growth of the leading provider of traveling healthcare professionals and contingent staffing solutions for hospitals across the United States. In this high-impact role, he oversees all technology and marketing initiatives for this rapidly expanding private equity-backed company, which generates hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue and employs several hundred professionals. His responsibilities include leading The Gypsy Nurse, the world's largest healthcare traveler community platform with over 500,000 members, and organizing TravCon, the premier conference for travel nurses and allied healthcare professionals. Before this role, Fred was the Chief Product & Technology Officer of BFS Capital, where he played a key role in the digital transformation of this prominent alternative lender catering to small and medium-sized businesses. Fred brings to the table more than two decades of executive leadership experience, having served in various capacities such as CTO, CIO, CPO, and CMO. He has managed technology, product management, marketing, and operations functions at both Fortune 500 companies and entrepreneurial ventures in the financial services, marketing, and technology sectors. His extensive career spans firms like Bigfoot Interactive, First Data Corporation, Dun & Bradstreet, and IBM Corporation. Notably, he was the CTO and CMO at CAIS Group, a fintech marketplace platform, and the CTO at TRANZACT, where he launched autoinsurance.com and directhealth.com in collaboration with Walmart. In addition to his corporate roles, Fred shares his expertise as an adjunct professor, teaching enterprise technology strategy in the graduate program of the NYU School of Professional Studies. Fred holds a B.S. in Computer Science from Manhattan College and an M.B.A. from New York University's Stern School of Business. He also earned an M.S. in Telecommunications from Pace University. His professional qualifications include being a Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) and receiving Six Sigma Black Belt training. Furthermore, he is a distinguished member of the Forbes Technology Council.

name: Jason Keith
title: Senior Product Leader
bio: Jason Keith is a seasoned product executive with nearly 15 years of experience leading transformative digital, AI, and data product teams across global enterprises and high-growth startups. Renowned for his expertise in SaaS platform strategy, AI-powered automation, and enterprise data solutions, he has built a distinguished career scaling product organizations, launching market-defining innovations, and delivering measurable business value. Currently, Jason serves as a senior product leader at Kobie Marketing, where he is spearheading the company’s broader AI strategy and developing real-time, event-driven platforms that power next-generation loyalty program operations. His initiatives have driven automation, enhanced operational efficiency, and generated multi-million-dollar revenue impact for enterprise clients. Before joining Kobie Marketing, Jason held executive product leadership roles at EPFR, S&P Global, and IHS Markit, where he managed P&Ls ranging from $25 million to $180 million and led large cross-functional teams spanning product, engineering, data science, and marketing. Over the course of his career, he has successfully launched more than a dozen products—including financial data platforms, predictive modeling tools, and customer engagement dashboards - that have significantly advanced organizational growth, retention, and market differentiation. Jason is widely recognized for his ability to build and scale high-performing teams grounded in customer-centricity, data-driven decision-making, and Agile execution. He has led major strategic transformations such as platform cloud migrations, AI agent deployments, and workflow automation initiatives, consistently aligning executive vision with product execution to achieve measurable outcomes. A passionate and empathetic leader, Jason is committed to fostering team development, improving employee engagement, and creating structured career pathways for product talent. His collaborative leadership approach and strategic acumen have established him as a trusted advisor to C-suite executives and a mentor to emerging product leaders. He holds a Master of Business Administration from the University of Massachusetts Isenberg School of Management and a Bachelor of Science from Northeastern University.

name: David Kelly
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: David Kelly currently serves as the Chief Technology Officer at TUDOR INVESTMENT CORPORATION. Prior to this role, he held the position of CTO at CMG. Before his tenure at CMG, David was a Partner, CIO, and Co-Chief Operating Officer at Pine River Capital Management, a global alternative investment firm specializing in relative value trading across various strategies, markets, regions, and asset classes. In this capacity, he played a leadership role in overseeing the information technology and operations functions, managing systems, tools, and processes governing the entire trade lifecycle, from the front office to the back office. Preceding his time at Pine River, Kelly served as the CTO of the Enterprise Division at Thomson Reuters from 2010 to 2012. He also held a significant position at SAC Capital from 2001 to 2010, where he eventually advanced to the role of Managing Director and CTO. His career trajectory includes a period from 1996 to 2001 when he worked for Barclays Capital in various capacities, including Head of Technology for the Americas. Additionally, from 1994 to 1996, David contributed his expertise at JP Morgan Chase as an Associate of Global Technology of Operations. Earlier in his career, from 1992 to 1994, he gained experience as a Programmer/Analyst at Unilever. David Kelly's educational background includes holding an M.S. in Information Systems from Pace University and a B.S. in Business Administration and Management Information Systems from Boston University. His extensive career and educational qualifications underscore his significant impact in the field of technology and finance.

name: Clarice Kennedy
title: Vice President, Revenue
bio: Clarice Kennedy is Vice President of Revenue at RGP, a publicly traded global consulting firm (NASDAQ: RECN) providing strategy and execution for Fortune 500 companies across many industries. With more than 20 years of experience in the technology industry, Kennedy brings a wealth of experience to her current role, driving the strategic development of RGP’s Information Management practice, specifically in the areas of IT governance, business process transformation and program/project management. Kennedy began her career as a systems programmer and supported European traders at Security Pacific Bank, now Bank of America. Recognizing the emerging demand for technology services as North American companies began using computers enterprise-wide, the entrepreneurial 24-year-old founded CCN, Inc., a technology consulting firm. As CCN’s CEO for over two decades, she guided the company’s growth and expansion into a full-service provider of technology services and talent management solutions for some of the world’s leading companies. Kennedy serves on the Board of Directors for Dress for Success, a global non-profit organization that empowers women transitioning back into the workforce. She has been a mentor for Columbia University’s Executive Technology Management program since 2009, co-organizer of Women in Blockchain NYC meetup and serves as an advisor for the Society of Information Managers(SIM) NY Chapter. Kennedy holds a B.S. in Information Systems Management from Buffalo State College in Buffalo, New York. She resides in New York with her husband and her three greatest accomplishments – her children, Dylan, Connor and Aidan.

name: Caroline Kenyon
title: Director
bio: Caroline Kenyon was Founder/Director of The Food Awards Company, which creates, owns and delivers world-class food awards. Its flagship project is Pink Lady® Food Photographer of the Year, the world’s leading awards for food photography and film, showing how food touches every aspect of our lives across the globe. Partners include the UN World Food Programme and the Royal Photographic Society. Other awards programs include Tiptree World Bread Awards UK and Tiptree World Bread Awards USA. She also co-founded FoodFM, a podcast platform telling personal and political food stories from around the world. Caroline has an MA from Cambridge University in Modern & Mediaeval Languages and Law, and was subsequently called to the Bar. This was followed by a decade as a journalist and magazine editor, before founding her first business, a public relations agency. She is a Board Member at Lincoln International Business School, a Trustee of the Historic Lincoln Trust, Chair of Greater Lincolnshire Food Partnership and a Liveryman of the Worshipful Company of Bakers in the City of London.

name: Omar Khaled
title: Chief Executive Officer - Founder
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Omar Khaled is the CEO and co-founder of Clicks, a leading private technology company based in Cairo with expertise in providing innovative media, marketing, branding, and advertising solutions. Partnering with multiple monetization technologies, Clicks has managed to build a strong portfolio of clients including Al-Ahram, Mubasher, and Egypt-Today amongst several other major media, public relations, and news agencies in Egypt. With over 5 years of experience in managing data-rich online portals, Khaled has successfully developed, launched, and is currently overseeing the technical operations of Egypt’s largest online newspaper, youm7.com. Additionally, Khaled is a seasoned project manager with an early career in UI/UX research, web development, and online advertising and has positioned himself as a consultant of choice for many businesses with an interest in re-inventing their online presence and operations. Khaled enjoys both sharing and gaining knowledge on the newest and latest in the world of technology and is a frequent attendee of many global tech forums. He holds a B.S. in Computer Sciences from the German University of Cairo, where he currently resides alongside his wife and two beautiful daughters.

name: Stuart Kippelman
title: Chief Information Officer & Commercial Cybersecurity Business Leader
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Stuart Kippelman serves as the CIO of Parsons Corporation. Prior to his current role, Kippelman held the position of Senior Vice President and CIO for Platform Specialty Products, a global organization specializing in high-tech specialty chemicals and technical services. Their offerings include a wide range of specialty chemicals used in various industries, such as agrochemical, animal health, electronics, graphic arts, plating, and offshore oil production and drilling. Before joining Parsons, Kippelman was the Senior Vice President and CIO for Covanta. During his tenure, he spearheaded global IT initiatives, employing innovative technologies to revolutionize the company's operations and revenue generation. His transformative work at Covanta earned him several prestigious industry recognitions, including the Top 100 CIOs in STEM in 2015, Computerworld's 2014 Premier IT Leader Award, and the CIO.com Top 100 Award in 2012, 2013, and 2014. Stuart Kippelman also gained valuable experience during his time at Johnson & Johnson, where he held various leadership and technical roles with increasing responsibilities. These roles included Corporate Director of Technology M&A and Emerging Market Growth, CIO/CTO of Health & Wellness, VP of Global Infrastructure Technology Engineering and Development, as well as positions in engineering, architecture, research, portfolio management, deployment, and operations. In addition to his corporate roles, Kippelman is a monthly guest columnist for the Wall Street Journal and a featured blogger on Computerworld.com. He holds multiple patents in data mining and Business Intelligence. Stuart is a seasoned speaker, frequently delivering keynote presentations at major industry conferences on a wide range of topics, including Leadership, Innovation, Mobile Computing, Consumerization of IT, Cloud, and Cybersecurity. Stuart Kippelman actively contributes to the development of the next generation of IT leadership and serves as a career mentor for the CIO Executive Council Pathways program. He holds an M.B.A. in Technology Management and a B.S. in Computer Science from the University of Phoenix.

name: Jim Knight
title: CEO/Principal
bio: Jim Knight is CEO & Principal of Knight Insights. In this role, Knight provides comprehensive consulting to drive IT optimization by employing an outcome-based approach at a lower cost. From 2007 to March 2016, Knight was Global CIO at Chubb Corporation, where he provided vision and leadership to maximize the usage of information technology to create and maintain leadership for the corporation worldwide. Knight is a project management professional (PMP) and he serves on the boards of the Society of Information Management (Executive Vice Chair) – International, and the Association for Cooperative Operations Research and Development (ACORD). Knight brings over 30 years of experience in the delivery and management of information technology primarily in the property and casualty insurance industry. Prior to Chubb, he worked at Utica National Insurance Company, Home Insurance Company, and Merck-Medco. Knight holds a B.A. in Computer Science and a M.S. of MIS from Utica College.

name: Shawn Knox
title: Program Executive
bio: Shawn Knox is part of the NJ Medicaid Modernization Program Executive at Trigyn Technologies. Prior to Trigyn, Knox was the CIO of Stratos Consulting. Before Stratos, he was SVP and CIO of Ansell Healthcare, a leading global manufacturer and marketer of barrier protection products for the healthcare industry. Knox joined Ansell in March of 2005 as a member of Ansell's executive management team reporting to Ansell's CEO and managing director. Prior to joining Ansell, Knox served as Vice President of Global IT at Photronics Inc., a global manufacturer of photomask, an enabling technology supplier to the semiconductor industry.Prior to Photronics, Knox held senior global IT positions at Duracell, Inc., a consumer battery manufacturer and at American Saw Manufacturing, an industrial cutting tool manufacturer.

name: Dan Koloski
title: Professor of the Practice and Head of Learning Programs, Roux Institute
bio: Dan Koloski is the head of learning programs and professor of the practice in the College of Professional Studies at Northeastern University’s Roux Institute. At the Roux Institute, Koloski carries administrative responsibility for all learning programs across degree and non-degree contexts. Professor Koloski’s own teaching is in analytics and its applicability in business, and in addition to his work within various Northeastern degree programs, he works with the Roux partnerships team to develop and teach custom non-degree courses to partner employees. Koloski joined Northeastern after spending over twenty years in the IT and software industry, working in both technical and business management roles across large and small companies. Most recently, Koloski was vice president of product management and business development at Oracle. Before Oracle, he was CTO and director of strategy of the web business unit at Empirix, which he helped spin out and sell to Oracle. Koloski holds a bachelor’s from Yale University and an MBA from Harvard Business School. Koloski has lived in Portland for several decades with his wife and two daughters. He is an avid sailor and skier, in addition to maintaining an active performing schedule in the Portland-area music and theater scene.

name: Aju Koshy
title: Chief Executive Officer
bio: Aju Koshy is Founder and CEO of ConvergePoint headquartered in Houston, TX. ConvergePoint ( https:// www.convergepoint.com/ ) is a leading provider of Enterprise Regulatory Compliance Software solutions. In 2018, ConvergePoint made it into Inc. Business Magazine’s list of fastest growing private companies in the United States. The company offers five full-featured software products for the complete life-cycle management of your Policies & Procedures, Contracts, Conflict of Interest Disclosures, Safety & Incident Tracking and Investigative Case Management. Our customers include several Fortune 500 companies, large and mid-size organizations across all industry verticals. We cater to organizations with at least 250 employees in size and currently serve customers in 35 states around the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, the European Union and Australia. Koshy has over 20 years of experience in the IT industry. In 2002, he founded a software consulting company in New York City. In 2012, the corporation changed its strategy and pivoted to becoming a company totally focused on software products. He is responsible for managing the day to day operations of the company as well as overall growth of the company.

name: Inder Koul
title: Founder and Managing Partner
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Koul is currently the Founder and Managing Partner at Koul Insights. He was also the Co-Founder and President of an early-stage healthcare technology startup, and serves as the Strategic Partner and Head of Emerging Fin-Tech Products and Services at CE3 Solutions. Koul has experience as the Chief Information Officer at 1st Source Bank. With over 30 years of experience in global leadership and innovation, business transformation, technology, digital, cyber, and operations, Koul also served as the EVP and CIO for First Niagara Financial Group. As a member of the Management committee, he chaired the Enterprise Investment Governance Committee and managed the Enterprise Chief Digital and Data Officer functions for the bank. This included overseeing Digital Technology, Digital Products, Digital Marketing, Data, and Social Media operations. Koul also provided vision and leadership for the development and implementation of the company's 4-year Strategic Investment and Business Transformation Program. Prior to First Niagara, Koul was with Huntington Bank where he served in the roles of CIO for the bank’s Commercial, Auto Finance and Corporate Services groups, and as the Director of Enterprise Project Management Office. Before that, he was with Bank of America for seven years in a variety of senior executive positions, including business processing, operations, customer service and IT program management roles. Inder has managed business development and implementation of large-scale programs at IBM, KPMG Consulting, Verizon, Morgan Stanley and Citibank UK. Koul is a member of the Technology Committee at Roswell Park Cancer Institute and a member of Board of Advisors at the FAIR Institute, which is focused on advancing the risk profession towards financially-oriented and business-aligned decision making. He has served on the Board of BCAT (Buffalo Center for Arts and Technology) as well as a Board Member of the Strategic Advisory Board at FTV Capital – a leading growth investment fin-tech equity firm. Koul is a graduate of BITS-Pilani University in India, with a bachelor’s degree in Electronics Engineering. During his career, he has lived, worked and done business in North America, Europe, India, Latin America and Asia.

name: Nicolas Kozakiewicz
title: CIO
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Nicolas Kozakiewicz currently serves as a Digital Euro Market Advisory Group Member at the European Central Bank, where he contributes his expertise and insights to shape the digital Euro market. He has 24 years of experience in Innovation and R&D, split between big corporations and founding, funding, and developing start-ups in Europe and the Silicon Valley. Mr. Kozakiewicz has led multinational engineering teams through upcoming trends and technologies to business-applied, revenue-generating services. For the past few years as Chief Innovation Officer at Worldline, he has driven Global R&D and Innovation, leading teams to focus/find, evaluate, prioritize, and drive to market disruptive technologies/usages, while instilling innovation, igniting new services, and increasing performance and efficiency in the portfolio. Mr. Kozakiewicz joined Atos group in 2009 and holds a SW/HW engineering degree from EFREI as well as a start-up specialized M.B.A. from HEC in France. He is also a Fellow in the Expert group of Atos/Worldline. Mr. Kozakiewicz lectures on blockchain at Columbia University, Polytechnique, Telecom ParisTech, Institut Mines-Telecom, and HEC. He has worked on current trends including blockchain and stable coins, quantum-resistant cryptography, and artificial intelligence.

name: Kurt Kratchman
title: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Chief Revenue Officer (CRO)
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, New Business Ventures
bio: Kurt Kratchman is currently the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) at virti. He also holds the position of Founding Partner at Makude Investment Partners LLP. His past position was GVP, Product Development at Oracle Data Cloud at Oracle. He is the former COO of Grapeshot and led global operations across offices in New York, Cambridge, London, and Singapore. Grapeshot applies advanced keyword technology to the programmatic advertising market. Kratchman began his career in traffic engineering in New York City. From there, he applied his data management skills to data visualization, rich media creation, content management, digital distribution and software development. After two successful exits to WPP (Blast Radius and Schematic), he worked as a consultant helping companies commercialize technology transfer opportunities, economic development, and most recently in private equity with a focus on technology company turnarounds. Kratchman studied Philosophy and Jewish Studies at Tulane University and The Hebrew University of Jerusalem, and new media at The Vancouver Film School.

name: Warren Kudman
title: Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer
bio: Warren Kudman is the SVP & CIO of Turner Construction. ​Previously, he was the CIO of Sealed Air Corporation, a $7.8 billion global manufacturer and world leader in food and industrial packaging and cleaning and hygiene solutions. In this role, Kudman was responsible for delivering information services that enabled the execution of the Sealed Air’s strategic objectives, improved customers’ ease of doing business with the company, and supported continuous improvement in Sealed Air’s day-to-day operations. During his time as CIO, Kudman was also selected to lead the Integration Program for Sealed Air's acquisition of Diversey, a $3 billion global entity. Prior to joining Sealed Air, Kudman spent six years with McKinsey & Company in the New York, New Jersey, and Copenhagen offices. During this time, he advised clients in market strategy, information technology strategy and management, operations improvement, and sales force productivity in the transportation, financial services, telecommunications, and pharmaceutical industries. Kudman is on the Advisory Board of IT Central Station, a social network where IT professionals share their experience and expertise on a wide range of IT products and services.

name: Balaji Kumar
title: CTO
bio: Balaji Kumar is a proven Chief Information Officer with extensive experience across the Finance, Retail, and Telecommunications sectors. He has led large-scale digital transformation initiatives in highly regulated industries, driving business growth, enhancing customer experiences, and optimizing operational efficiency. With deep expertise in public cloud adoption, technology modernization, and AI-driven innovation, Balaji has successfully delivered scalable and secure digital ecosystems that empower teams and strengthen organizational competitiveness. His leadership is defined by a strong focus on aligning technology strategy with business goals while maintaining rigorous compliance with industry standards.

name: Luc LaFontan
title: Vice President, Technology
bio: Luc LaFontan currently serves as the Vice President of Technology at Teladoc Health since January 2022. In this role, he leads a technology team of over 100 professionals, overseeing various domains such as End User Services, Identity & Access Management, Infrastructure, Network, Database Engineering, Application Services, and Tech Financial Management for the global platform. As an adept technology executive with a global perspective, Luc LaFontan is known for his ability to drive digital transformation, cultivate high-performing teams, and harness enterprise technology management capabilities to deliver tangible business change through process enhancements and innovative technology solutions. LaFontan's expertise lies in developing technology roadmaps and crafting innovative solutions for large-scale environments encompassing up to 200+ countries, with over 2400 sites and 200,000 users. Moreover, he excels in building IT operations founded on service excellence, employing best practice frameworks to provide highly responsive, high-quality, and cost-effective IT services to Fortune 500 and global multinational organizations across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. Additionally, Luc LaFontan is recognized for his prowess in building project management capability by leveraging best practices and principles such as Agile, Scrum, PMP, and Prince2. This expertise enables him to deliver large-scale business transformations that drive real business value in terms of revenue, productivity, and business competitiveness. Prior to his current role at Teladoc Health, he served as the CIO of ApiJect Systems Corp, where he continued to demonstrate his knack for creating a real business competitive advantage through technology-driven solutions.

name: Pascal Lauffer
title: CEO
bio: Pascal Lauffer is the CEO of Onbrane. Lauffer is a business transformation manager with over 20 years of experience in fast changing environments. He has served as CEO, COO, CIO, and CTO at global organizations with over 1000 employees, and has led numerous strategic programs that rationalized and improved organizational competitiveness. Prior to Onbrane, Lauffer was a Partner at Capco, where he helped CIOs to align and contribute to their company’s business strategy, continuously improve their organization’s agility and take the lead in introducing value creating innovations. Previously, he was president of Astrance Capital, a private equity fund, and chairman of Descamps, a leading French home textile company. Prior to founding Astrance, Lauffer was acting global Chief Operating Officer of Newedge and led the post-merger integration of Fimat and Calyon Financial. Lauffer joined Fimat Americas in 2004 to manage the acquisition of Preferred Trade Inc. Preceding Fimat, Lauffer held several global chief information officer positions at Société Générale, supporting businesses such as capital markets, commercial banking and commodities. He led large-scale reengineering programs resulting in deep transformations of businesses and in significant efficiency gains. Lauffer holds an M.B.A. in finance from Columbia Business School and an M.S. in computer science and mathematics from Nancy University.

name: Bruce R. Lee
title: Senior Vice President, Chief Technology Officer
bio: Bruce R. Lee is the SVP, CTO of Centene Corp. Lee is the former SVP and Head of Operations and Technology at Fannie Mae where he led a full digital transformation of the IT function and re-platformed major finance, risk, employee and customer facing systems. He was previously EVP and Group CIO at NYSE Euronext during the transition to ultra-low latency trading. Lee was also COO & CIO of HSBC Americas Investment banking and Global Chief of Staff for IT, HSBC global businesses. There, he managed IT for HSBC in the Americas for its global banking and markets businesses, with development teams in Canada, New York, Mexico, and Brazil. Lee was also responsible for all risk, control, strategy and planning activities of global business IT covering asset management, security services, private banking, global banking and global markets across the HSBC network in 86 countries. Prior to HSBC, Lee was the CIO at BNP Paribas in Tokyo for two years, and the CIO at BNP Paribas Americas for seven years. Lee holds a B.S. with first class honors in computer science from Hertfordshire University.

name: Jay Leek
title: Managing Partner & Co-founder
location: West Palm Beach, FL, United States
bio: Jay Leek is a Managing Partner at ClearSky. He also serves as the Managing Partner & Co-founder at SYN Ventures in West Palm Beach, Florida, United States, a position he has held since March 2021. Leek was the former CISO for Blackstone, where he oversaw the Blackstone Portfolio Company Information Risk & Security Community. Prior to joining Blackstone, Leek established, built, and headed up global information risk and security programs for Equifax and Nokia. Over the past 20 years, he has also worked as a product manager for information security vendors as well as a consultant to numerous telecom companies, government agencies, and financial institutions, assisting them with business development, strategic planning, and architectural design required to meet their information risk and security objectives. He also acts as an industry advisor for information security organizations and government agencies. Leek currently serves as a Board Director for Optiv Security, RedOwl Analytics, and the NY Metro ISSA Chapter, Board Observer for Cylance and Phantom Cyber, and on the Advisory Board for iSIGHT Partners and Risk IO.

name: Maya Leibman
title: Senior Advisor
bio: Maya Leibman is a Senior Advisor at American Airlines. In her previous role as CIO, she was responsible for all information technology systems, including systems development, infrastructure, operations, and strategy. Prior to her position as CIO, Leibman served as president of the AAdvantage loyalty program, where she oversaw all aspects of the AAdvantage program and led the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) and customer research initiatives for the airline worldwide.
