She originally joined the airline in 1994 in the Revenue Management department and led various pricing and yield management teams focused on optimizing airline revenue. Leibman serves on the Dallas Theater Center board of trustees and volunteers for a number of local organizations, including the So SMAART program, where she works with minority girls ages 9 to 12, teaching them about technology and life. She is an executive sponsor for Living Green — American’s Employee Business Resource Group that promotes living green at work and in the community in order to reduce our carbon footprint and explores new ways to ‘‘reduce, reuse and recycle.’’ Leibman holds a B.A. in English literature from the University of Chicago and an M.B.A. from the University of California, Berkeley.

name: Beatrice Leon
title: Coach
bio: Beatrice Leon is currently serving as a Coach at GoalSpriing-Choose Your Coach, where she leverages her expertise to guide individuals in achieving their goals. She also holds the position of Business Coach at BLCoaching, assisting individuals and businesses in their growth and development. Beatrice Leon is an Executive Director, External Relations at WOS, a 501-C Non-profit dedicated to helping place high school students and veterans in long-term career and educational opportunities. Leon’s has spent the past 30 years of her career in IT. She spent 10 years at Pernod-Ricard Group, an 8.2BEuro revenue company worldwide co-leader in the Wine and liquor industry. Leon was CIO for Pernod-Ricard Americas (subsidiary of the Pernod-Ricard Group), a 2 billion revenue business, (USA, Canada, and Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Colombia and Venezuela). Prior to this role, she was CIO for Pernod-Ricard North America. She joined Pernod-Ricard in 1999 and during her terms she successfully integrated the acquisition of Seagram (Chivas, Martell, Seagram Gin, etc.) Allied Domecq (Malibu, Beefeater..), as well as Vines & Spirits (Absolut) from a business process, system and team point of view. Leon redefined the IT application strategy to support current and future business, consolidated the Data Center in Canada, reorganized the IT support, globalized the IT team at the Americas level and standardized the ERP applications resulting in a better service to the Americas Region and a decreased Global IT budget. Prior to joining Period-Ricard, Leon was IT Director of DMC Inc., the U.S. arm of DMC SA, a 1 billion textile company, with U.S. customers including The Gap, Levi’s, and Wal-Mart. There, she successfully reorganized the IT department, cutting maintenance cost by 30 percent and drastically improving rate of service. Leon started her career in France at Fromageries Bel SA, the maker of Laughing Cow and Babybel, and moved to the US in 1989 to take the role of the information systems manager at Fromageries Bel, Inc., the U.S. subsidiary of Fromageries Bel SA. Among major achievements, she developed and implemented a sales and marketing system for headquarters and sales managers, using client server technology to give access to sales and marketing data. In 2014 Leon achieved an Executive Coaching Certificate from Columbia University. She holds an M.S. in Economics from the French University Paris-I. Leon is a sailor and a Black belt Karateka.

name: Yves Leon
title: Justice EXPERT Appeal Court ; AIX EN PROVENCE
bio: Yves Léon has dedicated his professional career to computer systems and the digital realm. Currently serving as the President of the "Europe Commission" at the CNCEJ (National Council of Judge’s experts in France), his primary responsibility is to establish a European global approach for judge’s experts, collaborating with colleagues from European and Northern American countries. Yves LEON is actively involved in digital operational strategy programs, focusing on Risk Management, Transition, and Digital Strategic Plans. In addition to his role at the CNCEJ, Yves is a member of various boards, including "Internet Encounters" Vercors, National Council of Forensic Judge’s Experts ( www.cncej.org ), Container and Canal Company ( www.canal2point0.fr ). He is also a member of the National Company of Judicial Experts in Information Systems and Automated Processing, Groups of experts at the Court of Appeal of Aix, HEC Alumni Group, Information Systems. During his time at HEC, he contributed to a research project on the Boolean method of analysis. Before his current position, Yves spent 15 years with the shipping company Compagnie Générale Maritime (now CMA-CGM), serving as the CTO of the Mediterranean branch. His contributions include the development of world passenger booking systems (ESTEREL-AMADEUS) and inter-professional shipping computer systems for Marseille and Le Havre Port Authorities. His experience extends to the approach of International Consumer online Information Systems. In 1987, he established his consulting firm, specializing in IT strategy, and later created a startup, GET, in 1992. GET was selected by the French IRS (Ministry of Finance) to bring the income tax form online. Seeking an industrial partnership, Yves sold GET to the BERGER LEVRAULT group. Concurrently, he founded SYSTEMIA (1988-2010), an internal research structure, and a pedagogical branch developing Master's degrees (Conférence des Grandes Écoles) in computer science. These degrees focused on Artificial Intelligence, Internet technologies (administration and e-commerce), and Database management. Yves is a judge’s expert in computer systems and digital matters, serving the Court of Appeal of Aix-en-Provence since 1983 and the Administrative Court of Appeal of Marseille. He brings extensive experience in IT projects across large international organizations and small enterprises in both private and government sectors, including La Poste, VEOLIA, Simelectro, Richemont, and PacBell (Telecom operator of California). Furthermore, Yves contributes to education as a lecturer at Columbia University in New York City (School of Professional Studies).

name: Diane Levin
title: Vice President, Digital and Technology
bio: Diane Levin is the former Chief Information Officer of McCormick. At McCormick she led the global IT organization and drove transformative initiatives to create value across business functions and segments. With over twenty-five years of broad functional business experience in consumer goods, medical devices, and technology industries, Diane's expertise is extensive. Her previous roles at Johnson & Johnson, including Vice President of IT Global Customer Development and CIO Consumer EMEA, provided her with significant leadership skills and a comprehensive understanding of IT's role in the consumer goods sector. During her time with McCormick, Diane's focus was not only on ensuring scalable, compliant, and efficient technology solutions but also on leveraging technology for competitive advantage and innovation. Her commitment to fostering inclusive work environments was evident in her active participation in broader corporate initiatives as a member of the McCormick Global Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Council. An additional testament to Diane's advocacy for gender diversity and inclusion in the consumer goods industry is her sitting on the Advisory Board from 2015 to 2019 for the Leading Executives Advancing Diversity (LEAD). She currently serves on the Board of Directors of LifeBridge Health, demonstrating her continued commitment to community and health services. Her educational background includes a Bachelor of Arts in Economics from George Washington University.

name: Josh Lewis
title: Managing Partner
bio: Josh has over 25 years of experience in technology, startups, finance, and marketing. As Managing Partner at Level49, he provides marketing and advisory services to early-stage technology companies and investment groups. He is also a partner at Grays Peak Capital, an alternative investment firm focused on transformative consumer and technology companies, and serves on the advisory board of a robotics and smart automation company in the aerospace industry. Formerly, he was CIO at Aries Acquisition Corporation, an investment vehicle targeting companies in the Space, AI/ML, Cybersecurity, Quantum, and Blockchain industries. Prior to Level49, he was CTO at a global technology consulting firm serving the capital projects industry. Responsible for technology strategy, product development and project delivery, he led the firm’s growth from its inception to become a leading technical integrator in its space. Previously, he led data engineering and analytics initiatives at Davidson Kempner Capital Management, an alternative asset management firm, and was a Director at CIBC World Markets, a global investment bank. Josh holds a B.A. in Psychology from UC San Diego and is a graduate of the Global Executive MBA program at Columbia Business School and London Business School.

name: Kevin Lupowitz
title: Senior Director of Engineering
bio: Kevin Lupowitz is the Senior Director of Engineering at Paxos. Paxos is a regulated blockchain infrastructure platform, building a new, open financial system. Prior to Paxos, Kevin was the CIO at Tassat Group LLC, where he helped deliver the first 24x7 blockchain based payment platform for a US regulated Bank. Prior to Tassat, he was the CIO and Global Head of Technology at CLEAR, a biometric Identity as a Service organization that supports over two million clients on a 24/7 basis. Before that, he was CIO at FXall, one of the largest institutional foreign exchange (FX) marketplaces, which was subsequently acquired by Thomson Reuters. Before FXall, Lupowitz was a founding employee, CIO and Global Head of Technology, at Liquidnet, where he spent 11 years building and expanding the first equities "dark pool" which supported trading in 40 markets globally for over 700 of the largest asset managers in the world. Lupowitz started his career as a software engineer for the first electronic order management system for institutional trading of equity and fixed income instruments. He holds both FINRA Series 7 and 24 certifications and is a named inventor on 18 US patents covering electronic securities, trade execution, and tokenless biometric security screening.

name: Venkatesh Mahadevan
title: Founding Board Member
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Venkatesh Mahadevan is the Founding Board Member at I am CAAS. In the past, he served as the CIO of Dubai Investments PJSC, In this role, he is responsible for all IT initiatives and the technology functions within the organization. One of his primary responsibilities is to keep the Dubai Investments group of companies at the forefront of cutting edge technology. Mahadevan has over two decades of progressive work experience in leadership positions with top Fortune 100 companies such as Motorola, Coca-Cola and Procter & Gamble. He has led Digital Transformation programs at global corporations and been a part of the Transformation leadership team. His background includes extensive exposure in managing strategic initiatives, planning, establishing, directing technology programs and leading IT functions spanning multiple geographies. During his professional career across several industries, including FMCG, Telecom & Retail, he has held several leadership positions and been a member of the Executive Committee, serving on Leadership Boards. Prior to joining Dubai Investments, Mahadevan was in a technology leadership position at Motorola where he was instrumental in implementing key strategic technology initiatives across the APAC region and a key member of the Global Supplier Management Leadership Team, accountable for driving strategic relationships with global/strategic vendors/partners out of India. Mahadevan holds an M.B.A. from Newport University and is a research scholar. He is also the recipient of the CXO 50 and several CIO leadership awards. He is an accomplished ENERGY & Peak Performance Speaker, and a Premier Skills Coach. In addition to holding several prestigious certifications from accomplished international organizations, Mahadevan is also an Internationally Qualified Counsellor and a distinguished Guest Faculty at leading management institutes, where he mentors Engineering and Management students.

name: Dirk Manelski
title: CTO
bio: Dirk Manelski is CTO at PIMCO. Pior to PIMCO, Manelski was Managing Director at JP Morgan, where he was responsible for the technology that drives JP Morgan’s Macro Trading Business which includes Rates, Foreign Exchange and Commodities. He was also responsible for technology functions across J.P. Morgan’s Corporate & Investment Bank including Electronic Trading, Regulatory Systems, Market Data, and the firm’s common development platform, Athena. Previously, Manelski was Head of Trading and Risk Technology at Deutsche Bank where he was responsible for designing and delivering the platforms that powered DB’s financial markets businesses as well as running High Frequency Technology. Before joining Deutsche Bank, Manelski held positions including COO of Fixed Income Technology at Bear Stearns, Chief Architect of ICAP plc, and CEO and founder of Crosslinks Systems, a development firm specializing in building trading systems and exchange Manelski holds a degree in Applied Physics from Columbia University. He sits on Columbia’s Engineering Council and is a Non-Executive Director at OpenFin.

name: Thomas Marcin
title: Senior Manager Advisory & Transformation
bio: Thomas Marcin is a senior manager at West Monroe, known for his entrepreneurial and innovative approach. His ability to challenge the status quo has led to significant impacts, such as transforming the network for a Fortune 50 global manufacturing company, reducing annual spending by over $50 million while increasing network capacity by 62%. With experience in creating and managing international start-ups, Thomas brings a global perspective to building cross-functional teams. For a Fortune 100 company, he established their first IT innovation office, leveraging it successfully for various business units’ transformation initiatives. Thomas, equipped with an engineering background and Six Sigma Blackbelt skills, combines analytical thinking with a focus on data and science to identify improvement opportunities and address operational inefficiencies. His soft skills and training expertise contribute to evolving organizations into high-performance cultures. Thomas joined West Monroe from Pace Harmon, where he served as a director. His previous roles include vice president with a network engineering and IT organization, along with various IT leadership positions at DuPont. He holds a bachelor’s degree in engineering and computer science from Bucknell University. What sets Thomas apart is his extensive global experience, having lived and worked on four continents and fluently speaking German, Spanish, and Portuguese. Outside of work, he enjoys outdoor activities with his family, including hiking, fly fishing, mountain biking, and golf. Thomas has made a significant impact as an executive advisor at Columbia University’s Center for Technology Management, mentoring MBA students and young professionals. Additionally, he leads a church youth group on missions to the Dominican Republic, participates in fundraising and team leadership for Habitat for Humanity for over 30 years. Notably, he has a personal connection with international families and has engaged in memorable experiences, including a fly-fishing trip at Lake Titicaca in Bolivia and receiving a golf lesson from Tiger Woods.

name: Don Marinelli
title: Adjunct Professor
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Dr. Donald Marinelli is a Senior Consultant at Point Park University and an Adjunct Professor at Northeastern University. Previously, he held the position of Director of Innovation for Inven Global Esports, the English-language website for Inven-Korea, the leading eSports and video game website in Korea with over an 80% market share. Dr. Marinelli also serves as the Associate Director of the Entertainment Technology Management program at Columbia University in the City of New York and as a Visiting Professor in the School of Arts, Media, and Engineering at Arizona State University in Tempe, Arizona. Dr. Marinelli retired from Carnegie Mellon University in April 2012, concluding an illustrious 31 years of service to the university in a variety of capacities. He is renowned for co-founding the Carnegie Mellon Entertainment Technology Center (ETC) alongside the late computer science professor Randy Pausch, known for "The Last Lecture." Additionally, Marinelli held a tenured position as a Professor of Drama and Arts Management at Carnegie Mellon University. Dr. Marinelli played a pivotal role in the creation of several notable programs, including the Master of Arts Management program at Carnegie Mellon University, the Master of Fine Arts in Acting degree program with the Moscow Art Theatre School in Russia, and the Master of Entertainment Technology degree program within the ETC. A native of Brooklyn, New York, Dr. Marinelli completed his undergraduate degree at the University of Tampa. He earned a Master of Arts in Clinical Psychology, specializing in Existential-Phenomenological Psychology, from Duquesne University. He later attended the University of Pittsburgh, where he received his Ph.D. in theatre history, literature, and criticism in 1987, with a dissertation focused on the early life and career of the Italian Futurist Filippo Tommaso Marinetti. Dr. Marinelli authored a book titled "The Comet and the Tornado," published in the spring of 2010 by Sterling Innovation, a division of Barnes & Noble. This book recounts the six years he spent alongside Randy Pausch, creating the Carnegie Mellon Entertainment Technology Center, which has gained international recognition as Carnegie Mellon's "Dream Fulfillment Factory." The book was named Barnes & Noble's "Book of the Month" in April 2010. In his current role, Dr. Marinelli serves as the EVP of Vissman Management, a merger, acquisition, and venture capital firm based in Pittsburgh. He has also been an adjunct professor at Duquesne University in Pittsburgh, consulted for numerous clients, and is a sought-after motivational speaker on the impact of digital media in education and society.

name: Andrea Markstrom
title: Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Andrea Markstrom is the Chief Information Officer for Schulte Roth & Zabel LLP. She works closely with the Executive Committee to identify, plan and implement technology solutions that align with the goals of the firm, practice groups and business departments. She leads the IT teams responsible for the firm’s various technologies and associated operations, and works to ensure that IT continues to deliver superior technology services and support. Andrea has more than 30 years of experience leading global information technology organizations for large law firms and corporations. She is the founder and CEO of i.WILL, a professional networking organization helping women advance in their careers. She is a two-time winner of the Inspire Orbie CIO of the Year Award for Corporate, is the Chair for Inspire CIO’s New York City Chapter, a board member with a number of LegalTech companies and an advisory committee member for LexisNexis and Gartner’s Evanta organization.

name: Sarah Martin
title: Vice President, Partner Solutions
bio: Sarah Martin is the Vice President of Partner Solutions for the National Alliance division of BlueCross BlueShield of South Carolina. In this role, Sarah has primary responsibility to develop, maintain and expand partnerships with over a dozen BlueCross BlueShield plans. This offering provides extremely flexible benefit configuration options to the nation’s largest and most complex self-funded employer benefit plans. Sarah is known as an innovative leader by challenging industry norms through disruptive solutions and unique partnerships. Prior to this role, Sarah led the enterprise customer experience initiatives and large employer product marketing division where she championed and implemented numerous consumer engagement efforts including strategic direction of well-being and incentive programs, virtual visits, and remote health, finding and pricing care and the company’s digital strategy. She holds a bachelor’s degree in social relations from Michigan State University and a master’s degree in political management from George Washington University. Sarah is heavily involved in the Columbia, SC community through Junior League of Columbia and serves as President of the Christian Assistance Bridge (CAB) food pantry.

name: Alejandro Martinez
title: Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Alejandro Martinez serves as the Chief Information Officer at Fortrea and holds a role as a Member of the Board of Advisors at Quantum Metric. He previously held the position of SVP and Chief Information Officer at Capri Holdings. With a distinguished career spanning multiple industries, including retail, automotive, and pharmaceuticals, Mr. Martinez has established himself as a global technology leader. He joined Capri Holdings in March 2021, where he oversees the global IT teams and leads critical initiatives, such as eCommerce, digital transformation, SAP ERP implementation, and the development of a long-term systems roadmap to support future growth and business requirements. Notably, as the former CIO of Walgreens, Mr. Martinez spearheaded a comprehensive transformation of the company's retail digital platform. His earlier career included various CIO roles at General Motors, as well as serving as SVP and CIO at QuintilesIMS. During his time at QuintilesIMS, he worked across LATAM, EMEA, North America, and London, where he held the role of CIO for Europe. Boasting over two decades of experience as a global executive in both mature and emerging markets, Mr. Martinez has led numerous successful transformations. His expertise spans digital transformation, customer-centric strategies, supply chain management, and the implementation of cloud-native applications at scale. Alejandro Martinez is a graduate of Universidad La Salle, where he earned a B.S. in Computer Science and an M.S. in Sciences. He has also completed several certifications, including one from MIT Sloan School of Management in e-Business, as well as an Advanced Management and Leadership Programme from the University of Oxford.

name: Rocky Martinez
title: Senior Vice President, Technology
bio: Rocky Martinez holds the position of Senior VP for technology at Frontier Foundry and is the owner of RM Technology Systems. The team at RM includes Enterprise Architects skilled in design patterns, Data Systems Architects focusing on enterprise data solutions, Systems Architects specializing in high availability technologies, Security Architects with expertise in identity and security patterns, Network and Telecommunications Engineers with experience in robust telecommunications, and J2EE and .Net Development experts. Before joining Frontier Foundry, Rocky served as the Chief Technology Officer of the Reference Data Utility and accumulated over 29 years of experience in financial technology. He has held senior leadership roles at various software companies catering to Fortune 500 companies, such as Bank of America, Merrill Lynch, Allstate, Motorola, and consulting firms like Accenture and PriceWaterhouseCoopers. Rocky's focus revolves around driving digital transformation and creating opportunities for innovative products and services. He is currently pursuing a Doctorate in Business Administration from Rutgers University and holds an undergraduate degree from Fordham University and a Masters in Technology Management from Columbia University. Rocky is actively involved in developing disruptive digital services for the financial sector, particularly focusing on streamlining trade reference data through machine learning. He has also authored published articles and been featured in interviews. Additionally, Rocky hones his skills through his role as an Associate in Columbia’s University Technology Management Program at the School of Professional Studies.

name: John Martino
title: MD Clearing IT
bio: John Martino is the Managing Director of Clearing IT at The Options Clearing Corporation (OCC), which was recognized as Risk Magazine’s 2022 Clearing House of the Year and is the world's largest equity derivatives clearing organization. Before joining OCC, he served as the Global Head of IT & Operations for PRIME Services Clearing at Societe Generale's Global Banking Services Chicago Branch. He is a dynamic leader known for driving successful international transformation projects, achieving operational excellence, and fostering innovation. His career has been dedicated to the global financial services sector. In addition to his current role in Chicago, Martino spent four years in Societe Generale's Paris office, leading a global software development transformation. He began his career at Societe Generale in New York in 2002, heading the local Fixed Income IT team. Prior to his tenure at Societe Generale, Martino led the NY Fixed Income IT team at Credit Lyonnais (now Credit Agricole) for four years and was responsible for Citibank's Treasury Information Services for a decade. Martino holds a B.A. from Stockton University and an M.S. from Dartmouth College in Information Systems Management. He is an active sponsor of the Diversabilities Employee Resource Group at Societe Generale.

name: Matt McCormack
title: Chief Information Security Officer
bio: Matt McCormack is Managing Director and Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) for BNY Mellon, leading the Information Security Division within Global Operations & Technology. As CISO, he is responsible for defining, building, and operating a high functioning, enterprise-level cybersecurity organization that protect BNY Mellon’s $43 Trillion in Assets Under Custody, securely enable core businesses processes, protects the employees of the company, and preserves and enables the growth of shareholder value. Before joining BNY Mellon in June 2022, Matt held a series of senior security executive positions, including CISO for GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals, CISO for EMC, and Global Chief Technology Officer at RSA. Throughout his career, he developed a specialized competency: designing global security operations centers for Fortune 100 companies and governments; modernizing enterprise security and risk teams; and developing cloud security migration strategies, merger-acquisition, and divestment security plans in addition to board risk management plans. He also spent a large part of his career working within the US Federal Government and as a Cryptology Officer in the US Navy, assuming Senior Executive Service roles as CISO of the Defense Intelligence Agency, and Director of Security Operations and Director of Security Engineering at the Internal Revenue Service. Matt maintains CISSP and CSSLP security certifications. He earned a Masters and a Bachelor of Science degree in Industrial Engineering, both from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute as well as an MBA from the University of West Florida.

name: James McGlennon
title: Executive Vice President and Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: James McGlennon is an accomplished former Chief Information Officer of a Fortune 100 company, recognized for his ability to drive growth through digital transformation, process simplification, AI, and machine learning. With over 30 years of cross-industry technology experience, he possesses a deep understanding of both regulated and unregulated environments and has led teams in the US, Europe, India, and Africa. James leverages a forward-thinking approach to business strategy, enabling companies to stay ahead of the technology curve and evolving consumer expectations. As Executive Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Liberty Mutual Insurance Group (LMIG), James played a pivotal role in transforming the company into a technology-driven organization that provides industry-leading insurance solutions. He made technology innovation a central pillar of the company’s strategy and led a team of nearly 15,000 employees, contractors, and consultants worldwide in creating a global digital ecosystem that offers world-class products and services. James set industry standards by leading the transition to the cloud and implementing Agile software development capabilities that increased technology development cycle time and eliminated supply chain drag. A champion of innovation, James co-sponsored Liberty Ventures to support startup companies aligned with LMIG’S goals and created an internal engine for creating and testing new products. He was also instrumental in ensuring successful acquisitions worldwide, leading the technology integration of the company’s largest acquisitions. In addition to driving business growth, James also led a cultural transformation at Liberty Mutual Technology, which contributed toward a Computerworld ranking of “Best Place to Work in IT” in 2020, 2021, and 2022. He developed and launched a “Best Tech Secret” strategy to attract top technology talent and built a "GoForCode" program offering hundreds of business analysts the opportunity to expand their skill sets by attending coding schools free of charge. James also mentored many professionals who have advanced to top leadership roles within the industry and made it a priority to incorporate meaningful DEI and ESG efforts into performance goals. Before joining Liberty Mutual, James led software development at BellSouth Corporation and has experience consulting and leading technology functions in banking. He completed a year-long international assignment in Africa building the technology infrastructure needed to establish the National Social Security Authority of Zimbabwe. James is a highly respected board member who has served as Chairman of Liberty IT, a LMIG global subsidiary, as well as on multiple not-for-profit boards. He has been interviewed by multiple outlets as a thought leader on a range of topics related to technology transformation and global growth. His many accolades include the Forbes CIO Next 2021 award, Irish American Magazine’s Business 100 Award, and a 2018 CIO of the Year Orbie Award. James holds both a Master and Bachelor of Engineering from the University of Galway in Ireland and has completed Harvard Business School’s Advanced Management Program.

name: Dennis McIntosh
title: Managing Partner
bio: Dennis McIntosh is the founder of Prosperity Partners Consultancy, LLC., a full-service consulting company dedicated to creating the right solutions that resolve challenges and accelerate the value of their clientele. Dennis is responsible for improving cash flow, implementing forecasting systems, and building FinTech infrastructure to support business expansion. Dennis serves a broad spectrum of private, public, and not-for-profit companies and is committed to helping businesses build respectful trusting relationships with all key stakeholders. He is an expert in benchmarking, business modeling, value analysis, reorganization, major expansion and integration initiatives, and implementing risk management portfolio strategies to protect investments. Prior to founding Prosperity Partners Consultancy, Dennis spent 21 years leading the CFO and internal audit functions for international technology, manufacturing, publishing, and insurance companies. Notable experiences include taking Alfi, Inc. through an IPO, Senior Manager for E&Y, SVP for SBLI USA, Audit Committee Chairman for Intelligroup, and a variety of CFO positions, solving client capital issues, coaching leadership teams, and leading the transformation of financial operations. Dennis received his MBA from UCONN and his B.A. in business, biology, and psychology from Andrews University.

name: Sanjeev Mehra
title: Co-Founder and Director
company: Little Artists Art Studio Pte Ltd
bio: Sanjeev Mehra is the co-founder of Little Artists, a group of companies based in Singapore. Little Artists is a leading fine arts school with a mission to use technology to expand its established methodology and pedagogy into a platform for delivering high-quality arts education to children worldwide, making it accessible and inclusive. In addition to his role at Little Artists, Sanjeev has a wealth of experience in the corporate technology and Fintech sectors. He previously served as the CIO, Managing Director, and Head of Platform Innovations for Global Consumer Technology at Citi. In this capacity, he played a crucial role in driving Citi's digital transformation and pioneering platform innovations, positioning the bank as a global leader in digital banking. With nearly three decades of international experience in financial services and technology, Sanjeev Mehra is a recognized thought leader known for his ability to lead innovation, integrate new technology, conceptualize novel capabilities, and create ecosystems for global software deployment. During his time at Citi, he held various leadership positions, including CIO and Head of Product Development, where he led groundbreaking initiatives like Open Banking, Rainbow Transformation, and Smart Banking. His contributions were instrumental in Citi's success in obtaining a patent for the Smart Banking ecosystem. More recently, he successfully implemented Citi's Open Banking strategy, enabling the bank's products and services to rapidly expand on a global scale. Sanjeev Mehra's educational background includes earning a B.S. in Engineering and an M.B.A. from the Indian Institute of Management (IIM), Bangalore, India.

name: André Meillassoux
title: Partner
focus: Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: André Meillassoux is a highly specialized French attorney with expertise in information technology, data protection, and intellectual property. Proficient in French, English, German, and Russian, he manages both transactions and disputes in the fields of IT, data, and IP. Presently, he holds the position of Partner at ATM Avocats-Associes, a niche law firm specializing in technology information, intellectual property, and data law. Meillassoux collaborates with clients from France and around the world, providing legal counsel on various aspects of IT, including investments, software project implementation, internet issues, databases, data protection, and technology transfers. His outstanding contributions to the legal field have earned him recognition as a highly recommended professional by Legal Who's Who and acknowledged for his strong reputation by Decideurs Juridiques et Financiers. With a history of leadership in professional associations, André Meillassoux previously served as the President of the French Computer & Telecom Law Association (AFDIT) and represented France as a Local Representative in the International Technology Law Association (ITechLaw). Additionally, he actively participates in the International Federation of Computer Law Associations (IFCLA), which unites 15 national computer law associations. Meillassoux's commitment to knowledge sharing is evident through his organization of conferences and postgraduate courses on topics such as IT contractual techniques, Big Data, and data protection law. These educational initiatives are conducted in collaboration with institutions like the École Nationale de la Magistrature, École Centrale Supelec de Paris, and Lyon-1 University.

name: Ramon Millan
title: Chief Information Technology Officer
bio: Ramon Millan is a recognized business technology executive with over 25 years of experience in leadership roles working for multinational Fortune 100 companies and start-ups. His diverse experience includes technology leadership roles in the industries of Transportation, Travel, Hospitality, Consumer Products and Goods, Healthcare, and Consulting. Millan is currently the Chief Information Technology Officer at TECO Energy. Prior to that, he served as the Chief Information Officer at Virgin. He had previously held the role of Chief Technology and Digital Officer at the American Automobile Association (AAA). In this capacity, he was responsible for developing and implementing all technology efforts in support of AAA's business lines. He spearheaded several transformational programs and initiatives to support AAA's purpose of delivering outstanding service to more than 60 million members. Before his time at AAA, he served as the technology head for Carnival Corporation, Pfizer, and PepsiCo. Ramon Millan's career began as a consultant with Andersen Consulting and Booz-Allen. He holds M.S. degrees in Technology Management and Organizational Leadership from Columbia University and an M.B.A. from Yale University. Currently, he resides in South Florida with his wife and three children.

name: Laura Miller
title: Executive Vice President & Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Laura Miller is a transformational leader with a passion for defining and executing digital strategies to increase revenues, operational efficiencies and customer and employee engagement. Ms. Miller is distinguished by her inspirational leadership and trusted relationships that enable her to drive continuous business improvements. As CIO of Macy’s, Ms. Miller’s responsibilities include technology strategy, delivery, operations and security for all of the Macy’s brands in over 600 locations. Her role is to apply technology solutions to help Macy’s achieve its goal of becoming a digitally led omnichannel retailer. Previously, as Global CIO of IHG, Ms. Miller held similar technology responsibilities for more than 5,900 hotels in over 100 countries, where she helped to increase the number of hotel rooms in the franchise by 18%, increased digital revenue by 24% and overall revenue by 23%, launched two new brands, added six new brands to the portfolio and integrated several new major partners. A portion of the digital revenue increases were due to the expansion of digital technology in China including the launch of IHG.cn with WeChatPay and AliPay and the launch of the IHG WeChat mini-program. She has delivered several organization-wide transformational changes including the implementation of a new central reservation system that delivers increases in revenue and lowers operational costs. Ms. Miller’s focus on product innovation was also evident in her ideation of IHG’s new hotel operator platform (IHG Concerto®), that integrated workflow and data to improve overall guest service and hotel operations. Laura also provides strategic guidance and governance as a member of the board of EVO Payments (EVOP), a global merchant acquirer and payment processor. Ms. Miller’s background includes extensive multi-industry experience in both the public and private sector. While SVP at First Data Corporation (FDC), a highly regulated global payments processing leader that serves more than six million merchant locations, thousands of card issuers and millions of consumers, Ms. Miller navigated an extremely complex private equity ownership (KKR) environment, where she led several transformational initiatives to rearchitect the global business model to deliver operational and financial improvements. She worked with the international leadership teams to develop and implement strategies to address the global financial crisis. At TD Ameritrade, she was a key member of the technology M&A team where she led several aspects of the seven M&A integrations during her tenure. In addition, Ms. Miller has served in other senior leadership capacities at The Patent and Trademark Office, The Housing Opportunities Commission of Montgomery County, Maryland, and British Aerospace, PLC. Laura Miller holds an M.Sc. in Computer Systems Management from the University of Maryland. She has earned significant recognition throughout her career, most recently as the Super Global Georgia CIO of the Year awarded by ORBIE. Ms. Miller has spoken at several conferences ( https:// www.youtube.com/watch?v=vwUQXfafg8Y , https:// www.youtube.com/watch?v=vijJ8VDPORs , https:// www.youtube.com/watch?v=GvHLkbHbiQw ) and has been interviewed for many publications. When not serving in her corporate capacity, Ms. Miller works with her husband in their residential remodeling and property management business, which they have owned and operated for more than 20 years.

name: Margaret Miller
title: CIO, Board member, Board advisor
bio: Maggie Miller is a CIO, Board Member and Board Advisor at various companies. Miller is the former CIO of the New York State Office of Information Technology Services, appointed by Gov. Andrew M. Cuomo. In this role, she set statewide technology policy for all state government agencies and drives innovative solutions to the state’s technology challenges. Previously, Miller was the CIO of Girl Scouts USA. During her time there, she was responsible for transforming the Movement-wide IT services and platforms to better support and improve the experience for nearly 3 million girl and adult members in 90 countries. Prior to that role, Miller held senior technology roles at companies including Warner Music Group, J. Sainsbury PLC, National Westminster Bank PLC, The Merrett Group, TUI Travel, and Dell.

name: Steve Mills
title: Global CIO
bio: Steve Mills is Global CIO for iHeartMedia, Inc., which includes iHeartMedia and Clear Channel Outdoor. In this role, Mills is responsible for all aspects of information technology for iHeartMedia's businesses, working closely with all divisions to ensure the alignment and optimization of the company’s overall technology systems with the business priorities of its multiplatform assets, and overseeing systems and applications, infrastructure, data center operations, IT security and employee productivity. Prior to iHeartMedia, Inc., Mills held CIO positions at Motorola Mobility and Rackspace. His experience also includes serving as VP of Enterprise Systems for T-Mobile USA, Director of Technology Delivery for Texas Online and senior roles at BearingPoint, Inc., Alex eBanking, Intelligent Technologies, Berkom USA, pcOrder.com and E-Systems.

name: Joel Molinoff
title: Global Head, Third-Party Risk Products and Services
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Joel Molinoff is currently the Global Head, Third-Party Risk Products and Services at BlueVoyant, a global cybersecurity company focused on proactively defending organizations against advanced cybersecurity threats. He has over twenty-five years of experience in cybersecurity, data privacy, risk management, strategy, and operations. His experience spans both public and private sectors, including leadership roles at Fortune 500 companies, the National Security Agency (NSA), and The White House. Previously, Joel served as Executive Vice President, Chief Information Risk Officer, and Chief Information Security Officer for CBS Corporation, where he was responsible for managing information security, data privacy, and content protection for CBS' diverse portfolio of global media and entertainment businesses. Prior to joining CBS, Joel was Assistant Director of the President’s Intelligence Advisory Board at The White House where he supported the President of the United States and other senior government officials in the evaluation of intelligence-related matters. Previous to his time at The White House, Joel was an executive of the National Security Agency (NSA), where he developed and implemented signals intelligence, information assurance and cybersecurity operations. Joel is a respected leader in the information security industry. He is an advisor to numerous emerging technology and security companies and has served on various boards, including the U.S. Federal Communications Commission’s Communications Security, Reliability and Interoperability Council (CSRIC). An advocate for veteran support initiatives and career mentoring, Joel has served on the board of American Corporate Partners, a veterans’ support non-profit that helps returning veterans transition to the private sector. Joel actively mentors returning veterans and spouses, as well as students from Columbia University’s Graduate Technology Management Program. Earlier in his career Joel held positions at J.P. Morgan, GE Capital and Greenwich Associates and was an adjunct professor at Georgetown University’s Graduate School of Business where he designed and taught the school’s first course on corporate cybersecurity. Joel holds an M.B.A. from Columbia Business School and a B.A. from Colgate University. He is a Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP) and has earned several technical information security certifications from GIAC/SANS.

name: Mark Mooney
title: Professor/Lecturer
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Mark Mooney is the Managing Director for Veramea and a Principal for MyEdulife. Veramea provides transformational services to organizations undergoing growth and technological change. MyEdulife provides data governance software to organizations in the field of education. Previously, Mooney was SVP and CIO at McGraw-Hill Education, where he directed the use of technology to enhance learning, assessment, and other initiatives. Prior to joining McGraw-Hill Education, Mooney was VP and CIO for Harcourt Education, Reed Elsevier. He also served as SVP and CTO at Vivendi Universal’s Houghton Mifflin Company, where he was responsible for the integration of technology between Vivendi and Houghton Mifflin. Mooney has also held senior technology positions at the Bureau of National Affairs and Interactive Marketing Services. Mooney holds a B.S. in engineering from the U.S. Naval Academy, an M.S. in information information technology from Johns Hopkins University, and a Ph.D. in organizational systems from the Fielding Institute. Mooney has been awarded several achievement awards for his efforts related to implementing technology in organizations. In addition to receiving the Babson College Center for Information Management Achievement Award, he was an adjunct professor at George Washington University, and was a Fellow at the Change Management Program at John Hopkins University.

name: David Moradi
title: CEO & Member Board of Directors
bio: David Moradi is an accomplished entrepreneur, investor, and advisor to several market-leading technology companies. Currently, he serves as the CEO and Member of the Board of Directors at AudioEye, Inc., providing strategic leadership. Additionally, Moradi is the Founder and CEO of Sero Capital, LLC, a private investment firm focused on growth opportunities in the technology sector. He also holds the position of Co-Founder and Executive Chairman at First Contact Entertainment Inc., a leading virtual reality (VR) video game development studio. Before assuming his current role at AudioEye, Moradi spent a decade as the Founder and CEO of Anthion Management, a technology-focused fund that expanded to $1 billion in assets. In 2013, Anthion underwent a transformation into a family office, investing in various asset classes, including early-stage technology companies, corporate debt, real estate, and public equities. Prior to founding Anthion, Moradi served as a Portfolio Manager at Pequot Capital Management and worked as an analyst and Portfolio Manager for Soros Fund Management. His career began in 2000 as a special situations analyst for Imperial Capital LLC. Moradi holds a B.A. in psychology from the University of California, Los Angeles. Additionally, he is the Chairman of the David Moradi Foundation, a charitable organization dedicated to supporting education and veterans.

name: Harry D. Moseley
title: Board Advisor
bio: Harry Moseley currently holds the positions of Board Advisor, Senior Advisor, and Investor at Arrangr, Deutsche Börse, and The Journey Platform. Previously, he served as the Global Chief Information Officer (CIO) of Zoom Video Communications and held the position of CIO at KPMG, a prominent audit, tax, and advisory services firm with operations spanning 87 offices and over 23,000 employees and partners across the United States. Before his roles at Zoom and KPMG, Moseley was a partner at the Blackstone Group, where he also served as the Chief Information Officer. In this capacity, he supervised information technology, presentation services, publishing, market data, and research. Prior to joining Blackstone in 2005, Moseley led technology initiatives at Mantas, an enterprise software company specializing in compliance and anti-money laundering solutions. During his extensive career, Moseley held significant positions, including Managing Director at Credit Suisse First Boston for five years and Managing Director/Chief Technology Officer - Americas at the Union Bank of Switzerland for 14 years. In these roles, he was responsible for devising and executing technology strategies, overseeing deployment and operations, and managing technology integration projects such as UBS/SBC and CSFB/DLJ. His expertise spans front-office technology, encompassing trading, risk management, settlement, financial reporting, money transfer, and compliance, as well as managing distributed computing, networks, and general technology infrastructure. Harry Moseley earned a degree in engineering, mathematics, and computer science from Trinity College Dublin, Ireland. Currently, he serves as an advisor to the board of Enigmatec, is a member of the CIO Group, and holds a position on the Thomson Financial Executive Advisory Board.

name: Subroto Mukherjee
title: Chief Strategist, Startup Advisor
industry: Consumer Goods/Retail, Consulting Services, Healthcare, Startup/Entrepreneurship, Telecommunications
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Subroto is a global leader in digital transformation, innovation, and emerging technologies with over 20 years of experience; currently the Chief Strategist at FutureGlobalTech, a digital transformation boutique advisory firm. Before the current role, Subroto was the Global Strategy Lead for Cross Industry Solutions in AI, Analytics, OpenAI, ChatGPT, Gaming, and the Metaverse at Microsoft. Before his current role at Microsoft, Mr. Mukherjee was Head of Innovation for GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Health, Americas. Within GSK, his primary responsibility is to expedite digital innovation by leveraging emerging technologies, applying AI and machine learning, design thinking, gaming, automation, and immersive technologies (AR/VR/XR) to drive innovation. Mr. Mukherjee has been a strategic advisor to CxOs and has led executive briefings and conferences. He was also a guest speaker for the EMBA program at Cornell University and the MBA program at the Nigeria University of Technology Management. He judged the UPenn Tech Venture class. He is also a strategic advisor to GSK’s Shopper’s Science Lab’s (SSL) Digital Test and Learn Incubator platform. SSL encourages commercial testing with retailers and shoppers for “beyond the pill” and digital medicine initiatives by exploring new business models, self-care coaches, and connected devices/Internet of Things (IoT). Mr. Mukherjee was the managing principal of Enterprise Systems Integration for Verizon Enterprise Group. He led turnkey value-driven enterprise products and solutions in collaboration technologies such as cloud solutions, computer telephony integration, mobile enterprise application development, IoT solutions, and self-service applications. Mr. Mukherjee holds an MS in Computer Science from Columbia University and educational certificates from NYU. He is also currently continuing an Innovation and Entrepreneurship program at Harvard University.

name: Asli De Munnink
title: Global Director of Digital Products
bio: Asli de Munnink is a dynamic senior executive bringing the Tech, Digital & Marketing worlds together to accelerate revenue growth. A global digital transformation leader with multi-industry experience covering unique roles taken across B2B and B2C portfolios of strategy, product, marketing, IT, data, CX, operations and talent. Most recently, she took a unique leadership role at Gartner, a $5.5 Billion company and a member of S&P 500. Brought in to drive a digital transformation effort to grow & significantly transform C-suite & senior executive experiences, globally. Asli is a former Chief Digital / Information Officer with awards and recognition across industry groups including SAP, Salesforce, Gartner and IDC. Very strong C-level background, hands-on leader, comfortable with setting strategy and executing it. High tech global marketing veteran of HP, HP Enterprise and Cisco. Her global track record as a CX innovator, data strategist and a digital ecosystem builder, is coupled with her engineering background, astute business acumen and analytics. As a Gartner client, Asli was chosen to do a video case interview sharing her vision and success to 20,000 CIOs that participated at the global CIO Agenda of the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo™ 2021. Before joining the tech industry, Asli was a senior consultant conducting market intelligence, business development & strategic communications. Her clients were cross industry and global enterprises, including Caterpillar, The Economist, Lafarge, Sempra and IBM. Asli is known for her "positivity, integrity, perseverance, and loyalty." Recognized at the most senior levels in her work, on mentoring colleagues, and for leadership excellence across organizations. Active member of T-200 (Technology 200) and founding member of Women in Tech in Connecticut - USA. She is passionate about elevating individuals in the technology space. Fundraiser for pediatric cancer. B.S. in Civil Engineering and M.S. in Civil/Environmental Engineering. She did her master’s thesis at Universitat Stuttgart in Germany. MBA from Heriot-Watt Edinburgh Business School .Lived and worked in the US, UK, Germany, Switzerland and Turkey.

name: Apurva Munshi
title: President
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Apurva Munshi currently serves as the President of Right Size Byte Size LLC, an IT Services and IT Consulting company. Previously, he co-founded GenDeep, where he held the role of Chief Technology and Operations Officer. GenDeep was a cloud-based startup focused on helping organizations improve results with AI-enabled decision-making. Munshi co-founded GenDeep with fellow alumni from the EMSTM program at Columbia University. Munshi is an experienced digital transformation executive with a track record of increasing revenue and enhancing efficiencies across multiple industries. He has excelled in defining and executing business strategies with technology, establishing PMO, framework, and methodology standards, implementing vision using agile and DevSecOps, and managing large IT operations teams of over 500 professionals. He has collaborated closely with C-level executives to drive transformations in areas such as CRM, HR, Research, Finance, Marketing, Ratings, and Compliance. Munshi also served on the executive advisory board of Apttus for five years and witnessed the company's growth to a $1 billion valuation. He has provided guidance to smaller startup firms on their journey to Nasdaq. Over his career, Munshi has overseen programs with an overall business case yielding benefits exceeding $750 million. Munshi holds an Executive Master of Science in Technology Management degree from Columbia University in New York City and an MBA/MS-MIS from Boston University.

name: Patrick Naef
title: Managing Partner
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Patrick Naef is Managing Partner at Boyden Executive Search and non-executive director on the board of Franke Group, a USD 2bn globally acting manufacturing company headquartered in Switzerland. He is also Founder and CEO of ITvisor GmbH, a boutique consulting firm specialized in advising organizations on their digitalization journey and coaching IT executives. Naef is also Senior Advisor to McKinsey &Company. Naef is a member of the board of directors of several start-ups, lectures at several universities and sits on advisory boards of technology companies and venture capital firms based in Silicon Valley. From 2006 to 2018, he was CIO at Emirates Airline & Group in Dubai as well as a non-executive director on the board of SITA, a global telecommunications and IT company focused on the airline industry. From 2006 to 2014, Naef was CEO of Mercator, a subsidiary of Emirates Group. Other professional positions include CIO at SIG and Swissair as well as senior positions at Zurich Insurance, HP and Bank Julius Baer. In 2011 the German "CIO Magazin" and IDG honored him with the prestigious "CIO of the decade" award. Patrick Naef holds a Master’s Degree in Computer Science from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology (ETH) in Zurich, Switzerland, and an Executive MBA from the University of St. Gallen, Switzerland.

name: Adam Noble
title: Operating Partner - Information Technology | Cyber Security | CIO | CTO
bio: Adam Noble is the Operating Partner for Information Technology and Operational Excellence at Littlejohn and Company. Prior to Littlejohn, Adam was the SVP, CTO and Innovation Officer for American Water Works, where he provided vision and leadership by partnering with the business to deliver innovative digital solutions to his internal and external customers. Adam continues to focus on ways to utilize digital technology to help the business innovate, generate revenue, and increase profitability for an organization. He is a Project Management Professional (PMP), a member of the Society for Information Management (SIM), participates on several IT executive advisory boards, and is a member of several industry organizations. Adam is also a Computerworld Premier 100 IT Leader. He has over 30 years of experience in delivering digital and business solutions to global organizations. He has experience as a global CIO in the Pharmaceutical, Banking, Manufacturing, Telecommunications, Logistics, Consulting, Utility and Chemical Industries. Prior to joining Littlejohn, Adam had worked with American Water Works, GAF, Veritiv Corporation, JPMorgan Chase, Mars Incorporated, AlliedSignal Corporation, UPS, AT&T and Bristol Myers Squibb. Adam holds a B.S. in Political Science from Binghamton University and an M.S. in Management Systems Analysis from Kean University.

name: Pierre Noel
title: Group Chief Security Officer and Chief Privacy Officer
bio: Pierre is a world-recognized leader in designing and building digital resilience, cybersecurity, and enterprise strategies, with over 35 years of experience worldwide. Pierre is the Group Chief Security Officer and Chief Privacy Officer at Le Collectionist. He is a member of the board of advisors of Fortune 200 organizations, notably providing ongoing advice to the executive management and boards of Airbus, the International Committee of the Red Cross, the Swiss Stock Exchange, The Oman Investment Authority, the National Cybersecurity Authority, and other international firms & agencies. Pierre co-founded Innixus, a global online community for digital resilience practitioners. Previously, Pierre worked as the Chief Security Officer for Microsoft, covering the vast Asian and Pacific regions, and as the worldwide Chief Security & Privacy Officer for Huawei and Grab. Pierre was also the CEO of two successful international companies: TruSecure, acquired by Verizon, and Arial, acquired by IBM.

name: Filipe Nunes
title: Chief Information Officer - Transition Manager
bio: Filipe Nunes is a strategic technology leader with over 25 years of international experience in IT management, primarily within the banking sector. He has a proven record of aligning digital strategies with business objectives, leading complex transformations, and driving innovation while maintaining strong security and operational resilience. Holding a specialized master’s degree in digital transformation, his academic research explores Green IT governance and AI scalability in financial institutions, showcasing his commitment to sustainable and forward-thinking technology solutions. As a passionate mentor and advocate for knowledge sharing, Filipe is dedicated to guiding the next generation of leaders and helping students navigate the evolving challenges at the intersection of technology and management.

name: Patrick O'Callaghan
title: Project Manager
bio: Patrick O'Callaghan is a Project Manager at EirGrid. O'Callaghan has over 30 years experience in software, technology and project management. He has advised CEOs, CIOs and Finance Directors in utilities, infrastructure, communications, pharmaceuticals and Government. He has extensive planning, coaching and mentoring experience and teaches on MSc, MBA and MBS programs including University College Dublin, Liverpool John Moore’s and Dublin Institute of Technology. He is a recognized consultant and adviser to many large companies such as eir (formerly eircom), ESB (Ireland’s largest power generator and supplier), EirGrid (Ireland’s National Grid), CK Hutchison, Telefónica, Stryker and Johnson & Johnson. O'Callaghan studied Computer Engineering at the University of Limerick, holds an M.B.A. from London’s City University and carries professional certifications in Project Management, Business Analysis and IT Maturity.

name: Graham O'Sullivan
title: CIO for UK and Europe and Group CISO
bio: Graham O’Sullivan is the CIO for UK and Europe and Group CISO at Riverstone International, a specialist insurance firm. As a leader in the legacy sector, Riverstone International employs more than 700 professionals operating across multiple offices and affiliates spread across the UK, US, Malta, Ireland and Bermuda. Prior to his role at Riverstone, Graham held senior positions at NatWest, Lloyd’s Banking Group, and Citigroup, further showcasing his ability to lead and drive change. With twenty five years of corporate experience, Graham has excelled in various technology, regulatory change, data and analytics, and business transformation roles. His expertise extends to technology innovation, where he remains well-versed in developments at a detailed level, including artificial intelligence, machine learning, and blockchain. Outside of his professional responsibilities, Graham’s commitment to community service is truly inspiring. He actively contributes to the community as a member of the Board of Trustees at CAFOD, an international NGO addressing global poverty and providing disaster relief in over 40 countries, with an annual budget exceeding £50 million. He also serves on the charity’s Finance, Legal, Audit, and Risk Committee, demonstrating his dedication to making a positive impact beyond the corporate world. Graham holds a degree in Electrical Engineering and Microelectronics from University College Cork, Ireland.

name: Fola Ogunsiakan
title: Director and Founder
bio: Fola Ogunsiakan is a seasoned professional with over two decades of experience in various management, finance, technology, operations, and strategy roles, focusing on early-stage companies and startups. He is the CEO and Managing Director of Cedar Capital, an operating investment and advisory firm that specializes in real estate and early-stage technology businesses across sub-Saharan Africa. In addition to his role at Cedar Capital, Fola Ogunsiakan has taken on several new positions in recent years. These roles include Director and Founder at RESE, a PropTech subsidiary of Wondo Technologies, where he has been serving since January 2022, and Group CEO and Co-Founder at Wondo Technologies [Holding Company], a position he has held since June 2018. Furthermore, he serves as the Investment Committee Chairman (AIPF I & II) at Africa Plus Partners Limited since April 2017, and as a Non-Executive Director at CREDIT DIRECT LIMITED since April 2017. Fola Ogunsiakan's multifaceted background and his active involvement in various ventures make him a valuable contributor to the business landscape in sub-Saharan Africa and beyond. Prior to founding Cedar Capital, Ogunsiakan was a Partner and Co-founder of Constant Capital, a West Africa based Financial Advisory and Investment firm. Ogunsiakan's career spans over two decades, covering general management, finance, technology, operations and strategy roles with a particular focus on early-stage companies and start-ups. His experience spans over two decades with companies such as McKinsey & Co., Motorola, Accelon, Cummins, and Robertson Stephens & Co, which when coupled with his entrepreneurial activities has developed him into a well-rounded professional that is able to manage businesses through their full cycles, from inception to steady-state operations and eventually, financial exits. Prior to Constant Capital, Ogunsiakan worked at Motorola Networks where as Head of Business Operations & Business Development for Sub-Saharan Africa, he oversaw and helped manage transaction and strategic activities for Motorola Networks Division’s business development portfolio. During this period, he also served as a member of Motorola’s Sub-Saharan Africa (SSA) Senior Leaders Strategic Growth committee. As a principal of a South Africa based venture management group, Africa Venture Partners, Ogunsiakan started up and managed Accelon Nigeria - now Internet Solutions (IS) West Africa - a premier provider of data telecommunications solutions, which earned the Nigerian Telecoms Award for Best ISP of the Year in 2006. Upon exiting Accelon, Ogunsiakan served as Program Manager and Strategy Advisor on the 2006/2007 billion dollar acquisition of Nigeria’s national telecommunications operator – NITEL. Prior to moving to Nigeria, Ogunsiakan was a management consultant at McKinsey & Co. in Johannesburg, South Africa where he worked on multi-country engagements in the areas of strategy, capital productivity, process development, operational improvement, and restructuring, across various industries including Media & Telecommunications, Manufacturing, and Oil and Gas, across Africa. Prior to earning his M.B.A., Ogunsiakan served as Director of IT; Telecommunications at a distribution subsidiary of Cummins Engine Company where he established and managed the long-term technology strategy for the company’s Southern California and Mexico locations, as well as the company’s integration with the global Cummins sales and product networks. Ogunsiakan earned his M.B.A. from the Harvard Business School, and his B.S. Arch from Obafemi Awolowo University (University of Ife). Fola also serves on the board of various companies and associations.

name: James Oliver
title: Director
bio: James Oliver is the Chairman and CEO of Principle One, a multi-disciplinary, IT and audiovisual services company that operates throughout Asia. Oliver is also co-founder and Managing Director of One Space, a unique integrated architecture, technology and interior design practice. Both companies are head-quartered in Hong Kong. His background is in Computer Science, and he spent his early career at British Telecom Laboratories. In 1993, Oliver joined Goldman Sachs in London. There he was located on the equities trading floor and helped run the systems that enabled ever speedier, more complex financial transactions be structured and executed. In 1996, Goldman Sachs relocated Oliver to Hong Kong to manage what was known as Enterprise Technology – covering all information technology except in house application development. Following a 10 year career at Goldman Sachs, in what would be his final role as head of Enterprise Technology for all Asia, it was time for Oliver to sate his entrepreneurial appetite. He wanted to challenge himself to be at the centre of a business rather than playing a supporting role. In Hong Kong in May 2003, he opened the first Principle One office. Principle One now has people located in four countries, with blue-chip and boutique clients in more than thirty locations across Asia. As momentum built for Principle One, Oliver saw the opportunity for a unique collaboration of disciplines, to better serve businesses. In December 2004, he co-founded One Space with Architect Greg Pearce. One Space is the only integrated architecture, technology, and interior design practice in Asia. This award-winning practice shares offices with Principle One.

name: Susan Paternoster
title: Senior Vice President & US CIO
bio: Susan Paternoster is the SVP & CIO, US of Wilton Re. Paternoster is a senior IT manager with over 20 years of experience within global financial service organizations. She joined New York Life in 2006 as the company’s CIO for its Investment Management Group and went on to serve as the CIO of the Insurance Group before being named to SVP and COO in 2013. Earlier in her career, Paternoster served as a global technology executive with Citigroup, the head of US technology with Deutsche Bank and the Director of US Technology for the private banking division with Bankers Trust. Paternoster also spent two years working in management consulting with accounting mega-firm KPMG.

name: Andrew Peel
title: Managing Director, Strategy & Analytics FSI
bio: Andrew Peel is Managing Director, Strategy & Analytics FSI at Deloitte. Prior to Deloitte, Mr. Peel was Senior Managing Partner at Gartner. In this role, he was responsible for leading the talent, technology solutions, and strategic direction of Philadelphia Insurance’s IT Department. Mr. Peel previously served as Managing Director for Alsbridge and was the EVP & CIO of ACE Insurance’s International Division. Prior to ACE, he held various managerial roles across the IT hardware, software, services and advisory spectrum at IBM (US), The Continuum Company in Europe (now part of CSC), Ernst & Young, AIG, and BearingPoint. Andrew Peel holds a B.A. in International Relations & Comparative Politics (Political Science) from Yale University; an International M.B.A. from Henley Management College in the UK; and a Diploma in Marketing from the UK Chartered Institute of Marketing.

name: Steven Peltzman
title: Head of FeedbackNow by Forrester and Forrester Chief Business Technology Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: As Forrester’s first “chief business technology officer,” Steve Peltzman was responsible for guiding the transformation of Forrester’s own internal technology efforts from the traditional information technology (IT) approach to what Forrester advocates companies should adopt: business technology (BT). Now, in leading Forrester’s subsidiary FeedbackNow business and as a member of Forrester’s executive team, Steve is responsible for helping drive the company’s revenue, innovation, and growth as well as client value and success. FeedbackNow (feedbacknow.com) collects real-time signals from people, processes, and places to help companies lower costs and raise customer experience. Prior to joining Forrester, Steve spent a decade as the chief information officer at The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) in New York City. He led the technology design and implementation for MoMA’s $858 million new building project from 2001 to 2004. Earlier in his career, Steve spent seven years as a United States Air Force officer, where he developed and assessed stealth technology and tactics for the B-2 Stealth Bomber Program Office and served as a program director for the Joint Mission Planning Program Office. Steve holds a B.S. in Aeronautical Engineering from MIT and an M.B.A. from Columbia Business School. In addition, he is an instrument-rated general aviation pilot.

name: Mark La Penta
title: Strategy and Transformation Consultant
bio: Mark La Penta of LaPenta Consulting Group is a Strategy & Transformation Consultant. La Penta is a thirty-plus year industry veteran who supports in the creation and implementation of strategic business decisions with clarity and direction. This enables for savings while also ensuring future profitable business growth in the commercial and retail banking sectors. He formerly worked as a Principal with CCG Catalyst Consulting Group and as the CIO of Bank Leumi USA. La Penta previously worked at MetLife Bank as CIO, CTO, and Chief Operations Officer. He formerly worked at Fidelity Information Services and Summit Bank. La Penta was named one of banking's Most Innovative CIOs by Bank Systems & Technology Magazine in 2006, and he previously worked in MetLife's IT department.

name: Wendy Pfeiffer
title: Board Director and (Ret.) Public Company Tech Executive
bio: The former CIO of Nutanix, Wendy Pfeiffer, currently serves on several Boards of Directors, including Qualys, Sada, and the American Games Association. Pfeiffer has held leadership positions in technical functions at notable companies such as GoPro, Yahoo!, and Cisco. She is also a proud board member of Girls In Tech. Known for her commitment to operational excellence, delivery, and a strong focus on innovation, she has played a crucial role in envisioning and implementing digital technologies on a global scale. In 2018, Pfeiffer received recognition as one of the National Diversity Council's 50 Most Powerful Women in Tech and was named one of the Women of Influence by the Silicon Valley Business Journal. She holds a B.S. in Business Administration with a specialization in Technology Management and Operations from the University of Phoenix.

name: Arka Mukherjee, PhD
title: CEO
bio: Dr. Arka Mukherjee is CEO of Global IDs Inc., a data management software company based in New York City. The company provides software and services for solving complex data analysis and integration problems in global companies. Dr. Mukherjee was formerly a principal in IBM, leading the ERP data warehousing area, and brings leadership expertise from consulting firms Accenture and KPMG. Dr. Mukherjee holds an M.S. from the Indian Institute of Technology (Kanpur) and a Ph.D. from Princeton University.

name: Ken Piddington
title: Vice President & Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Ken Piddington is currently serving as the VP & CIO of U.S. Silica Company, where he applies his extensive experience in technology and business operations to drive profitable business growth and transformation. In addition to this role, Ken holds the position of Faculty Member at IANS, where he imparts his knowledge and expertise to future leaders in the field. He also serves as the President, CIO, and Advisor at Compass42, LLC, further showcasing his leadership and advisory roles in the industry. Prior to this, Ken was the CIO of SGR Energy, Inc., where he played a pivotal role in defining and executing the company's business technology strategy and contributed to the company's M&A strategy team. Ken's career is marked by a legacy of success in setting strategic visions, scaling organizations, and driving execution for high-performing businesses. He is also recognized as an award-winning thought leader and published author, highly regarded for his industry contributions, and passionate about talent development and customer satisfaction. Piddington is the former CIO at Global Partners LP, a Fortune 500 midstream logistics and energy products marketing company, where he was responsible for leading, developing and implementing the business technology strategy during four years of unprecedented growth where the company revenues grew from $6 billion to $20 billion. This included the successful integration of multiple acquisitions and the implementation of supply chain technologies necessary to support dramatic geographic and line of business expansion. Piddington is the President of the Advisory Board for the Energy Institute High School, chairs the Technology Committee for Stages Repertory Theatre, and is a board member at HITS Theatre. Ken is also an Advisory Board member for the CIO Executive Council and a mentor in the Pathways program. He is recognized as an industry thought leader, is a regular speaker at business and association conferences, and has been featured in multiple media publications. He has been named a Computerworld Premier 100 Technology leader, is a CIO100 award winner, and has twice been recognized as an InformationWeek 500 Top Technology Innovator. Piddington holds a B.S. in Management from Northeastern University and attended Graceland College where he played football and baseball. Today, Ken makes Houston, Texas his home.

name: Mark Polansky
title: Operating Advisor
bio: Mark Polansky currently holds a diverse range of positions, serving as an Operating Advisor at RECOGNIZE, the Executive Director of TLDC at Women Leaders In Data & AI (WLDA), and as the Founder/CEO & Managing Consultant at Outcome Solutions LLC. In addition, Mark was Senior Partner in Korn Ferry's Technology Officers Executive Search Practice, a role he has successfully headed for more than twelve years. He has been based in the firm's New York office since joining Korn Ferry in 1997. Mark extensively recruits chief information officers, chief technology officers, chief information security officers, senior digital, data and transformation executives and other senior IT leadership talent across various sectors including Industrial, Consumer, Life Sciences, Technology, Higher Education and Not-for-Profit. His consultative approach to search encompasses talent strategy, organization design, search process and diversity recruiting. Mark’s clients range from the Fortune 100 to smaller high-growth companies as well as prestigious public and private universities, and mission-driven organizations. Mark frequently addresses conferences and writes on information technology and career management topics. He is the creator of the “Executive Career Counsel” column in CIO Magazine and CIO.com. He has served on the Advisory Boards of The Information Technology Senior Management Forum (ITSMF), the national organization dedicated to fostering executive talent among African-American IT professionals; HITEC, the Hispanic Information Technology Executive Council; and Columbia University’s Executive Technology Management advanced degree program. Mark holds a master’s degree in Computer Science from Pratt Institute and a bachelor’s degree in Mathematics and Electrical Engineering from Union College.

name: Landon Pollack
title: Executive Director
bio: Landon Pollack is the CEO of Champz Health and the Executive Director of Path2Proximity. Pollack has been many things: athlete; entrepreneur; art director; herpetologist; inventor; social activist; investor and sports manager. By crossing many traditional frontiers during his 30-year career, he has developed a one-of-a-kind, 360-degree view of business.
