His foundation for success was established during his time training to play professional baseball. Since then, Pollack has been a founder of, or founding investor in, more than two dozen companies. Before reaching 20, Pollack had started four businesses, earning his first million dollars at age 13 selling sports memorabilia. In the ensuing 20 years, his ventures have included: a beverage company (Juice Bowl- acquired by Dole/Pepsi ); a multi-media company (ClubCom- acquired by Amer Group Fitness Equipment business, Precor Inc.); a robotically-controlled exercise system (Intelligent Health); a coaching and performance company (Personalized Lifestyle Programs- private sale); health clubs (Firm Fitness), a cable company (Cable Satisfaction- IPO); a non-alcoholic beverage conglomerate (Liboza Holdings, Inc.); a chain of yoga studios (Vitalize Studios); a telecom company (Uniconnexions); a social networking company (WeMeUs); a digital music company (Playvuu) and two animal protection organizations (StubbyDog and Zoe Nature). Pollack has been a consultant to some of the most recognizable brands in the world and to some of the most innovative start-ups. He has counseled a diverse set of companies, providing human capital, corporate development, marketing and organizational development services. Additionally, Pollack serves as an advisor to GlobalGiving.org, Harvard Medical School (The Division of Sleep Medicine), Hernia Help, Astia, and Coalition to Transform Advanced Care (C-TAC). He is also a mentor at Springboard Enterprises and Unreasonable Institute.

name: Mark Popolano
title: Senior Executive Technology Consultant (freelance)
bio: Mark Popolano, formerly the SVP, CTO, and Managing Director of Business Innovation and Offer Management at Claro Enterprise Solutions, currently serves as an Executive Technology Advisor. In this role, he specializes in providing guidance to start-ups and enterprise-size organizations across a range of industries. Mark's expertise lies in helping these organizations craft strategic plans, secure funding, and develop innovative solutions that foster revenue growth and operational optimization. His knowledge spans various domains, including green initiatives, oil and gas extraction, financial and AI models, crypto technologies, and web 3.0 platforms. He also facilitates connections with investors and assists with business development, making him a valuable resource for organizations dealing with complex challenges and opportunities. Mark Popolano has a distinguished career history, having held C-suite executive positions, consultative partner roles, sales and technical leadership positions, and solution architecture roles. His experience encompasses a wide array of verticals, including Government, Financial Services, Insurance, Publishing, Management Consulting, Telecommunications, and Managed Services. Notably, he has managed global professional organizations with internal budgets exceeding $2.5 billion. As a thought leader, Mr. Popolano has authored articles such as "Demystifying the Cloud" and has been a speaker on panels and major events. He has been quoted and interviewed by various publications during his tenure on Wall Street and actively participates in customer advisory and technical boards. He advocates for the use of social media to express his opinions and offers proactive solutions to pertinent issues in the realms of business, society, and technology. Throughout his career, Mark Popolano has played a crucial role in digitally transforming major organizations. He has accomplished this by enhancing the customer experience, optimizing and transforming operational processes, and developing innovative business models with business leadership. He has collaborated with CEOs, CFOs, CIOs, and their executive teams in shaping and executing global and divisional business digital strategies. These collaborative efforts have enabled companies to embrace technologies such as mobile, IoT, cognitive analytics, big data, cloud applications, and automated data center capabilities, facilitating their journey towards digital transformation.

name: Rafail Portnoy
title: Chief Technology Officer
company: State of NY Metropolitan Transportation Authorit
bio: Rafail Portnoy is the CTO of the State of NY Metropolitan Transportation Authority. Prior to the MTA, Portnoy was the SVP of Technology at the Anti-Defamation League. Before that, he was CIO at Safe Horizon, the largest domestic violence victim’s assistance agency in United States. In this capacity he was responsible for all aspects of Information Technology, overseeing project management, application development, and IT infrastructure support teams. Portnoy has over 18 years of technology experience, with over 10 years of managing complex IT environments. Prior to joining Safe Horizon, Rafail was a founding CIO at The Levin Institute and held a variety of technical and management positions at IBM Corporation. He holds a B.B.A. degree from Pace University and an M.S. from Polytechnic University.

name: Banesh Prabhu
title: CEO
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Banesh Prabhu is the CEO and Chief Strategy Advisor of IntellectDesign Arena’s insurance and data management vertical. He is also a member of IntellectDesign’s Executive Committee, and helps design and manage significant digital transformations for the company’s 200+ clients globally. Prabhu is a Fintech Services veteran and pioneer in Technology & Operations management. He has developed and globally executed strategies in digital transformation and client servicing. Prabhu has managed senior global responsibilities in Operations, Distribution and Technology for over 35 years, of which 23 years were in Citibank where he was responsible for servicing clients in 55+ international markets directly managing several billion in expenses p.a. During these years, he pioneered the creation of several global Centers of Excellence for Operations and Technology that included implementing significant digital services. He was also a member of Citigroup Global Consumer Planning Group, Citigroup Global Information Technology Council and Citigroup Global Operations Council. After Citibank, Prabhu worked for Siam Commercial Bank, one of Thailand’s leading universal banking groups. There, he was a member of the Executive Committee and head of the Technology & Operations Group. After Siam Commercial Bank, Prabhu went to Boston Consulting Group (BCG) as a Senior Advisor. Prabhu is a Fintech/IOT Investor and Partner in Green Shores Capital, a U.K. FCA approved fund. Green Shores Capital is a co-investment growth fund typically investing in Pre-Series A and Series A firms. He is also an Investor and Member of the Excom of GlobalLinker, a business networking platform solution for SMEs and startups. Prabhu has received several awards and has appeared as a speaker and panelist at major events globally.

name: Gregor Purdy
title: Executive Director of Engineering
bio: Gregor Purdy serves as the Executive Director of Engineering at MGM Resorts International. He is also notable for co-founding Pro.com and holding the position of Chief Technology Officer (CTO) there. Furthermore, he serves as a strategic advisor for Doxxa. Before embarking on his journey with Pro.com, Mr. Purdy made significant contributions at Apple, where he built and led two engineering teams. His work included re-engineering Content Management and Product Management for the Online Store. Notably, Gregor Purdy played a crucial role in leading the implementation of the initial version of the Apple Store iOS app, which, at its launch, became the fastest app to reach a million downloads. He is recognized for presenting twice at WWDC, with one session covering the construction of hybrid native-web apps and another focusing on architecting for server-driven user experiences in apps. His latter talk garnered significant attention, drawing a standing-room-only crowd and necessitating a repeat session for overflow. In the early 2000s, Gregor Purdy was responsible for overseeing Amazon.com engineering efforts for large accounts, including the pioneering work with Target.com, Amazon's first billion-dollar partner on its platform. His leadership was instrumental in directing a team of nearly 50 individuals in executing a substantial re-engineering initiative to transition Target.com to Amazon's new platform. Additionally, Mr. Purdy conducted technical due diligence assessments for potential acquisitions and evaluated the feasibility of implementing enterprise customer solutions. Before his role at Amazon, Gregor Purdy worked as a consultant in the field of Data Warehousing. He provided his expertise to Fortune 50 clients, including Kroger, Nationwide Insurance, Walgreens, and Home Depot. Furthermore, he conducted high-profile research on software and Internet patent prior art. Mr. Purdy is also an accomplished author, having written several books for O’Reilly Media. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering from the University of Michigan. His extensive career journey and accomplishments reflect a rich background in technology, engineering, and leadership.

name: Greg Puschnigg
title: CEO
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Greg Puschnigg is the Founder and CEO of BOSS Controls, a commercial IoT energy management, controls and data analytics and services company for buildings. He holds several patents and has held multiple senior executive positions in leading-edge technology companies including Ciena, AirFiber, Intel and Powercast. As President of Powercast, he launched the world’s first commercial wireless power product with sales to multinational customers and won Best of Show for new emerging technology at the 2007 Consumer Electronics Show. Before that, Puschnigg launched Intel’s first two generations of Centrino, WiFi that is used around the world daily. Puschnigg began his career as an electrical engineering officer in the US Navy, stationed on the USS BOONE (FFG-28), a frigate out of Mayport Florida. He completed two deployments to the Red Sea and Mediterranean Sea in support of UN sanctions in the first Gulf War in Iraq and as part of NATO supporting operations in the break up of Yugoslavia. He then went on to serve in Washington DC at the Office of Naval Intelligence holding TS/SCI clearances. Puschnigg graduated from the United States Naval Academy with a B.S. and received his M.B.A from Loyola College in Maryland.

name: Ramesh Razdan
title: Global CIO
bio: Ramesh Razdan is Bain's Global Chief Technology/Information Officer. He is an accomplished board member, thought leader and operating executive with close to 30 years of experience. He has a strong track record of delivering exceptional results through tech-enabled transformations to drive business growth, profitability, and innovation. He has partnered with CEOs and Boards to assess the impact of technology and to build strategies to evolve businesses and business models. Prior to joining Bain, Ramesh gained significant experience in leading technology organizations for complex global companies in the professional services, technology, and consumer products industries. Ramesh combines strategic thinking and execution skills with exceptional organizational and strategic leadership abilities to deliver business value. He is recognized as a highly strategic executive committed to developing and motivating high-performing teams. Ramesh is recognized as an industry expert, thought leader, and noted public speaker on wide-ranging technology topics, including leadership for the modern era, Cyber Risk, Product Management / Agile, Analytics, and Cloud. Ramesh holds a degree in Electronic Engineering from Pune University.

name: JR Reagan
title: CEO
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, New Business Ventures
bio: Dr. JR Reagan serves as the CEO of IdeaXplorer Global and formerly held the position of Vice Dean at the Endicott College of International Studies at Woosong University in Daejeon, South Korea. He also holds advisory roles and is an executive fellow at Woxen University while serving as adjunct faculty at Northeastern University. With a career spanning over 25 years in Management Consulting across more than 20 countries, he brings extensive real-world global business experience to academia. Notably, Dr. Reagan previously held the role of Global CISO for Deloitte and taught for a decade at the Carey Business School of Johns Hopkins University. His research interests encompass Industry 4.0, Innovation, Big Data & Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, and Cybersecurity. Dr. Reagan holds a Ph.D. in Organizational Leadership from Shenandoah University, an M.S. in Management Information Systems from Bowie State University, and a B.A. in Sociology from The University of the State of New York.

name: Robert W. Reinckens
title: Chief Technology Officer
company: The Cooper Union for the Advancement of Science and Art
bio: Robert Reinckens is the CTO of The Cooper Union. He is also a Founding Partner of RwR Consulting, which focuses on IT strategy and transformation, as well as application portfolio optimization. Reinckens is a former Partner with IBM Global Business Services. He was also previously CIO for Coty’s North American and Latin American operations. At Coty, Reinckens was responsible for all operational systems in the Americas, the corporate data center, as well as all global business intelligence applications. During his 11 years at Coty, he completed the implementation of ERP applications, supply chain planning, warehouse and transportation management, business intelligence and CRM applications. Prior to joining Coty, Reinckens served as a Director in Coopers & Lybrand’s management consulting practice. While at IBM and Coopers & Lybrand, he assisted numerous Fortune 100 clients in developing and implementing IT solutions.

name: John P. Repko
title: EVP & Chief Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: John Repko is EVP and CIO of American International Group. He is the former SVP and CIO of Tyco, where he was responsible for its information technology strategy, systems and services. Before joining Tyco, Repko was the SVP and CIO of Covance, one of the world’s largest drug development services companies. Repko joined Covance in March 2003 as Vice President of Global Applications. In this role, he organized and led the centralization and globalization of application development, application support, and quality control across Covance. He was promoted to CIO in January 2006. Prior to joining Covance, Repko served as Senior Vice president and CIO at GE American Communications, a division of GE Capital. Repko holds and M.S. in Business Administration and Information Technology from Drexel University, and B.S. in Accounting from Villanova University. He is a certified Six Sigma Master Black Belt.

name: Atefeh (Atti) Riazi
title: Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer
bio: Atefeh Riazi is the Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Hearst. She was also the CIO of Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center. Riazi is the former CIO of the United Nations and is the Executive Director of CIOs Without Borders, a global non-for-profit organization focused on using technology and innovation for the good of humanity. In her previous role as Executive Vice President and CIO of the New York City Housing Authority (NYCHA), Riazi directed all IT operations of one of North America’s largest housing agencies. She was charged with bringing innovation and cost containment practices that help advance the mission of the organization while transforming its services and improving the quality and efficiency of the operations. Riazi was recently the Senior Partner and Global Chief Information Officer of Ogilvy and Mather Worldwide, a leading global marketing and communications agency with 497 offices in 125 countries supporting more than 2,300 clients. In her role as CIO, her focus was in driving technology strategy to create efficient business processes across the organization. Throughout her career, she has had a tremendous track record of innovation and successful large-scale deployments allowing for productivity gains and competitive advantage. Riazi was ranked in #49 amongst the top 100 global CIOs. Under Riazi's leadership Ogilvy and Mather has earned a place on InformationWeek's 500 list of innovative IT organizations.Riazi is a graduate of electrical engineering with over 23 years experience managing large organizations, private and public, in the manufacturing, engineering, advertising and transportation sectors. Most recently, she was Vice President and CIO of Technology for MTA New York City Transit responsible for implementing the $1.5B MetroCard in New York City. Prior to that she was the Sr. VP of Manufacturing. Riazi has published many articles and studies. She speaks frequently on issues involving technology, organizations and work. She has delivered keynote addresses and seminars globally and serves on the Board of major financial and marketing organizations.

name: Peter Richards
title: CTO
bio: Peter Richards was the CTO of Cloudreach, one of the leading Cloud Enablement groups that focuses on intelligent public cloud adoption for the Enterprise. Prior to this role, he was the Head of Cloud Operations, Products, Partnerships and Software at the company. Prior to Cloudreach, Richards worked in the financial services industry for the past 25 years. At Bank of America he was the Investment Bank CTO and had core responsibility across the mid-range platform and their extensive network structure. In 2008 at J.P. Morgan, he was responsible for the architecture, engineering, and the appropriate use of the distributed platform, which included servers, storage, database, application server, and application messaging services for the business units of the firm. He was CTO at Bear Stearns. Previously, Richards built and ran the Core Infrastructure Group at Goldman Sachs, where he provided common technology services to the firm across three continents. During his tenure at Goldman (1990-2004), he also served as co-head of Enterprise Technology, co-manager of the Equity Derivatives Development Group, and manager of the Japanese Equity Warrant Development Group. Before working in the financial services industry, Richards was in the oil industry, software and military research, and also technology consulting. Richards holds a Physics degree from the University of Bristol.

name: Jaap Van Riel
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: Jaap van Riel is the Chief Technology Officer at KnitWell Group, a PE owned holding company for iconic American apparel brands Ann Taylor, Chico's, Haven Well Within, Lane Bryant, LOFT, Soma, Talbots, WHBM (White House Black Market). KnitWell operates online and in ~3000 stores, and generates over $6 billion in annual sales. In his role Jaap leads all aspects of Technology. He drives the Digital & Data Transformation and Technology Innovation agenda across all brands and channels. In December 2020, Jaap was part of the executive team responsible for restructuring the remaining brands of Ascena Retail Group, acquired by Sycamore Partners during the bankruptcy process. Since that time, the team led the brands to double-digit year-over-year EBITDA growth, while transforming the business to financial health and stability. These brands, together with Talbots and Haven Well Within, where Jaap was the Chief Information Officer since 2018, became one company in August 2023 under the name KnitWell Group. In 2024 KnitWell Group acquired Chico's FAS (Chico's, Soma, White House Black Market). Prior, Jaap led the global business transformation at Tiffany & Co, delivering Omni Channel solutions for Tiffany customers across the world, as well as modernizing the Tiffany global technology landscape. Jaap held various other IT Leadership and Transformation roles at Avon and Wrigley. Jaap has extensive international experience. He lived and worked in 6 different countries and led global teams across 5 continents. Earlier in his career Jaap worked at Deloitte Consulting both in Europe and the United States. Jaap holds an Executive MBA from the Australian Graduate School of Management, as well as a BSc in Chemical Engineering from The Hague University of Applied Sciences.

name: Debra Robinson
title: SVP/CIO Oracle Technology & Operations
bio: Debra C. Robinson is the SVP of CIO Oracle Technology & Operations at Hearst. In Fall 2008, she was named SVP and CIO of Hearst. In this newly created position, Robinson was responsible for leading and coordinating IT initiatives and operations across all Hearst Magazines business units, which include 15 U.S. consumer magazine titles; Hearst Magazines Digital Media; CDS Global, the Hearst-owned international data management company that provides customized fulfillment services to publishers and direct marketers; Periodical Publishers’ Service Bureau (PPSB), the Hearst-owned circulation and subscription services company; and the National Magazine Company Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hearst Magazines in the U.K. Prior to being appointed CIO, Robinson was VP of Technology, Production and Digital Media for Hearst Magazines. She was instrumental in developing and organizing the newly-created department. She was the lead person for developing the technology and production needed to create and implement Hearst Magazines Digital Strategy. Before joining Hearst, Robinson was SVP and CIO at Primedia for five years. She also held executive positions at CMP Media, CVS Pharmacy, Delaware North Companies and Aramark. Robinson is a graduate of the Wharton School and also has an M.S. from the University of Pennsylvania.

name: Jorge Rodriguez
title: Chief Executive Officer
location: Salt Lake City, UT, United States
bio: Jorge Rodriguez currently serves as the CEO of Sorenson Communications. Before assuming this role, he held the position of President & CEO of Claro Enterprise Solutions. Prior to his role at Claro, Rodriguez served as the President & CEO of Telmex USA and Telvista Inc. In this capacity, he also held the senior officer position overseeing the firm's global enterprise segment. He was responsible for managing international product and BPO organizations and played a pivotal role in shaping the company's global strategy. Additionally, he served on multiple company boards. With nearly 25 years of experience in telecommunications, management, and international business, Rodriguez has made significant contributions to the field. Before joining Telmex, he held various roles at AT&T and AT&T Latin America. His achievements at AT&T and AT&T Latin America included launching startup operations across Latin America, establishing a fully integrated regional operation in support of Global Clients, and serving as a Board Member of several AT&T Latin America operating companies. Jorge Rodriguez's educational background includes holding an M.Sc. in Technology Management from Columbia University, an M.B.A. from RIT (Rochester Institute of Technology), completing the Keenan Flagler Advanced Management Program at UNC Chapel Hill, and earning a B.A. in Biology/Molecular Biology from Colgate University. His extensive experience and educational qualifications reflect his significant impact in the telecommunications and business sectors.

name: Richard Ross
title: Managing Partner
bio: Richard Ross currently serves as the Managing Partner of Decision Strategies Group. Prior to his role with Decision Strategies Group, he held the position of Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Senior Vice President of IT at Atlas Air until his recent retirement. In this capacity, he was responsible for overseeing Atlas Air's global business systems, IT operations, general IT controls, emerging technologies, innovation, cybersecurity, and the cybersecurity aspects of the relationships between Atlas, its customers, and its partners, including the Federal government. With over 40 years of experience in Information Technology (IT) spanning various industries, Richard has a well-documented track record of establishing robust, high-performing, and compliant operations in public companies, as well as driving success in privately funded startups. His extensive board experience includes serving as Chairman for an early-stage tech startup and offering advisory services to boards and management teams in multiple sectors, including Transportation, Healthcare, and Media. He also represented IT and Cybersecurity matters to the Atlas board. Richard's previous roles encompass serving as the first global CIO for Amerada Hess, founding Decision Strategies Group, a strategy and operations consulting firm, and holding the position of VP of IT for Salomon Brothers in London and Tokyo. He also served as Research Director for Gartner Group in its flagship Industry Service, worked as an engineer for Wang Labs and IBM, and functioned as a general business consultant for McKinsey & Company and Index. His extensive professional experience covers areas such as strategic restructuring, turnaround strategies, mergers and acquisitions, supply chain management, profitability improvement through reengineering and automation, customer and product line profitability, and the implementation and monitoring of corporate cybersecurity programs. Richard has been an active participant in various industry groups, including his role as co-chairman of the National Defense Transportation Association's (NDTA) Cybersecurity Committee, involvement with the Defense Industrial Base (DIB), contributions to the Chamber of Commerce's Digital Security Working Group, participation in Airlines for America's Cybersecurity Committee, and engagement with the Department of Homeland Security's ADIAC. His accomplishments include being recognized as Innovative CIO of the Year by Salomon Smith Barney in 2001 and earning recognition from CIO magazine in 2002 for innovation related to seismic processing within his organization. Richard Ross's academic background comprises a B.S.E.E. in Electrical Engineering/Biomedical Engineering from Washington University in St. Louis and an M.S.E.E. with a dual major in Computer Science and Electrical Engineering from Northeastern University.

name: Trisha Rozas
title: Managing Director - Technology Strategy & Advisory
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Trisha Rozas is Managing Director, Strategy & Consulting at Accenture. Ms. Rozas is a strategic business and technology executive with over 20 years of expertise providing IT leadership and direction to multi-million dollar enterprises. She has specialized experience in leading the delivery of cloud transformation engagements, ensuring client’s goals are established and met, as well as overseeing the professional services teams performing the work. She recently led a cloud transformation engagement for a healthcare client in New Zealand. With the overarching goal of improving patient outcomes, Ms. Rozas was accountable for ensuring the client leveraged the benefits of the cloud to automate and modernize operations, upskill staff, improve agility, and enhance reliability. Ms. Rozas has worked for leading Fortune 1000 companies across the financial services industry including AIG, Marsh & McLennan, Goldman Sachs and the CIT Group Inc. She is currently a Senior Technology Consulting Executive with a market-wide focus on Cloud Advisory and Transformation. Ms. Rozas is accountable for providing cloud-first, strategic guidance to clients embracing the use of cloud services across their businesses.

name: Monica Ruffo
title: Founder
bio: Monica is a serial entrepreneur known for her ability to lead, grow and inspire exceptionally innovative purpose-driven companies. A graduate of McGill University with certificates from Harvard Business School, Columbia and Cornell Universities, Monica began her career at Canada’s largest communications agency, working with some of the world’s most coveted brands. She then co-founded Amuse, an agency that was acquired three years later by Interpublic Group where she was named Canadian CEO. Under her leadership, the company earned multiple awards and was named TOP 25 smartest creative agencies in the world. At the height of her career, her life took an unexpected turn when she was diagnosed with a very aggressive form of breast cancer. Throughout her 15 months of cancer treatment, she uncovered what she felt was a huge gap in the market: most “natural” health products were in fact not natural at all. Driven to change this, she completed a Certificate in Plant-Based Nutrition from Cornell University and founded the Well Told Company, a company that developed completely clean and highly efficacious supplements from organic plants and nothing else. Four short years later, the company had grown to 21,000 points of distribution across Canada and the US and IPO-ed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. The company was later acquired, and Monica exited in 2023 only to go on and found Originarium, an investment fund and advisory to start-ups. She is also currently the executive Chairwoman of Flare Companion, an AI start-up, and she is on the expert advisory panel of McGill University’s Desautels School of Management where is she also a regular guest lecturer on entrepreneurship. Throughout her career she has been repeatedly recognized, receiving over 250 international awards including Cannes Lions and was named TD Bank’s Top 25 Game Changing CEOs in 2023. She has also been mentioned and appeared countless times in major press publications including Forbes, the Harvard Business Review, CNN, Good Morning America, Today Show, and others.

name: Michael Saffitz
title: VP of Engineering
focus: C-Level Management, Data Management & Analytics, New Business Ventures
bio: Michael Saffitz, Magnify's VP of Engineering, leads the engineering and applied science teams, leveraging a proven track record in scaling engineering organizations. Formerly the Head of Engineering at Stripe for Reporting and Analytics, he co-founded Apptentive, a Seattle-based startup later acquired by KKR-backed Alchemer. At Apptentive, Saffitz served as CTO, building and leading a diverse Product Engineering team that contributed to over 1 billion SDK installations. Prior to this, he was a Program Manager at Microsoft, overseeing the Windows Phone developer experience. Saffitz holds a B.A. in Computer Science and Mathematical Economics from Pomona College, and an M.B.A. with Honors from the Foster School of Business at the University of Washington. He presently serves as an advisor and board member for Apptentive.

name: Regina Salazar
title: Chief Digital & Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data & Analytics Strategy, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Regina Salazar is a transformational technology executive with over 25 years of progressive leadership experience spanning digital innovation, information systems, product development, and operations across automotive, consumer goods, and metals industries. Regina’s career reflects a trajectory of growth and ability to successfully adapt to new challenges, from aggressive cost optimization to transformational initiatives, leading, motivating, and coordinating cross functional teams and complex projects. Regina cultivated cultures of high collaboration, integrating digital expertise into complex matrixed business operating models and functions, guiding organizations on how to improve processes and navigate the adoption of new AI/LLM technologies through partnership, accountability, governance, focus, design-for-scale, and quantifiable outcomes. Most recently, Regina served as Senior Vice President and Chief Digital & Information Officer at Novelis Inc., the world's leading aluminum producer and recycler, with $18B in annual sales across 44 locations globally. She led digital transformation strategy, chaired the Executive Digital Committee, and served as executive sponsor of the Hispanic and Latino Network. Prior to Novelis, Regina held leadership roles at Whirlpool Corporation as Regional CIO for the $12B North America region and CIO for the Global Product Organization. She spearheaded customer-centric product innovation and connected product strategy for the $24B enterprise, driving digital transformations that elevated consumer ratings from 1.0 to 4.3+ stars across Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Jenn-Air, and Maytag brands. Earlier, she held progressive positions at Rockwell International, Delphi Automotive (Aptiv), and Goodyear. Regina holds a B.S. in Computer Science from UNICAMP (Brazil), an M.S. in Science Administration from Central Michigan University and an Executive Certificate in Digital Transformation from MIT. She is a DDN boardroom-certified Qualified Technology Expert. Regina is a recipient of the Automotive Hall of Fame Young Leadership Award in recognition of her leadership and contributions to the automotive industry. She and her team have received the IDG CIO Magazine Digital Edge 50 Award in 2017 and 2018. In 2018, she was a finalist of the Women in Tech Industry Leadership Award sponsored by CGT. In 2023 and 2024, she was a finalist of the prestigious Inspire CIO GeorgiaCIO Orbie Award, and in 2024 she was recipient of the HMG Global Leadership Institute Award. She continues to be heavily engaged with the industry and supporting leadership development and diversity teams: currently a member of the GeorgiaCIO advisory board and a mentor for the InspireCIO Next Generation Leadership Program, member of the Atlanta Women in Technology (WIT) advisory board, a member the Society for Information Management (SIM) - Atlanta Chapter and a faculty member for the World 50 Organization.

name: Joseph Santamaria
title: Director, WW Energy and Utilities Solution Architecture
bio: Joseph Santamaria is the Director of Customer Solutions & Migrations at Amazon Web Services. Prior to Amazon Web Services, Santamaria was the CIO an VP of IT for PSEG Services Corporation. In this role he was responsible for leading, directing, planning and managing all activities relating to the company’s information technology needs and for ensuring innovative, reliable, timely, cost-effective, and secure delivery of information technology to all areas of PSEG. Santamaria previously served as CIO at UIL Holdings Corporation, an electric and gas utility in New England. While at UIL, he successfully integrated IT activities of a major acquisition, incorporated SCADA support into IT, established a best-in-class cyber security framework, and led the company to receive the 2011 SAP Best Run Utility Award for excellence in business transformation. Prior to his role at UIL, Santamaria worked at Pitney Bowes in several capacities, including global Vice President for enterprise business applications. Previously, he was a Principal Consultant at Pricewater house Coopers, managing commercial and delivery aspects of multiple business transformation projects. With 15 years of experience in global Fortune 500 companies and an extensive technology background, Santamaria has successfully managed business process transformation, strategic planning, and enterprise application development. Santamaria holds an M.B.A. from ESADE, in Barcelona, Spain; and an M.Sc. in Applied and Industrial Physics from the University of Barcelona, Spain.

name: Mauricio Santos
title: Information Technology Director - CIO
bio: Mauricio Santos is the Director, IT & Digital Transformation - CIO of Arca Continental. Prior to Arca, Santos was the CIO of Heineken Mexico, the largest operating company in the Heineken group. Based in Monterrey, Mexico, he led the IT digital transformation strategy and business alignment for the 16,000+ employee division. Before joining Heineken, he led the IT Management function at Whirlpool Mexico, commercial and manufacturing division of the appliance corporation based in Benton Harbor, Michigan. Before Heineken, Santos held several roles at Neoris Consulting, one of the largest IT consulting firms in Latin America. From 2005 to 2008 he was dedicated to large SAP implementations for CEMEX, in more than 15 countries in Europe. Later in 2008, he helped Neoris to develop the Nearshore Services business, managing contracts with global firms such as AMD, Lowes, ATS, and Ryder Logistics. Santos has written several articles about application services management, and how to transform a traditional IT operation into an optimized service-based model. He received his bachelor’s degree from the Monterrey Institute of Technology (ITESM) and his master’s in Business Management at the IPADE Business School. Santos completed his most recent technology management studies during 2018, at IMD and EPFL, in Switzerland.

name: Lenny Savino
title: Consulting Advisory
bio: Lenny Savino is the retired VP of Infrastructure Coordination Management in Global Business and Technology Solutions at Prudential. In this role he was responsible for managing large-scale technology implementations that require end-to-end coordination and relationship management with Prudential’s businesses. Savino joined Prudential in 1984. Throughout his career with the company, he held several positions in IT engineering and operations and management. His experience includes managing database, mainframe engineering, business continuation and audit functions. Savino has also worked on large-scale technology projects with international impact, such as conducting an infrastructure review of Prudential’s international insurance and investments businesses in Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Italy, Poland, Japan, Korea, and Taiwan. He was also responsible for managing the overall U.S. project resources related to the data center migration for Prudential of Japan and Gibraltar Life Insurance companies. This project involved procuring a data center for production and business continuation, establishing Prudential Systems Japan Limited as a legal entity, creating a network, building the technology infrastructure and migrating all business applications into the data center. Lenny Savino, a seasoned IT professional, actively contributes to the Community Foodbank of New Jersey since January 2018. As a mentor to junior IT talent, he's dedicated to nurturing the next generation of professionals and supporting community development. Savino holds a B.S. in Computer Science from Montclair State University and an M.B.A. in Corporate Finance from Pace University.

name: Renzo Scacco
title: Managing Director
focus: Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Renzo Scacco is an experienced senior executive with a track record of establishing and growing businesses locally and internationally across a range of sectors. He recently founded NXT Venture Collective, which works with executives and early-stage startup teams looking to create their own business – be it tech, product or service based – and their own sustainable consulting practice. Scacco’s background covers practice management and general management roles in Tier 1 consulting firms across strategy, operational performance improvement and branding. He also has experience in industry roles managing business lines in Australia, Asia and Europe across the FMCG, Healthcare and Education sectors. Scacco established an entrepreneurial platform for a major Australian university covering face-to-face and online practice-based education, a comprehensive startup program and pre-seed fund and links with industry and government.

name: Mahesh Shah
title: Chief Executive Officer
bio: Mahesh Shah is currently serving as the Chief Executive Officer at CoreTrust and has previously held the position of Board Member at RB Global Inc. He has also been the EVP, Chief Product and Technology Officer of CDK Global. Prior to CDK Global, he served as DXC Technology’s (NYSE: DXC) SVP and offering general manager of Application Services. Previously, Shah was general manager and VP, Acquisition and Divestiture, IT Consulting Services at Hewlett Packard Enterprise. In this role, he established a new business to deliver consulting and technology services to assist clients in executing the IT aspects of an acquisition or divestiture. Mr. Shah was responsible for all aspects of the startup, which experienced faster growth in revenues and margins than any business in HPE’s services portfolio. Prior to that, Shah served as CIO, Enterprise Services and Software and IT separation leader for HPE. In this role, he managed the technology required to run enterprise services and software. He managed all aspects of separating HP’s business applications into two Fortune 50 companies (Hewlett Packard Enterprise and HP Inc.). Other roles at HP included VP, Product R&D and IT; executive director, Security Product Management and Development; and several other executive roles in IT automation and cloud engineering. Shah was also a senior consultant for IBM Global Services, where he managed large-scale technology projects for many Fortune 100 companies. He serves on the board of advisors for several technology startups. Shah holds an M.B.A. from Northwestern University, J. L. Kellogg School of Management, and a B.S. in Commerce, Management Information Systems, from Concordia University, Montreal, Canada.

name: Dino Shiatis
title: Senior Banker
company: EBRD (European Bank for Reconstruction and Development)
bio: Constantinos Shiatis joined the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) in 2006. Since 2017, he has been a Senior Banker, in the Telecommunications, Media, and Technology team. An MBA-educated multilingual international development banker, he focuses on the Telecommunications, Media, and Technology sector as well as emerging markets. Dino is adept at leading complete project lifecycle from origination through to execution and exit. He’s also skilled at managing internal and external clients and collaborating with multiple stakeholders on complex impact-related challenges. A dedicated mentor of team talent, he’s well known for long-term focus and collegiality. In addition to leading debt and equity investments, Dino has played a key role in formulating and implementing EBRD's digital strategy. He has also worked closely with the EU ensuring that EBRD became an implementing partner for deploying EU development funding in the telecommunications and technology sector both within and outside the EU member states. Dino has an law degree from Oxford University and an MBA from INSEAD, France.

name: James Shira
title: US Chief IT Officer
bio: James Shira is the Global and US Chief Information & Technology Officer of PwC. Shira has made a significant impact in transforming the firm’s security landscape since joining the organization in 2015. The role forms part of the Global and US Leadership teams and is responsible for all aspects of technology across PwC. Prior to PwC, Shira was Group CISO at Zurich Insurance Group where he transformed Zurich's information security posture, developing new and innovative solutions and capabilities, and working to enable Zurich employees to work in new and modern ways while remaining secure. Prior to Zurich, James was the Chief Security Officer for American General Financial Services (AGFS), a subsidiary of AIG, and also held the position of America’s CISO for Bank Julius Baer.

name: Larry Sikon
title: Partner
bio: Larry Sikon is a Partner in the New York practice of Fortium Partners. He brings more than 30 years of executive-level management experience in financial services and information technology, with a proven track record of leading large application development groups and successful project delivery. Most recently, Sikon was senior vice president of home office and corporate systems development for LPL Financial, the nation’s largest independent broker-dealer, where he managed a group of 100 developers and a large number of international contractors in the development of in-house and corporate applications that supported over 12,500 financial advisors. Before LPL, he was SVP for Union Bank, where he was a member of the executive management team responsible for the global markets and wealth management business during a four year, $200 million project to deploy an integrated banking platform. Prior to Union Bank, Sikon was the CIO of Thomas Weisel Partners, where he supported its transition from a four-office private U.S. firm to a 15-city public company located in five countries. Sikon has also held senior technology leadership roles as CIO and COO of Senterprise (a software product firm), CTO of Montgomery Securities and VP of branch office systems development for Merrill Lynch and Co., Inc. He was also the CIO for DHL Worldwide Express for six years, and the VP and director of MIS for Chase’s Institutional Banking Group. Sikon holds an M.B.A. in Finance from Fordham University and a B.S. in Mathematics from Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Harry Silver
title: Senior Fellow for Management / Senior Research Scholar / Adjunct Professor
company: Columbia University - School of International and Public Affairs
bio: Harry Silver has been a Senior Fellow and Adjunct Professor/Senior Research Scholar in the Faculty of International and Public Affairs (SIPA) at Columbia University since 2013. His role at SIPA includes teaching, extensive student career coaching, networking advice, resume preparation and the facilitation of several related workshops. At the request of the Dean, he also engages in a broader set of supportive activities including the admissions, alumni mentoring and professional development processes. Silver teaches a course built upon his significant experience in several organizations — ‘Leading and Sustaining Change’ — that seeks to equip students with tools and skills to lead change efforts during their careers in the public, non-profit and social sectors. He plans to deliver this course in an online format to a group of graduate students aiming for management careers (Social Enterprise Administration, or SEA Program) at the CU School of Social Work. At SIPA, Silver serves as faculty advisor for capstone course projects and several student independent study courses. Silver also mentors CU students enrolled in the Executive Masters of Science in Technology Management program, particularly with a public sector focus, within the School of Professional Studies. His mentoring activities extend to military veterans who recently left active duty within the American Corporate Partners program and also to weekly reading sessions with a student at neighboring P.S. 36 within the Read Ahead program. Formerly a Managing Director with Goldman Sachs, Silver served during 2012-13 as a Senior Advisor/Loan Executive with the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. At the DHS, he suggested potential areas of improvement in public-private collaboration related to critical infrastructure and provided independent and critical assessments of policies, procedures and tools impacting the private sector. During a career spanning four decades at Goldman, Silver led a number of global operational and risk management initiatives in the areas of investment banking, general (corporate) services, human capital management, compliance operations, crisis management, global security, and vendor risk management. Silver has also held positions as: SVP and Chief Administrative Officer at the (internet start-up) Wit Soundview Group; Executive Director at Cravath, Swaine and Moore; Vice President/Chief of Staff to the President of Personal and Banking Services at Chemical Bank; Principal with the consulting firm Booz, Allen and Hamilton; and Auditor at Arthur Andersen & Co. Silver holds a B.S. with honors in Accounting from Pennsylvania State University and an M.B.A. from Columbia University. He is a certified public accountant and formerly a FINRA registered supervisor.

name: Joe Simon
title: Chief Information & Technology Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Joe Simon currently holds the position of Chief Information and Technology Officer at Entain. His professional journey reflects an impressive track record in technology leadership and innovation: Prior to joining Entain, Mr. Simon served as an Executive in Residence at Activate Consulting, demonstrating his commitment to advancing technology and business strategies. Before his role at Activate Consulting, he was the Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Vice Media, a prominent media company known for its digital content and journalism. His role at Vice Media would have involved overseeing technology initiatives and innovation within the organization. Preceding his tenure at Vice Media, Mr. Simon took on the role of Chief Operating Officer (COO) at Encompass Digital Media, where he managed global operations related to the processing and distribution of high-value content for television, web, and mobile platforms. His responsibilities included the management of engineering and operational resources, financial management, and the delivery of numerous video streams to multiple content distribution networks. Additionally, Mr. Simon played a key role in the development and rollout of digital products at Encompass. Before his time at Encompass, Joe Simon served as the Chief Technology Officer for Univision Communications. In this capacity, he led digital technology initiatives related to web, mobile, and over-the-top (OTT) consumer platforms. His responsibilities included simplifying web platforms by migrating to open-source solutions and adopting Agile methodologies to reduce costs and time to market. He also streamlined and automated the video supply chain to enhance flexibility in delivering OTT services. Prior to Univision, Mr. Simon held the role of Chief Technology Officer at Condé Nast. In this position, he oversaw technology operations for Condé Nast's various media brands, internet properties, and other entities. He was responsible for developing the company's digital strategy and managing profit and loss (P&L) for digital businesses. He successfully unified technology groups to drive impactful initiatives and migrated IT services and web platforms to Amazon Web Services. Before joining Condé Nast, Joe Simon served as the Chief Information Officer (CIO) for Viacom. In this role, he led technology and operations for Viacom's divisions, including MTV Networks, MTVN International, BET Networks, Paramount, and Dreamworks. He played a pivotal role in managing technology across mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures while fostering innovation and revenue growth. His contributions included driving advertising optimization and global content distribution and introducing pioneering technologies in areas like online video, global asset management, content digitization, and mobile video. During his tenure at Viacom, Mr. Simon led the development of new businesses and platforms, including casual games and interactive television, and integrated acquisitions such as Neopets and AtomShockwave to expand Viacom's presence in gaming. Prior to his time at Viacom, Joe Simon spent nine years as a principal at Booz, Allen and Hamilton, specializing in the media, entertainment, and communications sectors. Joe Simon's career showcases his extensive experience in technology, innovation, and business strategy across various prominent organizations in the media and entertainment industry.

name: Piyush Singh
title: Independent Director
bio: Piyush Singh is the Founder and CEO of Terrene Labs, a company that was recently acquired by FRISS. Additionally, he serves as an Independent Director for Signal Mutual. Terrene Labs is at the forefront of leading companies in disrupting the small business commercial insurance industry. The company's mission is to provide an optimal balance, delivering a delightful experience for agents, brokers, and bankers, coupled with high veracity application data and additional risk evaluation data for insurance carriers. The ultimate result is the future of risk evaluation, offering a pleasant experience for users while enabling insurance carriers and banks to enhance their risk selection and pricing capabilities. Notably, Terrene was featured as one of the "17 startups to watch for 2017" within a year of its founding. Piyush Singh brings a wealth of experience from his successful corporate career, having held positions such as Director, Senior Vice-President, and Chief Information Officer at Great American Insurance Group, among others. His professional journey also includes consulting roles at companies like Price Waterhouse Coopers, ANZ Bank, and Citicorp. Singh is recognized as a business-savvy executive and industry thought leader who excels in preparing for an uncertain future. He possesses the remarkable ability to connect disparate concepts and ideas within and outside the insurance industry, paving the way for emerging patterns. Singh is known for his analytical skills and the capacity to dissect complex problems into effective solutions and business opportunities by leveraging process and technology. His dedication to industry transformation led him to leave a successful corporate career. His achievements in the insurance and technology sectors include receiving the "Elite 8 CIO" award from Insurance and Technology in 2005 and 2013, being named the "Top 10 CIO Breakaway Leader" at the Global CIO Executive Summit in 2011, and being recognized as one of the "26 Innovators to Watch" in 2018. In addition to his professional roles, Singh is an Adjunct Professor at the University of Cincinnati and holds a membership emeritus with the National Council of Advisors for the Foster College of Business at Bradley University. He has also served as a Trustee for Adler University and is the President and founder of the charitable organization KARNA, which is involved in disaster relief work, runs an after-school support system for approximately 175 children, operates an award-winning Karate school, and recently built a 1000-student auditorium in a remote graduate college. Singh has previously served as a Board member of IASA and is a member of the PCI IT Council, Chair of UC's IS Advisory Board, Chair of EDGE (a CA User Group), and Chair of the SYNON User Group. Beyond his professional and philanthropic endeavors, Piyush Singh has a passion for travel and photography. He has explored all seven continents and successfully trekked to the summit of Mt. Kilimanjaro.

name: Mary Sobiechowski
title: Partner, Interim CIO, Enterprise Technology Strategist
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Mary Sobiechowski is a Partner, Interim CIO & Enterprise Technology Strategist in the New York practice of Fortium Partners. Sobiechowski brings more than 20 years of technology leadership experience as a CIO and global IT transformation leader who drives organic growth and builds shareholder value through the creative use of technology resources. She is an enthusiastic technology pacesetter and transformation pioneer who boldly defines the strategic direction of product, process, and innovation. Sobiechowski is recognized as one of the top 100 social CIOs on Huffington Post’s Tech blog for her widespread social media influence and collaboration. Prior to joining Fortium, Sobiechowski was the CIO of the American Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (ASPCA), where she oversaw a $7 million annual budget for a team of 29 IT staff who supported 880 people in 9 diverse locations, plus over 100 remote staff, including a hospital and a poison control center. Within months of joining the ASPCA, Sobiechowski had identified opportunities to improve the business by adopting better platforms for donor and grant programs, creating new workflows, reducing infrastructure costs, improving cybersecurity, training team members on analyzing data from legacy systems and serving as executive sponsor for the corporate Workday implementation. Before the ASPCA, Sobiechowski served for 15 years in technology leadership roles for various companies owned by WPP, one of the largest holding companies of global advertising, marketing, and media companies. She was the first CIO of Kantar Health, a global leader in research and brand equity for the healthcare industry, where she helped guide the technology transformation of the merger of five global companies into a single $250 million global consultancy and market insights organization. She was also the CIO and CTO for Sudler & Hennessey, the healthcare communications division of Y&R Brands, where she had enterprise responsibility for a $2.2 million global budget for 1,200 people in 45 offices across 20 countries. She was appointed lead of the WPP Creative Technology Special Interest group, a forum of over 100 innovators collaborating to create best practices and synergies for the business. Earlier in her career, Sobiechowski served in consulting roles with clients including Miramax Film Corp., Disney, Talk Media, Ann Taylor, Estee Lauder Inc. and various small and medium-sized businesses. She often speaks at CIO events and mentors at Columbia University’s Masters in IT program. She was also recognized as an HPE Matter “Woman IT Leader to Watch” in 2015. Sobiechowski holds a B.F.A. with distinction from Wayne State University. She is a member of the Society of Information Management (SIM) New York Metro and New Jersey chapters. In her spare time, she is an avid cyclist and yoga instructor.

name: Christopher Soong
title: Chief Information Officer, EVP
bio: "Chris Soong is the Chief Information Officer at Huntington Ingalls Industriesí Technical Solutions Division. Named to this position in August 2021, Mr. Soong oversees all aspects of Information Technology inclusive of enterprise infrastructure, collaboration solutions, application development and data analytics. With 25 years of experience in the IT industry, he has repeatedly driven rapid growth and success for technology-driven corporations. Mr. Soong previously served as Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Alion Science and Technology. He also held executive-level positions at Booz Allen Hamilton and Sprint, where he served on the merger integration team of Sprint and Nextel, a two-year, $35 billion acquisition. Chris Soong holds a B.S. in Civil Engineering from Virginia Tech. He has a Leadership Certificate from the University of Maryland and participated in the CIO Institute at the MIT Sloan School of Management. Mr. Soong received the Corporate ëCIO of the Yearî ORBIE award in 2020, founded the Washington, D.C. chapter of the Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers (SASE) and served as president. He has been recognized as ìOnes to Watchî by CIO Magazine and ìOutstanding 50 Asian Americans in Businessî by Asian American Business Development Center. He volunteers with Lungevity, the National Capital Area chapter of the March of Dimes and the Food Allergy & Anaphylaxis Network (FAAN). He volunteers as a mentor for the Center for Technology Management at Columbia University and as an Alumni Board member to the College of Civil Engineering at Virginia Tech."

name: Ursula Soritsch-Renier
title: Group Chief Digital and Information Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, Media & Entertainment Technology Management, New Business Ventures
bio: Ursula Soritsch-Renier currently serves as the Chief Digital and Information Officer (CDIO) of Saint-Gobain and is a member of the Supervisory Board at Software AG. She is the former Group CIO and Digital Leader of Nokia. Prior to Nokia, Ms. Soritsch-Renier was the Group CIO and Group Digital Leader of Sulzer, a Swiss global manufacturing company. At Sulzer, she focused on leading cross-divisional and cross-functional business teams towards Sulzer’s digital transformation, increasing revenue through value-added services. In addition, she established one global Sulzer IT out of previously decentralized Divisional IT to provide agility and speed to adapt business constantly to market situations. Ms. Soritsch-Renier started her career at a software startup before joining Royal Philips Electronics in 1995. From 1995 until 2009, she held various assignments for Philips in Austria, Belgium, Netherlands and USA in and outside of IT. From 2009 until 2013, she served as Global Head of IT Strategy, Sourcing, Project Management as well as Enterprise Architecture at Novartis in Cambridge, USA before joining Sulzer. Ms. Soritsch-Renier holds an M.S. in Philosophy and a minor in Computer Science from the University of Vienna and a B.S. of WIFI from the College for Economics in Austria. She was awarded Swiss CIO of the year in 2014.

name: Leesa Soulodre
title: Founding General Partner
bio: Leesa Soulodre is the Founding General Partner of R3i Group and the Managing Partner of R3i Capital, a deep-tech cross-border venture capital firm specializing in artificial intelligence and sustainable development. With over 20 years of experience in cutting-edge innovation, technology, and strategy consulting, Leesa is a serial entrepreneur. She has provided advisory services to more than 400 multinationals and startups across 19 sectors in Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Americas. Leesa has successfully led companies from the seed stage to $14 billion USD, facilitating market expansion and raising over $270 million in capital for portfolio companies. Her expertise spans smart cities, cybersecurity, medtech and healthcare, and artificial intelligence. Leesa holds the position of Adjunct Faculty Member at SMU Cox Business School in Strategy and Entrepreneurship. Additionally, she serves as an adjunct faculty member at Singapore Management University, and IE Business School. She is a member of the advisory board for the global Think Tank, The AI Asia Pacific Institute—an independent, interdisciplinary research institute focused on exploring the implications of artificial intelligence. Leesa also holds Board roles on her portfolio companies Viewmind, Stratificare, and Meracle Health. Educationally, Leesa earned a Global Executive MBA from TRIUM (NYU Stern, HEC Paris, and the London School of Economics and Political Science) and a Masters in Management (Marketing) from the University of Southern Queensland. In recognition of her achievements, CIO Magazine named Leesa as one of the Five Most Rising Women Leaders to Watch in 2023, as well as one of the 10 Transformational Business Leaders in 2023. She was also honored with the title of EliteX – 10 Elite Business Women to Watch in 2023 and was included in CIO Times’ Top 5 Empowering Women Leaders to Follow in 2023.

name: Varadarajan Srinivasan
title: Advisory Board Member
bio: Varadarajan Srinivasan serves as a Strategy & Talent Enabler at Rajahrpro. In addition to his role at Rajahrpro, he is the Co-Founder & Mentor at Z & M Foods Pvt Ltd and holds the position of Advisory Board Member at Shades of India. With an impressive career spanning over three decades, Srinivasan has established himself as a Senior Human Resources Leader. He is renowned for his role in cultivating high-performing cultures within major growth brands, driven by his technical business acumen, effective communication, and the implementation of robust development programs. Collaborating closely with senior management, Srinivasan plays a pivotal role in orchestrating cultural development, promoting harmonious employee relations, and leading organizational change initiatives that align with corporate goals and strategies. Srinivasan excels in applying contemporary human capital practices to attract and retain high-potential talent, while simultaneously fostering harmonious employee relations across all workforce levels. He is adept at developing actionable strategies and deployable plans that inspire teams to surpass corporate objectives.

name: Robert Stefanic
title: EVP & CIO
bio: Robert Stefanic is the EVP and CIO of DentaQuest. Formerly, Stefanic was the VP and CIO for Sensata Technology, where he was responsible for setting the firm’s global information technology and shared services strategy. He also managed all back office integrations associated with M&A activities. At Sensata, Stefanic was a member of the executive strategy leadership team. Prior to Sensata, Stefanic was a Principal Managing Consultant at Princeton Consulting with a focus on public equity and technology risk management. Prior to Princeton Consulting, he was VP and CIO for Tyco’s Engineered Products and Services where he was responsible for developing overall global information technology strategy as well as implementing technology to improve Tyco’s competitive position globally. Prior to Tyco, Stefanic was CIO for Electrolux Home Products where he headed information technology for the Americas. Stefanic graduated with summa cum laude honors from the University of Massachusetts with a B.S. in Information Technology. He also holds an Executive M.Sc. in Technology Management from Columbia University.

name: Mark Stone
title: System (Chief Information Officer)
bio: Mark A. Stone serves as the System CIO for the Texas A&M University System. The Texas A&M University System is one of the most influential systems of higher education in the nation, with a budget of $3.8 billion. Through a statewide network of 11 universities, seven state agencies, two service units, and a comprehensive health science center, the A&M System educates more than 131,000 students and makes more than 22 million additional educational contacts through service and outreach programs each year. Externally funded research expenditures exceed $820 million and help drive the state’s economy. With more than 28,000 faculty and staff, the A&M System has a physical presence in 250 of the state’s 254 counties and a programmatic presence in every one. Prior to Texas A&M, Stone served as the SVP and CIO of Safety-Kleen Systems, Inc. from August 2009 until December 2012. From May 2005 to February 2009, he served as SVP and CIO of Zale Corporation. Stone was with Zale Corporation for 14 years and worked in IT, Planning, Database Marketing, and Distribution. Prior to Zale Corporation, Stone served as the Director of Financial Operations for the Resolution Trust Corporation. Stone is a Certified Public Accountant. He holds a B.B.A. in Accounting from Baylor University and an M.S. in Divinity from Westminster Theological Seminary.

name: Andrew Stricker
title: DL Architect
bio: Dr. Andrew Stricker is an analyst with Air University’s LeMay Center for Doctrine Development and Education. In this position he conducts research on advances in cognitive sciences and artificial intelligence (AI) for advancing Air Force future concepts and strategy. His research addresses AI-augmented decision making and performance support. Andrew also designs and develops simulations for researching and improving complex problem-solving among teams. Prior to coming to Air University Andrew was Associate Provost for Innovation Through Technology at Vanderbilt University for four years. He also held a faculty appointment as associate professor with the Vanderbilt University Medical School and taught courses in human factors engineering and biomedical informatics. As an associate provost for the university, he was responsible for working with academic, technology and administrative leaders to prioritize, plan, and enable innovations for improving learning, teaching and research. Andrew also spent four years at Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas, creating and then serving as Director of the Cognition and Instructional Technologies Laboratory, and Director of the Knowledge Engineering Complex with the Texas Engineering Extension Service. He also taught statistics and human factors engineering at the United States Air Force Academy for six years and served as a scientist with the Air Force Office of Scientific Research. He retired from the United States Air Force with 28 years of professional experience as an Air Force officer and scientist specializing in systems integration, human-factors engineering and cognitive sciences. His doctoral work was conducted at Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas with post-graduate studies at Yale University, New Haven, Connecticut. He is a member of the American Psychological Association and past member of the Human Factors Society. In 2020 he was elected to the Board of Directors.

name: David Sturm
title: Consulting
bio: David Sturm is a Consultant at David M. Sturm & Associates Consulting. Sturm is the former VP and CIO at the New York Public Library, a private nonprofit in New York City. There, he managed the strategic planning and operational management of technology for one of the most important and technologically advanced library systems in the world. At NYPL, Sturm directed all IT activities, including internal and external systems development, NYPL’s web site, technology support, technical design and engineering, networks, and telecommunications. Sturm also built a successful co-location business at NYPL supporting other New York-based nonprofits with data center, telecommunications, and consulting services, thus helping smaller institutions to leverage their technology budgets while providing NYPL with necessary earned income. Prior to NYPL, Sturm was VP with Citibank’s global information network organization and worked at Citibank for 19 years in operations and technology management. Sturm holds a B.S. in Engineering from the Cooper Union, an M.S. in Engineering from Stanford University, and an M.B.A. in Finance from New York University.

name: Eash Sundaram
title: Operating Executive
bio: Eash Sundaram is currently serving as an Operating Executive at Tailwind Capital, where he brings his expertise to guide and support the company's operations. Additionally, he is a Member of the Board of Directors at SolarWinds and SpiceXpress, where he plays a pivotal role in governance and strategic decision-making. Eash also serves as a Board Member at Concern Worldwide and holds similar roles at other companies. Prior to these current positions, he held the role of EVP, Chief Digital and Technology Officer at JetBlue, where he led the company's digital and technology initiatives. Previously, Sundaram was SVP and CIO at Pall Corporation, a global filtration, separations, and purifications business. Before joining Pall, he served in a variety of management positions with increasing responsibilities in information technology and supply chain with PSS World Medical, i2 Technologies, ALK Technologies, and the Sea Horse Group. Early in his career, he was involved in research and development of efficient intermodal operations of container terminals for the Port Authority. Sundaram holds an M.S. in Transportation Management from the State University of New York Maritime College.

name: Hubert Tardieu
title: Director
company: Gaia-X Association for Data and Cloud (AISBL)
bio: Hubert Tardieu currently serves as an Independent Director of the Gaia-X Association for Data and Cloud (AISBL). He has had a significant role in the organization, having previously been the Interim CEO in 2020 and then the Chairman of the Board in 2021. His current position focuses on managing relationships with governments as an independent board member. Tardieu's professional background includes a 27-year tenure at Sema, later known as SchlumbergerSema, and subsequently Atos. During his time at Atos, he held various executive committee positions, including EVP for Global Telecom, Global Finance, and Global Consulting & Systems Integration. He later served as an advisor to the CEO of Atos, contributing to the formulation of a vision for the world in the next five years. His efforts led to the creation of the Atos Scientific Community in June 2009, a global community consisting of 160 top scientists, engineers, and forward thinkers from diverse backgrounds. The community produces various publications, including a future vision report titled "Journey 2024 - Redefining Enterprise Purpose." They also contribute to whitepapers and blogs, as well as participate in innovation workshops and proof-of-concepts. Additionally, Mr. Tardieu is an author and has written several books on Information System Design Methodology, particularly Merise. His most recent publication, "Deliberately Digital: Rewriting Enterprise DNA for enduring success," co-authored by Tardieu and others, was published in March 2020 by Springer.

name: Alan Thompson
title: Former Senior Managing Partner
bio: Alan Thompson is a Senior Managing Partner at BSG Solutions Group. With a career spanning over 35 years in the banking industry, his most recent role was as Director, President, and CEO of an International Bermudian Bank that maintained offices in 10 countries. Before that, Thompson served as the President & CEO of Riyad Bank in Saudi Arabia. His extensive experience in the sector includes holding various executive positions during his 23 years with First Interstate Bank. His expertise encompasses line operating roles in retail and corporate banking, operations, wealth management, trust and asset management, and payment systems. Thompson's educational background features an M.B.A. from the Wharton School of Finance and a B.A. from Claremont McKenna College.

name: Conal Thompson
title: Chief Technology Officer
bio: Conal Thompson is the CTO of HireRight. Previously, Thompson was the CTO and VP of IT for Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS). Before joining CAS, he was the CTO for the IP & Science unit of Thomson Reuters based in Philadelphia. Prior to that, Thompson led the Bridge Data Network/Bridge Station and Reuters Plus Financial Markets infrastructures and desktop applications as well as a suite of editorial and content systems in St. Louis. Preceding that, he was CEO of a Reuters subsidiary, Equis, in Salt Lake City, appointed after leading their product development function. Conal started his career as a programmer and development manager for another Reuters subsidiary, Equity Systems, in Sydney, Australia. Thompson has an honors degree in Computer Science from the University of Sydney.

name: Doug Torre
title: Chief Information Officer
bio: A healthcare industry veteran with over 20 years of experience, Doug Torre has served in a variety of roles, including Chief Technology Officer of Northwell Health, Chief Information Officer for the Catholic Health System of Buffalo and his current role Chief Information Officer for White Plains Hospital. Mr. Torre’s professional career is focused on advancing digital adoption, transforming information technology, improving human health and advancing knowledge management. Mr. Torre holds a B.S. from Canisius College, and an M.B.A. from the University at Buffalo.

name: Nigel Underwood
title: Director
bio: Nigel Underwood is the Director of Blue Double U and CXO Value. Underwood founded Blue Double U in the 1990’s with his wife Anne, to pursue a portfolio career focused on investing in change. Underwood has held Global CIO positions at a number of world-leading organizations including International Airlines Group (the merger of British Airways and Iberia) and DHL Logistics (Supply Chain Management & Global Freight Forwarding). At DPWN he led the Corporate Business Process Optimization project across the Group and was a member of the DHL Logistics Executive Board leading the IT Integration with Exel where he had previously been CIO for five years. Prior to joining Exel, Underwood was SVP, IT for the Hilton Group. His early career was spent with consumer goods companies Boots, Mars, and Coca-Cola Schweppes Beverages. Underwood holds a B.Sc. in Mathematics from Nottingham University. He is a passionate supporter and Associate Director of Lincoln City Football Club.

name: Siby Vadakekkara
title: Founder & CEO
bio: Siby Vadakekkara is the Founder, Chairman, and CEO of Marlabs, a software services firm headquartered in New Jersey. Under his leadership, the firm grew from a three-employee startup in 1997 to over 2000 employees globally, with over $110 million in revenues. Marlabs is ranked number one in the application development category in CMP media’s Fast Growth 100, and is also rated as one of the “Ten software experts you should know.” The company was also recognized as one of the “50 Best Places to Work in New Jersey.” Mr. Vadakekkara was an Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year finalist and was the sole inductee in the INC Magazine “INC 500 Hall of Fame” in 2006, for having made the INC 500 list five times. He was awarded the Fortune Business Magazine “Best Boss” distinguished finalist award in 2006. Mr. Vadakekkara holds an M.S. in Engineering from Texas Tech University and a B.S. in Engineering from the College of Engineering Trivandrum in India. He has completed various executive education programs, including the YPO Harvard Presidents Program. He is a member of Young Presidents Organization (YPO), Potomac Officers Club, and is a charter member of The Indus Entrepreneurs (TIE). He has mentored several startups as part of the TIE mentoring program and also mentored M.B.A. students at Harvard as part of the YPO/HBS mentoring program.

name: Joseph Vajda
title: Chief Information Officer - CEE Region
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Joseph Vajda is a Retired IT Executive at GE. Prior to GE, he was the CISO at DPD Hungary Kft. In the past he was an IT executive for ALTEO Group and the CIO of Tungsram Group based in Budapest, Hungary. Vajda has substantial experience designing, leading, and implementing a broad range of corporate growth and transformation initiatives to drive growth, performance, profitability, and improve customer interaction. He has diverse experience in both high-growth and turnaround environments with multiple large matrix-based international organizations as well as integration of multi-billion dollar acquisitions and mergers. Vajda started his career with General Electric Company driving automation and IT initiatives in North America. His diverse cultural background provided an excellent foundation to lead global technology teams. Since 1999, he has held various senior executive roles (including CIO, Digital) focusing on Digitization, Simplification and IoT operations, with responsibilities in Asia, Europe, Africa, Middle East, Australia, North America and South America. For the last 6 years, Vajda has focused on marrying computer-programmed automation with digital technologies such as analytics, artificial intelligence (AI), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Since its inception, Industry 4.0 has spread beyond the factory walls to encompass a broader digital transformation that spans processes, functions, and industries. With his international background, Vajda advises companies with technology-driven transformation to help change the way many organizations make sense of information and act upon it to make decisions that could impact operations, deliver greater customer value, and improve performance.
