Vajda holds a B.S. in Computer and Information Science from Cleveland State University in Cleveland, Ohio; an M.B.A. from Tilburg University in the Netherlands; and a Master of International Management from Krannert Graduate School of Management from Purdue University in West Lafayette, Indiana.

name: Nicole Valentine
title: Author
company: Ex: Lerner Publishing, the Highlights Foundation
focus: C-Level Management, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Nicole Valentine is an Educator and Lecturer, as well as an author, speaker, and high-level strategic and technical consultant for companies interested in building a significant presence on the internet. She has worked with internet search engines, advertising agencies, online retailers, major investment banks, entertainment companies, and world news organizations. Her research and due diligence services have been used effectively by established financial institutions and startup companies seeking venture capital. Valentine's most recent full-time role was CTO of Figment.com, an online writing community for teens founded in 2010. The site allows teens to develop as writers and since its launch has shown itself to be an effective marketing and promotional platform for the YA marketplace. It was acquired by Random House in 2013. Prior to forming her consulting company, Valentine helped design, build, and manage CNNfn.com for Turner Broadcasting/Time Warner. Hired as a full-time employee in 1996, she signed on to be part of the core development/concept team in charge of orchestrating the birth of CNNfn.com. Her responsibilities included hiring and managing the technology department, developing the back-end of both the website maintenance and advertising engine, and leading the technical development of one of the most profitable websites on the internet. Valentine also held a position on the CNNfn business development team, acting as the chief technical liaison in third-party business deals with both portal and content sites. In 2000, she accepted the role of CTO for Space.com, where she orchestrated the birth of the leading space news internet agency. Valentine has also used her internet skills to be a driving force in the needlework and craft industry. In 2002, she was the Chief Technology Officer for Craftopia.com. When the firm was successfully sold to HSN.com and moved to Florida, Valentine remained in Philadelphia, re-formed her consulting company, and acted as a temporary CTO for start-ups still in their infancy. Valentine is one of the original members of the New York CTO Club and a graduate of both Fordham University and Vermont College of Fine Arts, where she earned her M.S. in Writing.

name: Garrett Vargas
title: Group Engineering Manager
focus: C-Level Management, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: Garrett Vargas is the Group Engineering Manager at Microsoft. In the past, he served as the Co-Founder & CTO of LegUp. a company focused on keeping working parents in the workforce by helping them find and enroll in quality care for their children. Prior to LegUp, Vargas was the VP of Technology and CTO of CarRentals.com, part of the Expedia Group. CarRentals.com is one of the leading global online travel agencies serving car rentals to leisure travelers. Before CarRentals.com, Vargas led several technology teams at Expedia Group including the Vacation Packages team for the Expedia brand, external partner APIs, and the Rails and Cars lines of business. Prior to joining Expedia Group, he led several different development teams at Microsoft Corporation, ranging from teams working on various components of Windows Phone to incubation projects in the Windows Emerging Markets team prototyping solutions to deliver value-added services to licensed versions of Windows in developing markets. Vargas is an active Startup advisor and angel investor, primarily focusing on companies in the emerging voice technology space and marketplaces. In this capacity, he has helped with initial product/market fit, business strategy, technical design and architectural review, and initial implementations. Vargas holds an M.B.A. from the University of Washington Foster School of Business. He also has an M.Sc. degree in Computer Science from Stanford University and B.S. degrees in Mathematics and Computer Systems Engineering from Stanford.

name: Likhit Wagle
title: Managing Partner Financial Services; IBM Consulting EMEA
bio: Likhit Wagle – Managing Partner, EMEA Financial Services, IBM Consulting Likhit Wagle has advised Banking and Insurance clients world-wide on business transformation programs realising major improvements in customer acquisition, revenue growth and profitability. Based in London, Likhit leads Financial Services for IBM Consulting in EMEA with the remit to grow the business double digit and gain market share. Immediately prior to this, he led IBM’s world- wide banking business, based out of New York. This is IBM’s largest sector by revenue and profits. Under his leadership, the business grew consistently and profitably, and achieved IBM’s mission to be the partner of choice for Digital Re-invention. Key elements of Likhit’s roles included: developing and executing the industry strategy with responsibility for sales and revenues, selection and development of IBM’s client portfolio in the sector, deployment of industry specific solutions across the globe; and thought leadership and external eminence. Nurturing and developing industry talent and expertise has been a key part of his responsibilities: - Clients: development of industry talent within IBM’s strategic clients – including development of the curriculum and key notes to Board level executives. - IBM: Likhit is a member of IBM’s Management Board for the Industry and is also a founder member of the Industry Academy – a community of the foremost industry experts in IBM. He oversees the training for the Company’s badging program and mentors executives applying for the Platinum Badge – highest level of industry expertise in the Corporation. - Business schools: Likhit has been a guest lecturer on Banking industry transformation at several business schools around the world including the Said Business School, Oxford; Cass Business School, London; and the Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad. Likhit is a thought leader and featured speaker in the industry with several published articles and whitepapers and is frequently quoted in the press. he is regularly invited to present to the Boards of the top Banks worldwide on major trends in the industry. Industry publications like the Banker Magazine, Bloomberg, the Economist and Linkedin Live regularly have Likhit on their panels to discuss the implications of top-of-mind industry issues such as digitisation, data at scale, regulatory compliance, and security. Previously, Likhit led IBM’s Financial Services business in the Asia Pacific region. In this role he led leading edge work to digitally transform incumbent banks and enable them compete successfully financial disruptors: At State Bank of India – the largest Bank in the country – capabilities have been built in an accelerated way over a 24-month program to enable 350 million customers to use their smart phones to make balance enquiries; borrow money and buy mutual funds. A marketplace has also been created for the bank that combines banking with lifestyle products and services to rival platform companies like Amazon and Flipcart. Outcomes for customer acquisition, revenue and profit growth have made this one of the most successful digital transformations in the Banking industry. At DBS, recently voted Best Bank in the world by the prestigious Global Finance Magazine, the IBM team has supported the Bank’s digitalisation program developing applications, transforming processes through cognitive process automation, and developing the Bank’s data strategy. Prior to this Likhit was Global General Manager of IBM’s Banking business based out of London. During this time Likhit led the strategy work that: resulted in setting up Financial Services as the first industry vertical across all of IBM; the acquisition of Promontory, one of the world’s leading regulatory consulting firms; and the development of the Regtech portfolio of cognitive software and services to address rapidly increasing compliance costs. supported the successful launch of the new Z14 Mainframe computer as the platform of choice for Banks seeking to digitise their infrastructure taking advantage of attributes such as pervasive encryption; ability to perform real time analytics; and open source architecture. Before joining IBM, Likhit was a partner at PwC and led their Strategic Consulting Practice in the UK. During this time, he led major programmes focused on improving shareholder value for organisations like Allianz in the Finance Sector and also at Novo Nordisk, Astra Zeneca, Roche, South African Breweries and Sony in other industries. This work was focused on identifying the most sensitive drivers of the company’s intrinsic value and devising initiatives to transform business performance under-pinning these drivers. At IBM, Likhit extended this approach to devise and execute several successful engagements that had creative commercial constructs where IBM put “skin in the game” by linking a major proportion of its fees to improvements in specific enterprise wide key performance indicators that were fundamental to the value of the client. Prior to this, Likhit led the M&A advisory business for PwC in the UK. He was a member of the European Management Board and advised on several divestments and leveraged buy-outs. During this time PwC was consistently ranked first in the league table for private company M&A advisory work. Likhit is a member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales and has a Honours degree in Economics and Business Finance from the University of Manchester.

name: John Walsh
title: Chief Product and Technology Officer
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation, New Business Ventures
bio: John Walsh currently serves as the Chief Technology Officer at Vori Health, with prior experience as the CTO of Zipari. Walsh was formerly the VP and CTO of Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield New Jersey, the state’s largest health insurer, with over 3.8 million members. In this role, Walsh was responsible for developing and overseeing the corporate technology strategy and leading key technology transformation programs. He also oversaw the IT Transform functions, which include the Business Solutions Group, Technology Architecture, Enterprise Architecture and Data Strategy. Prior to joining Horizon BCBSNJ in 2015, Walsh served at two other Blue Cross Blue Shield companies, as VP and Chief Architect at Excellus BCBS and as Chief Architect and Director of Data and Integration Services at HealthNow New York Inc. Prior to joining the health care industry, Walsh held technology leadership roles in the telecom industry as CTO with Callfinity and in the financial services industry at Fidelity Investments. A native of Ireland, Walsh holds a Bachelor of Technology degree in Information Technology and Telecommunications from the University of Limerick.

name: Terry Waters
title: Board Member
bio: Terry Waters is an experienced CEO, Board member, go-to-market strategist, and business builder. He currently serves as an Executive in Residence and Advisor Board Member at Northeastern University, where he provides valuable insights and guidance. Additionally, he is a Member at Nectar, Inc., where he contributes to the organization's activities. Terry also holds the position of President at Waters Advisory, where he offers leadership advisory and professional development programs for PE-backed CEOs, their Leadership teams, and their Boards, with a focus on various aspects of leadership and business development. Furthermore, he serves as an Advisor at GearBox Capital and as a Board Member at See|Me. Most recently, Terry was the CEO and Board member for Devada, a business focused on building online professional communities where software developers and technology professionals learn, share insights, and collaborate. He successfully sold Devada's AnswerHub software business to IgniteTech in November 2022 and then Devada's DZone media and marketing services business to TechnologyAdvice in May 2023. Prior to Devada, Terry ran his own go to market advisory firm, Waters Advisory working with PE firms, CEOs, and leadership teams on developing and executing content driven, subscription-based information and data driven business strategies, executing clear go to market plans, and driving organizational change management programs. Prior to this, from 2015-16, Mr. Waters was Board member, CEO and President of Roubini Global Economics (RGE), a leading independent, global macroeconomic research and analytics firm based in NYC which he sold to Continuum Economics in 2016. From 2008 – 2014, he was Board member, CEO and President of two PE backed businesses, Highline Financial and Yankee Group, leading both to successful strategic exits and as well during 2014 as Head of Digital for Evanta, a leadership development firm focused on CXO best practices and collaborative knowledge exchange, he designed, built, and launched three major new digital subscription-based membership product offerings for the firm. Terry spent 18 years at Gartner, the leading global IT research, events, and advisory services firm, starting as an account executive when the company was only $10M in revenues and ending as Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) and a member of the Executive Operating Committee when the company had grown to $1.2B in revenues and $4B+ in market cap (NYSE: IT). Over two stints, he led a significant part of the US sales organization, founded the highly successful EXP membership subscription business, built the global marketing team and led a $150M division of the Gartner global subscription based research business. In between his two stints at Gartner, he was the COO for Screaming Media, an Internet content syndication and business services provider that he helped grow to $25M in revenues and a successful IPO on NASDAQ in 2000. Mr. Waters started his career at Xerox after graduating from the College of the Holy Cross.

name: Børre Wessel
title: Head of Workspace for Trading
focus: Data Management & Analytics, Digital & Business Transformation
bio: Børre Wessel is currently serving as Head of Workspace for platform technology and Trading at LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group). In the past, he worked as a contractor at NatWest Markets Plc and BNY Mellon, and he is an independent consultant currently working at a leading global investment management and investment services company. Mr. Wessel has held a number of technology leadership roles for many large global financial services organizations, with a focus on digital transformation and, in recent years, cloud and SaaS migration in the trading space for financial services. Mr. Wessel holds a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Høgskolen i Vestfold (now University of South-Eastern Norway).

name: David Widup
title: Self Employed
bio: David Widup is a Coach at the Hudson Institute of Coaching. Widup was formerly a Principal at N. Allen & Associates, LLC. Before this, he was VP - Information Technology Strategy and Transformation for Becton, Dickinson & Co. Widup has lived and worked in seven countries across the Americas, Europe and Asia, leading enterprise initiatives in Information Technology, Change Management, Business Process Innovation and Supply Change Integration and Management. He has held leadership positions in several industries including Transportation, Government, Consumer Products, Medical Instruments and Biosciences. Widup is a creative writer, Court Appointed Special Advocate (CASA) and coach, focusing on working with professionals looking to make impactful changes in how they perform, individuals with significant life transitions and in helping organizations develop internal management capabilities, usually as part of large initiatives with significant change impacts. He also mentors and coaches Veterans and organizations that are actively supporting them.

name: Philip Wiser
title: Executive Vice President and Chief Technology Office
focus: C-Level Management, Cybersecurity, Media & Entertainment Technology Management
bio: Phil Wiser serves as the Executive Vice President, Chief Technology Officer, and Head of Multiplatform Operations at Paramount, where he oversees the global technology strategy, transformation, shared services, and operations for Paramount Global. Previously, he held the role of EVP and CTO at ViacomCBS. Phil Wiser is a prominent technology and digital media executive known for his pioneering work in the online digital music industry and the over-the-top TV market. He holds 18 issued patents and more pending in areas such as IP-based content delivery, machine recommendation systems, video delivery over the Internet, and cybersecurity. Wiser has a track record of leading technology and product development transformations, with past roles at Hearst Corporation and Sony Music. Furthermore, he is active as an angel investor, advisor, and board member for various emerging and established digital media and technology companies. Wiser excels at identifying emerging trends and translating them into valuable consumer experiences and disruptive business opportunities, whether in startup or Fortune 500 contexts.

name: David Wong
title: Managing Director
bio: David Wong is a Managing Director with Alvarez & Marsal’s Private Equity Performance Improvement Group in Boston. He leads the software and product due diligence offering for private equity firms and their portfolio companies. With over 25 years of experience as a strategy consultant, senior executive and thought leader, David has worked with a wide range of organizations, from start-up to mid-market to Fortune 50, in North America, South America, Europe and Asia. David’s notable experiences include leading a combined market and product/technology due diligence assessment of a leading player in the data analytics and integrity software markets with a $9 billion total address market, with segments of the broader enterprise information management market. Previously, David led a combined market and product due diligence assessment of a leading contact center infrastructure software provider. The assessment addressed the positioning of the existing product set and the organization for the market shift away from legacy on-premises solutions toward the cloud. In addition, David led the product and tech due diligence and was a senior team member of the synergy and cost takeout for the carve out and merger integration of a $400 million revenue digital marketing services business focused on automotive OEMs and their dealers. This carve out was assessed as part of potential integration with an existing marketing agency. Prior to joining A&M, David was a Managing Director with Bulger Partners. He co-founded and co-managed the strategy consulting practice, focusing on software and tech-enabled business services transactions and portfolio companies. Previously, he held various strategy consulting, marketing and emerging business innovation leadership roles at IBM. He joined IBM as part of its acquisition of Mainspring, a venture-backed strategy consultancy start-up, where, as a partner, he helped grow the firm twenty-fold in two years. He has also published articles and white papers on cloud and other tech-related issues, as well as on the integration of business and IT. In addition, David has spoken at a variety of conferences, events and client briefings. David earned a Bachelor of Applied Science in Mechanical Engineering with first class honors from Queen’s University at Kingston, and a Master of Public and Private Management from the Yale School of Management.

name: Paul Worthington
title: Consultant
bio: Paul Worthington's current role is Consultant at CXPLUS LIMITED. In the past, he held positions as Senior Advisor at Oliver Wyman and COO and advisor at Iothic Ltd. Paul Worthington was also Senior Research Advisor at Leading Edge Forum. He also has an independent consulting and advisory business that focuses on improving consumer experiences and propositions through better understanding and use of the opportunities enabled by new technologies. His corporate experience ranges from commercial and marketing roles to technology leadership positions at companies in the Financial Services, Retail, Energy and Utilities, Telecommunications and CPG/PMCG sectors. Worthington has significant board level experience across a number of sectors and disciplines with a particular focus and interest in the use of technology to deliver strategic advantage through innovative new consumer propositions. He has held a number of Chairman and a Non-Executive Director roles and was recently a strategic advisor to an investment company where he led an incubator to articulate the future use of technology across the food supply chain. Worthington holds an M.B.A. from Bath University.

name: David Wright
title: Senior Vice President, Global Technology
bio: David Wright currently serves as the Senior Vice President, Global Technology at TD SYNNEX. Additionally, he is the Founder of Results Now, LLC, a company dedicated to delivering impactful results to its clients. David Wright was the SVP, Global Technology of Tech Data. Prior to Tech Data, Wright was the Managing Director of Results Now, LLC, a business and technology consulting company. Before Tech Data, he held numerous executive roles in Financial Services such as CTO for LPL Financial, COO for Capital One Bank Europe, and SVP for Capital One. Wright’s experience also includes 10 years in IT with the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond. Wright holds an M.Sc. in Management of Information Technology from the University of Virginia, and M.B.A. and B.S. degrees in Information Systems from Virginia Commonwealth University. He is also a graduate of the Virginia Bankers Association School of Bank Management at the University of Virginia.

name: Al Zollar
title: Executive Advisor
bio: Al Zollar has been an Executive Partner with SIRIS Capital Group, LLC since February 2014. Zollar retired from IBM in January 2011, capping a 34 year career. He is a 2011 Fellow of the Advanced Leadership Initiative at Harvard University. Zollar was formerly General Manager of IBM Tivoli Software from July 2004-January 2011, where he was responsible for the executive leadership, strategy and P&L of the Tivoli brand, a global, multi-billion dollar business within IBM's $20B+ Software business. Previously, he was General Manager, IBM iSeries, where he was responsible for the executive leadership, strategy and P&L of the iSeries (formerly AS/400) server product line. Before that, he held senior management positions in each of IBM's diverse software businesses, including General Manager of IBM Lotus Software. He has been issued US Patent #8,261,329 B2, "Trust and Identity in Secure Calendar Sharing Collaboration". He is a board member of PSEG, Incorporated (2012), Bank of New York Mellon (2019) and Nasdaq (2019). He is also a board member of three private companies in the SIRIS portfolio: Stratus Technologies (2014), Pulse Secure (2014), and EFI (2019). He is a former board member of the Chubb Corporation (2001-16) and Red Hat (2018-19). Al is also member of the Executive Leadership Council, and a Lifetime Member of the National Society of Black Engineers. Zollar holds an M.S. in Applied Mathematics from the University of California at San Diego. In 2011 he was named one of 50 distinguished alumni of the university. He spends his free time with his family and friends, reading, playing golf and listening to jazz.

name: Hank Zupnick
title: Partner
bio: Hank Zupnick is a Partner at High Ridge Associates. Zupnick has over 30 years of leadership and technology experience, including 16 years in CIO roles for prominent European and American divisions of General Electric.Prior to High Ridge Associates, Zupnick was the CIO of AJC Global, a 110-year old not-for-profit supporting religious pluralism, human rights and democratic values worldwide. As CIO, he was responsible for the development and implementation of cost-effective, flexible productivity solutions that will enable AJC to continue to fulfill its mission, while at the same time ensuring a reliable and secure technology infrastructure worldwide. Before High Ridge, Zupnick served as CIO of Workforce Opportunity Services, a not-for-profit dedicated to bringing economically disadvantaged young adults into the IT profession. Zupnick is mentor at Streetwise Partners and the Society for Information Management, and a volunteer with LiveOn New York, an organ donation advocacy organization. He also mentors in a freelance, fee-based capacity with rising mid-career technology executives.
